Renekton.trading_pattern: Poke with Q1 from max range, never commit to full combo unless his E is down. Walk sideways to dodge his W stun after he dashes in.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton E — his double dash lets him engage and escape; bait the first E then punish before the second comes out.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (full Q combo + W), Spear of Shojin first item
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (empowered W stun), Blade of the Ruined King

Renekton.early_game: Respect his fury bar — empowered W chunks hard. Trade only when his fury is low. Use Q1 to poke and back off, don't commit to extended trades.
Renekton.mid_game: You outscale him from here. Keep farming and hitting Q sweet spots in skirmishes. He falls off if he didn't snowball early.
Renekton.late_game: You outscale significantly. Renekton becomes a mediocre frontliner while your teamfight is much stronger with ult resets.
Renekton.win_condition: Survive lane without dying, scale past first item. You win the 1v1 after Shojin as long as you land sweet spots.
Renekton.scaling: Aatrox favored — Renekton falls off hard after midgame while Aatrox maintains strong teamfight presence
Renekton.positioning: Stay at Q1 max range to poke. If he dashes onto you, E backwards and kite with Q2/Q3. Don't stand near your low-health minions — he dashes through them.

Garen.trading_pattern: Poke with Q1 edge repeatedly. When he runs at you with Q, walk into him to deny the silence range and trade back with your own combo.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q — the silence prevents ability use; walk into him so the enhanced auto range doesn't reach or sidestep at the last moment.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (full combo available), Spear of Shojin

Garen.early_game: You win every trade levels 1-5 if you land Q sweet spots. Zone him off CS with Q1 poke. Don't let him hide in a bush to regen with his passive.
Garen.mid_game: Continue to bully in side lane. Respect his ult execute threshold — don't overstay at low HP thinking you'll heal through it.
Garen.late_game: Garen gets tanky and his ult is a guaranteed execute. In teamfights, focus on other targets first and avoid being the low-HP frontliner he can ult.
Garen.win_condition: Build a CS and kill lead early through constant Q poke. Keep the wave in the middle of lane so he can't safely regen under tower.
Garen.scaling: Even — Aatrox stronger early, Garen even or slightly ahead late due to tankiness and point-click execute
Garen.positioning: Keep him at Q1 sweet spot range. If he activates Q to run at you, you can E sideways and Q2 to hit the edge. Don't stand on top of him during his E spin.

Darius.trading_pattern: Poke with Q1 from max range, never walk into his E pull range. Short trades only — if he gets five passive stacks you die.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius E — his Apprehend pull has a deceptive range; stay well outside it. If pulled, E backwards immediately and disengage.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (World Ender sustain to survive all-ins), Spear of Shojin

Darius.early_game: Do not fight him in extended trades. Poke with Q1 edge and immediately back off. He statchecks you hard if he gets on top of you. Use W to chain after Q1 hits and back away.
Darius.mid_game: You start winning if you avoided feeding him. Land combos from range and use ult sustain to outfight him. Build anti-heal if he takes Conqueror.
Darius.late_game: You have much better teamfight presence. Darius gets kited in teamfights while you can hit multiple people with Q sweet spots.
Darius.win_condition: Go even in lane, don't let him stack passive. Outscale him in teamfights where he can't just run you down in a straight line.
Darius.scaling: Aatrox favored mid-late — Darius is a lane bully who falls off in organized teamfights while Aatrox thrives
Darius.positioning: Always stay outside his E range (about 535 units). Poke from max Q1 range. If he uses Ghost to run you down, immediately ult and kite backwards with E.

Malphite.trading_pattern: You can never really all-in him. His passive shield absorbs your poke, and he just Q slows you and backs off. Try to break his shield with auto then trade.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite R — Unstoppable Force is a fast engage knockup; flash sideways when he reaches about 60% of the dash distance.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (combo + sustain), Spear of Shojin + Serylda's

Malphite.early_game: Try to pop his passive shield with an auto before committing to Q combos. His Q poke with Comet chunks hard — take Doran's Shield and Second Wind.
Malphite.mid_game: You struggle to kill him once he has armor. Focus on pushing waves and roaming for teamfights where you can be more useful.
Malphite.late_game: He outscales you as a tank. His R is a better teamfight tool. Focus on split pushing or catching squishier targets in fights.
Malphite.win_condition: Get an early lead before he stacks armor. If you can't solo kill him, push and roam. You need to be proactive because he outscales.
Malphite.scaling: Malphite favored — he becomes unkillable with armor stacking and his R is always relevant in teamfights
Malphite.positioning: Stay outside his Q range (625) when his passive shield is up. When trading, try to pop his shield first with an auto then immediately combo.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Never fight near your dying minions — she resets Q through them and builds passive stacks. Trade when the wave is healthy and her passive is down.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E — her Flawless Duet stun sets up her full combo; sidestep between the two blades or E sideways to dodge the line.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with ult sustain, Spear of Shojin
power_spikes.enemy: Level 4-5 (fully stacked passive with Q resets), Blade of the Ruined King

Irelia.early_game: Respect her fully stacked passive — she outdamages you hard with 4 stacks. If she Qs onto you with full stacks, E backwards and disengage. Only trade when her passive is empty.
Irelia.mid_game: She can 1v1 you if she lands E stun. Keep your distance and look for Q poke when she overextends. She's a strong duelist but you have better teamfights.
Irelia.late_game: You outscale her in teamfights. She has to commit to diving backlines while you can hit frontline and backline with Q sweet spots.
Irelia.win_condition: Don't let her snowball. Manage your wave so she can't get easy Q resets. Post-6 you can match her all-ins with ult healing.
Irelia.scaling: Aatrox slightly favored late — Irelia is a strong duelist all game but Aatrox teamfights better
Irelia.positioning: Keep the wave pushing slightly toward you so she can't build a minion highway for Q resets. Stand away from dying minions to deny reset paths.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: Dodge his E pull by sidestepping, then walk in and full combo him. He's slow and immobile — perfect for landing Q sweet spots.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser E — his Death's Grasp pull is slow and telegraphed; sidestep it and punish with a full combo while it's on cooldown.
Mordekaiser.early_game: You should dominate this lane. Dodge his E, punish with Q combos. He has no mobility to escape your W. Poke him down and all-in when he's half HP.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: Continue to bully him. In his Death Realm, you can still win the 1v1 if you land Q sweet spots and ult for sustain. Don't panic when ulted.
Mordekaiser.late_game: He can remove you from teamfights with R. Try to stay near squishies so if he ults you, his team loses their frontline. Build QSS if his ult is a problem.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Kill him early and snowball the lead. Don't let him passively farm into Riftmaker. His Death Realm steals your stats so get ahead before he uses it.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Even — Aatrox wins lane hard but Mordekaiser's Death Realm becomes a constant threat in teamfights
Mordekaiser.positioning: Stay at mid range where you can react to his E. Never stand directly in front of him — his E is a straight line and easy to dodge if you're offset.

Jax.trading_pattern: When Jax uses Counter Strike, do NOT use Q — he dodges it. Wait out his E, then combo him when it ends. Bait the E before committing.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E — Counter Strike blocks your Q damage and stuns; back off when he activates it and re-engage after the stun goes out.
Jax.early_game: Skill matchup that slightly favors Jax. Poke with Q1 but never commit to a full combo if his E is up. When he jumps on you, E backwards and wait out Counter Strike.
Jax.mid_game: He outscales you in the 1v1. Look for teamfights where your AoE is more valuable. Don't try to sidelane against him once he has two items.
Jax.late_game: He massively outscales. Avoid 1v1 split push scenarios. Group with your team where your AoE and ult resets are more impactful.
Jax.win_condition: Get an early lead with Q poke before his E is maxed. Transition to teamfighting where you're far more useful than a Jax.
Jax.scaling: Jax heavily favored — one of the hardest scaling duelists in the game; Aatrox must win through teamfights
Jax.positioning: Keep Q1 poke range. If he leaps onto you with E active, immediately E away and don't waste Q into his Counter Strike. Re-engage the moment E expires.

Gangplank.trading_pattern: Walk through his barrels to deny him combos. Land Q1 poke repeatedly — he's squishy early and can't trade back well if you dodge barrels.
Gangplank.ability_to_dodge: Gangplank Barrel — destroy his kegs by auto-attacking them when they tick down to 1 HP; this removes his burst and slow.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (full combo pressure), Spear of Shojin
power_spikes.enemy: Level 13 (barrel tick speed), Trinity Force + Collector

Gangplank.early_game: You should bully him hard pre-6. Walk up and Q combo him whenever he goes for CS. His Q poke hurts but you sustain it back with passive. Zone him aggressively.
Gangplank.mid_game: Push him in and look for roams. If he's playing safe with barrels under tower, shove and impact the map before he scales.
Gangplank.late_game: He massively outscales with barrel crit combos. Force fights before he gets three items. In teamfights, dive past him to backline — his barrels zone but you can ult through.
Gangplank.win_condition: Crush him in lane and end before he reaches his three-item crit spike. He's one of the best scaling champions, so early aggression is mandatory.
Gangplank.scaling: Gangplank heavily favored — his barrel crit combos can one-shot squishies and he provides global ult pressure
Gangplank.positioning: Stay on top of his barrels to deny chains. If he places a barrel, auto it to last-hit at 1 HP. Don't let him freely zone you with barrel setups.

Fiora.trading_pattern: Never use Q3 predictably — she Ripostes it for a free stun. Use Q1 to poke, bait her W with Q2, then hit Q3 after Riposte is down.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W — Riposte blocks your Q knockup and stuns you; bait it by feinting Q3 with E then delaying the actual Q3 cast.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (World Ender sustain), Spear of Shojin
power_spikes.enemy: Level 1 (vital poke is strong), Ravenous Hydra

Fiora.early_game: She wins short trades with vital procs. Don't let her proc vitals for free — stand against walls to hide vitals. Poke with Q1 and back off. Never throw Q3 predictably.
Fiora.mid_game: She wins the split push 1v1. Look for teamfights where she's weaker. Don't try to match her sidelane after she has Hydra.
Fiora.late_game: She hard outscales in duels. Her Grand Challenge shreds your sustain. Never 1v1 her late — group and force teamfights.
Fiora.win_condition: Get early kills by baiting her Riposte. If you can't, shove and roam. She's a weak teamfighter, so force 5v5s.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora heavily favored — she's the premier late-game duelist and Aatrox can never match her sidelane
Fiora.positioning: Hug walls to deny vital spawns on exposed sides. Stand at Q1 max range and poke. If she lunges in, E backwards and don't panic-Q3 into her Riposte.

Sett.trading_pattern: Poke with Q1 from range, kite backwards during his Haymaker. Never stand in the true damage center of his W — always sidestep it.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W — Haymaker's center deals true damage; sidestep to the left or right to avoid the true damage zone and only take the weaker outer edge.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult sustain for extended trades), Spear of Shojin

Sett.early_game: Don't let him auto you for free — his passive gives bonus regen and his punches hurt. Poke with Q1 and back off. If he E pulls you, E sideways to avoid the W followup.
Sett.mid_game: You start outscaling him. Land combos from range and ult to sustain through his burst. His Haymaker becomes predictable — always sidestep it.
Sett.late_game: You outscale in teamfights. His ult is a good engage tool but your AoE damage and sustain are more valuable. Avoid getting caught by his R into his team.
Sett.win_condition: Survive early levels, poke consistently, and outscale after first item. Dodge Haymaker center every time — that's his biggest damage source.
Sett.scaling: Aatrox favored — Sett's damage falls off and he becomes more of a utility tank while Aatrox stays relevant with ult resets
Sett.positioning: Stay at Q1 range. When he walks at you with E, angle sideways so you're never in line for both the pull and Haymaker center. Always dodge left or right, never backwards through W.

Ornn.trading_pattern: Dodge his Q pillar and punish with a full combo. He's tanky but immobile. Land W to chain him and follow up with all three Qs.
Ornn.ability_to_dodge: Ornn W — Bellows Breath makes him unstoppable, meaning your Q won't knock him up during it; wait for W to finish before using Q3.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 13+ (item upgrades for team), Sunfire Cape

Ornn.early_game: You win trades early. Dodge his Q pillar and E-Q combo him. Be aware that his W makes him unstoppable — your Q knockup won't work during it.
Ornn.mid_game: He becomes very tanky. You can still poke him but killing him solo gets harder. Look to shove and roam or help your jungler.
Ornn.late_game: He outscales through item upgrades for his entire team. His R is a massive teamfight engage. Try to end before his passive gives everyone upgraded items.
Ornn.win_condition: Bully him hard pre-6 and deny CS. His value comes from scaling and upgrading items — delay his power curve as long as possible.
Ornn.scaling: Ornn favored — his passive item upgrades for the team make him one of the best scaling tanks in the game
Ornn.positioning: Dodge to the side of his Q volcanic pillar. When he uses W, wait for the unstoppable to end before throwing Q3 for the knockup.

Jayce.trading_pattern: Second Wind and Doran's Shield are mandatory. Wait for him to swap to melee form to all-in — he's squishy up close. Dodge his ranged Q-E combo by standing behind minions.
Jayce.ability_to_dodge: Jayce Q+E — his Shock Blast through Acceleration Gate deals massive poke; stand behind minions to block it or sidestep the narrow projectile.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (World Ender lets you run him down), Spear of Shojin

Jayce.early_game: He pokes you down hard from range. Take Doran's Shield, Second Wind, and just survive. Don't walk up for CS when he has Q+E combo ready. Farm with your own Q if needed.
Jayce.mid_game: Once you have ult, you can run him down. Pop ult when he's in melee range and he can't escape your Q combo. He's squishy and has no sustain.
Jayce.late_game: You outscale in teamfights. He's still a ranged threat but you can dive past him to backline. Your sustain and AoE make you more valuable.
Jayce.win_condition: Survive lane, don't give kills. At level 6 you can all-in him with ult. If he can't snowball the lane advantage, he falls off compared to you.
Jayce.scaling: Aatrox favored — Jayce is a lane bully who falls off in teamfights while Aatrox scales with ult resets
Jayce.positioning: Stand behind your melee minions to block his Q+E poke. When he swaps to hammer form and jumps on you, that's your cue to full combo — he's squishier in melee than you.

Camille.trading_pattern: When she hookshots onto you, E sideways and Q2/Q3 where she lands. Her E is telegraphed and you can punish the landing spot every time.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E — Hookshot stun; sidestep the wall jump trajectory and punish with Q sweet spot where she lands.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (full combo advantage), Spear of Shojin

Camille.early_game: You win levels 1-5. Punish her whenever she walks up for CS. If she E's onto you, dodge and full combo. Respect her passive shield when it's up — don't trade into it.
Camille.mid_game: She starts matching you with Trinity Force. Her R locks you in place for ganks. Keep the wave on your side and don't overextend without vision.
Camille.late_game: She outscales in split push. Group for teamfights where your AoE is stronger. Her R is great for picks but weak in 5v5 compared to your ult.
Camille.win_condition: Punish early with your range advantage and combo. Deny her farm so she can't reach Trinity Force spike on time.
Camille.scaling: Camille slightly favored — her true damage and split push pressure outscale Aatrox in side lanes
Camille.positioning: Keep distance from walls so her E hookshot has longer travel time. When she jumps, predict the landing spot and place Q sweet spot there.

K'Sante.trading_pattern: Punish his weak early game with Q poke. Dodge his Q3 dash knockup by stepping sideways, then full combo him while it's on cooldown.
K'Sante.ability_to_dodge: K'Sante Q3 — his third Q dash forward is his main engage; sidestep it and he's left with nothing but autos.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (full combo dominance), Spear of Shojin

K'Sante.early_game: You hard-win early. He's squishy until he builds armor and his Q3 is easy to dodge. Poke him repeatedly and zone him off CS.
K'Sante.mid_game: Still favorable. He becomes tankier but you have sustain and he doesn't. In his ult form he trades tankiness for damage — punish him there with your own sustain.
K'Sante.late_game: He becomes very tanky and useful with his R in teamfights. Focus on hitting Q sweet spots on his carries instead of trying to solo kill the tank.
K'Sante.win_condition: Abuse his weak laning and build a CS lead. He's one of the weakest early top laners — press the advantage before he gets team-utility value.
K'Sante.scaling: K'Sante slightly favored — his tank utility and ult engage are more valuable late than Aatrox in organized play
K'Sante.positioning: Stand offset from his Q line so his Q3 dash misses. When he ults you through a wall, pop World Ender immediately for sustain in the 1v1.

Gnar.trading_pattern: Wait for his Mega Gnar to time out, then all-in Mini Gnar. In Mini form he kites you, but in the transition window between forms he's vulnerable.
Gnar.ability_to_dodge: Mega Gnar R — his ult stuns against walls; fight him away from walls or flash the ult knockback.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult to run him down), Spear of Shojin
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Mega Gnar R engage), Black Cleaver

Gnar.early_game: Mini Gnar kites you endlessly. Take Second Wind and Doran's Shield. Only look for trades when he's about to transform — he's most vulnerable in the transition.
Gnar.mid_game: You can kill him in Mini form if you land W and full combo. Respect Mega Gnar engage near walls. Split push vs him when he doesn't have Mega available.
Gnar.late_game: His Mega Gnar teamfight is one of the best in the game. Focus on flanking in teamfights so his R doesn't hit multiple people.
Gnar.win_condition: Track his rage bar. Engage on him when Mega Gnar is on cooldown. In Mini form he's squishy and your combo one-shots him if you land W.
Gnar.scaling: Gnar favored — Mega Gnar teamfight engage is extremely powerful and he kites Aatrox effectively all game
Gnar.positioning: Never fight near walls when his rage bar is full. Track his rage bar at all times — engage the moment Mega form expires and he's stuck in Mini.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Punish him every time he walks up to Q a minion. Full combo him repeatedly — he has no way to fight back early. Deny every stack possible.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W — Wither is a crippling slow; cleanse it with Mercs or simply kite backwards until it expires before re-engaging.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-5 (total lane dominance), Spear of Shojin

Nasus.early_game: One of your easiest matchups. Zone him off every CS. Combo him every time he walks forward. Freeze near your tower and deny him completely.
Nasus.mid_game: Keep denying him stacks. If he has less than 200 stacks by 15 minutes you've done your job. Push and roam if he's too far behind to matter.
Nasus.late_game: He outscales you hard. With 400+ stacks and Wither he runs you down. End the game before he becomes a raid boss — do not let this go to 35+ minutes.
Nasus.win_condition: Freeze lane and deny stacks. Kill him repeatedly. End the game before 30 minutes or he becomes impossible to deal with.
Nasus.scaling: Nasus massively favored — infinite Q stacking and Wither make him a 1v1 monster past 25 minutes
Nasus.positioning: Stand between him and the dying minions to deny Q stacks. Zone him off the wave entirely when you have kill pressure.

Sion.trading_pattern: Interrupt his Q charge with your own Q knockup. He's a tank with no escape — land W chains and full combo him freely.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q — cancel his Decimating Smash charge by hitting him with any Q sweet spot knockup before it finishes.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (combo disrupts all his abilities), Spear of Shojin
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R engage), Sunfire Cape + Titanic Hydra

Sion.early_game: Free lane. Interrupt his Q charge with your Q every time. He can't trade back effectively. Zone him off CS and look for kills.
Sion.mid_game: He starts getting tanky but you still win the 1v1. Watch for his proxy farming — don't chase him behind tower, just farm and push.
Sion.late_game: He outscales through pure health stacking and becomes unkillable. His R engage can win teamfights. Focus on ending before he becomes too tanky to kill.
Sion.win_condition: Bully him non-stop in lane. Deny farm so his passive health stacking is slower. End before he becomes a 6000 HP wall.
Sion.scaling: Sion slightly favored — infinite health scaling and reliable engage outvalue Aatrox in very late games
Sion.positioning: Stay near him to interrupt Q charges immediately. Don't let him fully channel from fog of war. Stand offset from his R charge path.

Shen.trading_pattern: Your Q passes through his W spirit blade zone — it blocks autos but not abilities. Combo him freely when his W is down. Don't auto-attack into his W zone.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: Shen E — Spirit Dash taunt; sidestep it and punish with full combo. It has a long cooldown early.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (Q combo ignores his W), Spear of Shojin

Shen.early_game: You win trades since your Q goes through his Spirit's Refuge. Punish him when he walks up. Cancel his ult with Q3 knockup if he tries to teleport to an ally.
Shen.mid_game: Shove him in and pressure — if he ults away, take tower plates. His map presence with R is his strength, so punish it by pushing when he leaves.
Shen.late_game: His ult gives his team massive teamfight advantage. Match his roam pressure by pushing side lanes hard when he ults away.
Shen.win_condition: Kill him in lane or take tower when he ults. His global pressure is his win condition — punish every ult by taking objectives.
Shen.scaling: Shen favored — his global presence and tank utility scale better than Aatrox in coordinated play
Shen.positioning: Don't auto-attack into his W zone — only use Q. When you see him channeling R, immediately Q3 to cancel it.

Dr. Mundo.trading_pattern: Dodge his Q cleaver and all-in. He's free to combo in lane — no mobility, no hard CC. Land W chains and full combo him.
Dr. Mundo.ability_to_dodge: Dr. Mundo Q — his Infected Bonesaw slow; sidestep it and he loses his poke and engage setup.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (full combo dominance), Spear of Shojin

Dr. Mundo.early_game: One of your best matchups. He can't fight you at all. Dodge his Q and all-in him every time. He has no way to escape your W chains.
Dr. Mundo.mid_game: You still win the 1v1 hard. He becomes tankier with R but you have enough damage and sustain to kill him. Build anti-heal if needed.
Dr. Mundo.late_game: He becomes very tanky and hard to kill with full build. But he offers nothing but being a meatshield — your teamfight is much more impactful.
Dr. Mundo.win_condition: Abuse him in lane and build a massive lead. Even if he gets tanky later, your early advantage should carry the game.
Dr. Mundo.scaling: Even — Mundo becomes an unkillable meatshield but Aatrox's teamfight presence with resets is more impactful
Dr. Mundo.positioning: Dodge his Q by sidestepping — it's a slow projectile. Once it misses, walk at him freely and full combo.

Volibear.trading_pattern: Kite backwards when he runs at you with Q. Space with Q1 and if he commits, E back and hit Q2/Q3 sweet spots as he chases you in a straight line.
Volibear.ability_to_dodge: Volibear Q — his Thundering Smash run and stun; kite backwards with E to outspace the stun, then turn and combo.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (full combo kiting), Spear of Shojin

Volibear.early_game: You win by kiting. When he runs at you with Q, E backwards and Q him as he chases. He's a juggernaut who needs to be on top of you — never let him.
Volibear.mid_game: Continue to kite and poke. He's good at tower dives with R disabling tower, so be careful if low HP under tower. Otherwise you dominate the 1v1.
Volibear.late_game: You outscale in teamfights. He's a mediocre frontliner. Your AoE and sustain are more valuable than his single-target engage.
Volibear.win_condition: Kite him and land Q sweet spots. He has no way to catch you if you space properly. Build a lead and carry teamfights.
Volibear.scaling: Aatrox favored — Volibear is a lane bully who falls off while Aatrox's teamfight stays strong
Volibear.positioning: Never stand still when he runs at you with Q. Kite backwards in a straight line, using E to maintain distance. Combo him as he chases.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Kill his ghouls with Q AoE before fighting him. If he traps you in his W cage, E out immediately — don't fight surrounded by ghouls and Maiden.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick W — Dark Procession cage; immediately E-dash out of it. If you're stuck inside with ghouls and Maiden, you lose the trade.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (AoE Q clears ghouls fast), Spear of Shojin
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Maiden of the Mist), Trinity Force

Yorick.early_game: You win early. Kill his ghouls with Q AoE and then trade into him. Without ghouls he does very little damage. Watch for his E — if it lands, ghouls jump to you.
Yorick.mid_game: He becomes a strong split pusher with Maiden. You can still 1v1 him but only if you don't fight inside his W cage with Maiden. Clear his ghouls first.
Yorick.late_game: His split push is insane. You might need to match him in a side lane because he takes towers incredibly fast with Maiden. Group when possible.
Yorick.win_condition: Kill his ghouls with Q AoE and fight him without his minion army. Respect Maiden damage and don't underestimate his all-in with full ghouls.
Yorick.scaling: Yorick favored — his split push with Maiden is nearly impossible to match and he takes towers faster than almost anyone
Yorick.positioning: Kill ghouls with Q AoE before committing to fighting him. If he lands E on you, the ghouls jump — sidestep his E before engaging.

Riven.trading_pattern: Space outside her Q range and poke with Q1. She wins short burst trades with her combo + shield. Look for trades when her E shield is on cooldown.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven E — her Valor dash shield lets her absorb your Q damage; wait for it to expire before committing to your combo.
Riven.early_game: She has faster burst combos. Respect her level 1 Q trades. Keep her at Q1 range and poke. If she dashes in, E backwards and don't try to out-trade her short combos.
Riven.mid_game: Skill matchup becomes more even. If you land W chains, you win the extended trade. She has to commit to fighting you — punish when her E is down.
Riven.late_game: She outscales in duels with high CDR. In teamfights she can burst your backline. Focus on peeling for your carries if she dives.
Riven.win_condition: Land W chains to force extended trades where your sustain shines. Never fight in her Q burst window without your own combo ready.
Riven.scaling: Riven slightly favored — her animation cancels and CDR scaling make her a constant threat in duels
Riven.positioning: Keep Q1 max range. Time your Q3 for after her E shield expires. If she third-Qs over you, turn and combo where she lands.

Ambessa.trading_pattern: Her dashes are short but frequent. Poke with Q1 at max range, don't commit to extended trades where she can weave autos and dashes around your Q sweet spots.
Ambessa.ability_to_dodge: Ambessa R — her ult dash engage; flash or E sideways to dodge the initial dash, then punish while it's on cooldown.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (World Ender sustain), Spear of Shojin

Ambessa.early_game: Skill matchup. She has lots of short dashes from her passive. Poke with Q1 from max range and don't let her weave dashes around your abilities. Short trades favor you.
Ambessa.mid_game: You scale similarly. Whoever hits their combo first wins the fight. Land W chains to lock her down — she's slippery but W forces her to commit.
Ambessa.late_game: Both are strong teamfighters. Your AoE and sustain are better in the front-to-back, while she's better at diving backlines.
Ambessa.win_condition: Win lane through superior Q poke and W lockdown. She's mobile but your range advantage on Q1 lets you chip her before she can dash in.
Ambessa.scaling: Even — both are strong teamfighters with different strengths
Ambessa.positioning: Stay at Q1 range. When she dashes toward you, E backwards and Q2 the space she's dashing into. Don't chase into her dash chains.

Singed.trading_pattern: Do not chase him through poison trail. Land W to chain him and combo from range. If he runs at you, E backwards and Q him as he approaches.
Singed.ability_to_dodge: Singed E — Fling tosses you behind him into poison; never walk into melee range. Hit Q sweet spots from outside fling range.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (W chain keeps him in range), Spear of Shojin

Singed.early_game: Do NOT chase him. He wins by making you run through poison. Stand your ground and Q him when he runs at you. If he proxy farms behind your tower, just freeze and wait for your jungler.
Singed.mid_game: He roams and proxies. You can't really catch him. Focus on matching his push and impacting teamfights. He's always going to be annoying in side lanes.
Singed.late_game: His teamfight with R is very strong — he runs through your team with Rylai's slow. Focus on hitting Q sweet spots on his carries, not chasing Singed.
Singed.win_condition: Don't chase him. Farm, scale, and outperform in teamfights. If you chase Singed you lose. Period.
Singed.scaling: Singed favored — his proxy and teamfight style make him very hard for Aatrox to deal with all game
Singed.positioning: Stand your ground and let him come to you. Never follow him into poison. Land W from max range to keep him in your combo zone.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Poke with Q sweet spots when he goes for CS. Don't fight him when he has full fury — his crits chunk hard. Respect his all-in at level 6.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere R — Undying Rage makes him unkillable for 5 seconds; disengage when he ults and re-engage when it expires.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (combo + sustain advantage), Spear of Shojin

Tryndamere.early_game: You win if you space correctly. Poke with Q1 and don't let him auto-attack you for free. His crits are RNG early but can surprise you — don't gamble on all-ins at low HP.
Tryndamere.mid_game: Respect his ult — disengage when he pops R and re-engage after 5 seconds. He splits hard so match him or force teamfights where he's weaker.
Tryndamere.late_game: He outscales in split push. His ult makes him unkillable in duels and he takes towers fast. Force teamfights where your AoE is more valuable.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Bully early, build a lead, and force teamfights. Don't try to match his split push late — group and end with your superior 5v5.
Tryndamere.scaling: Tryndamere favored — his ult and crit scaling make him a split push monster that Aatrox can't match late
Tryndamere.positioning: Stay at Q1 range and poke. When he ults, immediately disengage with E and wait 5 seconds. Don't waste abilities trying to kill through Undying Rage.

Urgot.trading_pattern: Dodge his E flip by sidestepping. If his E misses he has no way to trade effectively. Punish with full combo from outside his shotgun knee range.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot E — Disdain flip is his only engage; sidestep it and full combo while it's on a long cooldown.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (all shotgun knees active), Black Cleaver

Urgot.early_game: You win if his E misses. Poke with Q1 from outside his engage range. If he flips you, you'll take heavy damage from his shotgun knees — don't let him circle around you.
Urgot.mid_game: Even 1v1. Respect his R execute — it kills you at 25% HP regardless of your sustain. Don't duel at low HP thinking ult healing will save you.
Urgot.late_game: He becomes a ranged DPS tank-shredder. His R execute is a constant threat. In teamfights, focus on landing Q sweet spots and avoid getting low enough for his R chain.
Urgot.win_condition: Dodge his E and punish. If you get a lead early, snowball it before he reaches level 9 with all shotgun knees active.
Urgot.scaling: Even — both are strong in teamfights, Urgot's execute is more reliable while Aatrox has more sustain
Urgot.positioning: Stay outside his E range and poke with Q1. If he E's forward and misses, immediately full combo. Don't circle around him — his passive shotgun knees deal more damage the more sides you expose.

Akali.trading_pattern: Punish her pre-6 when she goes for CS. She's melee and squishy. Respect her shroud — don't waste abilities into it. Wait for her to come out and combo.
Akali.ability_to_dodge: Akali W — Twilight Shroud makes her untargetable; don't waste Q into her shroud. Wait at the edge and combo when she exits.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (double R dash burst), Hextech Rocketbelt

Akali.early_game: You bully her hard pre-6. She has to come into melee to farm — punish every CS attempt with Q. She's squishy and your sustain keeps you healthy.
Akali.mid_game: Respect her burst post-6. She can 100-0 you if she lands E shuriken. Keep HP high and combo her when her shroud is on cooldown.
Akali.late_game: She roams and assassinates backlines. You're a better teamfighter. Peel for your carries or dive their backline — don't chase Akali through shroud.
Akali.win_condition: Crush her in lane and deny roam timers. If she's behind she can't one-shot anyone and becomes useless in teamfights.
Akali.scaling: Aatrox favored — Akali needs to snowball while Aatrox is consistent in teamfights regardless of lane result
Akali.positioning: Stand near dying minions to punish her Q farm. When she shrouds, walk to the edge and wait — don't blindly Q into it.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Fight him when he has no minions to dash through. Your Q outranges his E dashes. Land W when he commits to a trade to prevent him from disengaging.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo W — Wind Wall blocks your W chain projectile. Don't W into his windwall; bait it first then use W after it's gone.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (Q combo when he has no minion wave), Spear of Shojin
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R combo off tornado), Infinity Edge

Yasuo.early_game: Fight him away from minion waves so he can't E-dash through creeps to dodge your Qs. In a clean 1v1 with no minions, you stat-check him. Watch for his Q3 tornado.
Yasuo.mid_game: He becomes dangerous with crit items. Respect his R setup off tornado. Land W chains to prevent his dashy trades.
Yasuo.late_game: He outscales with full crit. In teamfights your AoE is better but his R can swing fights. Bait his windwall before using W.
Yasuo.win_condition: Fight in open spaces with no minions. Land W chains so he can't dash away. Shut him down before he reaches two crit items.
Yasuo.scaling: Yasuo favored — his crit scaling and R in teamfights make him more dangerous late than Aatrox in duels
Yasuo.positioning: Fight away from minion waves. When he has tornado stacked, stay behind minions so his Q3 hits a minion instead of you.

Gragas.trading_pattern: Dodge his Q barrel and engage. His E body slam is short range — if he dashes in, W him and full combo. His W damage reduction is strong, so don't trade when he's glowing.
Gragas.ability_to_dodge: Gragas R — Explosive Cask knocks you away and disrupts your combo; flash or E sideways to avoid it during your all-in.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (World Ender all-in), Spear of Shojin

Gragas.early_game: Even lane. His sustain with passive is annoying but you out-trade him if you dodge his Q barrel. Don't fight him when his W damage reduction is active.
Gragas.mid_game: He's hard to kill with his sustain and disengage. Look to roam and teamfight where your ult is more impactful than his.
Gragas.late_game: His R is a great disengage/engage tool. In teamfights, be careful of getting ulted away from your targets. Flank to avoid his barrel.
Gragas.win_condition: Poke him down with Q, dodge his barrels, and all-in when he's low. His R can disengage your ult, so bait it before committing.
Gragas.scaling: Even — both have strong teamfight presence with different strengths
Gragas.positioning: Dodge his Q barrel by walking sideways — it has a visible landing zone. When you ult in, expect his R to push you away and save E to re-engage.

Ashe.trading_pattern: Aphelios can out-trade with Severum but Ashe's slows allow safe distance trading. Look for extended trades when Severum is active.
Ashe.ability_to_dodge: Ashe R (Enchanted Crystal Arrow) — dodge by positioning behind minions or moving unpredictably.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with Chakram stacks, First Legendary item (IE or Kraken)

Ashe.early_game: Trade evenly in short engagements. Avoid getting slowed in the wave as it locks you down for burst.
Ashe.mid_game: Position to avoid her ultimate from river. Use terrain to limit her arrow accuracy.
Ashe.late_game: Kite around her slows, prioritize grouping where her ult has harder time landing on your entire team.
Ashe.win_condition: Scale hard into team fights and out-dps her with proper positioning. Never get caught alone.
Ashe.scaling: Even — Both scale at similar rates, positioning determines fights
Ashe.positioning: Space away from walls in river to ensure you have room to dodge Ashe's ultimate. Stay mobile with Aphelios' reposition tools.

Caitlyn.trading_pattern: Caitlyn dominate early trades with superior range. Avoid standing in her trap zones and use minion wave for protection.
Caitlyn.ability_to_dodge: Caitlyn Headshot (auto attacks) — stay behind minions early, never face-check bushes.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with access to all weapons, completed Kraken Slayer
power_spikes.enemy: Level 2-3 with Headshot stacks, RFC (extended range)

Caitlyn.early_game: Play safe and avoid her trap zones. Don't contest early poke. Farm under tower if needed.
Caitlyn.mid_game: Group with team and leverage numbers. Aphelios scales much harder into mid game.
Caitlyn.late_game: Use superior damage in team fights to out-dps her range advantage. Stick with teammates.
Caitlyn.win_condition: Survive laning phase with minimal losses, then scale into late game where you deal more sustained damage.
Caitlyn.scaling: Aphelios heavily outscales — Caitlyn strong early, Aphelios dominates mid/late
Caitlyn.positioning: Hug the side of the lane away from Caitlyn to minimize trap placement. Space back when she moves forward aggressively.

Draven.trading_pattern: Draven can out-trade with catchaxe stacks, but Aphelios can kite if you use Infernum. Space carefully around his axe positions.
Draven.ability_to_dodge: Draven E (Cataclysm) — position near walls so you have room to dodge sideways, never get stuck against tower.

power_spikes.you: Level 5 with Chakram slow, Kraken Slayer completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 1-3 with stacked catchaxes, completed Duskblade

Draven.early_game: Focus on not feeding his axes. Use minion positioning to avoid his free gold generation. Play for scaling.
Draven.mid_game: Group with team and use range advantage to kite his engages. Infernum spread helps against supports too.
Draven.late_game: Proper positioning ensures you out-dps him massively. Never let him get close enough for true damage burst.
Draven.win_condition: Avoid his all-in threats early, then abuse superior late-game scaling and range in team fights.
Draven.scaling: Aphelios heavily outscales once Draven loses gold efficiency
Draven.positioning: Keep distance and avoid center lane where his E can trap you. Position away from walls during his engagement attempts.

Ezreal.trading_pattern: Ezreal has poke but Aphelios can out-trade in extended fights. Use Severum sustain and Chakram root when he mispositions.
Ezreal.ability_to_dodge: Ezreal Q (Mystic Shot) — never bunch up in the wave, stay spread to minimize poke damage.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with Chakram, Kraken Slayer completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3-4 with Sheen spike, muramana completed

Ezreal.early_game: Trade evenly and use sustain from Severum to outlast his poke. Don't get caught out of position near walls.
Ezreal.mid_game: Group with team and leverage superior sustained damage. Ezreal will scale but so will you.
Ezreal.late_game: In team fights, position where you can freely deal damage while using cooldowns to peel threats.
Ezreal.win_condition: Scale with Ezreal but outposition him in team fights. Make proper use of each weapon rotation.
Ezreal.scaling: Even — Both scale similarly, skill and positioning matter more
Ezreal.positioning: Spread horizontally in the wave to reduce his Q poke efficiency. Use terrain to limit his kite ability.

Jhin.trading_pattern: Jhin's every-fourth-shot damage is high but slow attack speed works against extended trades. Look for Severum or Chakram trades.
Jhin.ability_to_dodge: Jhin E (Captive Audience trap) — avoid walking into his trap zones, especially in river control situations.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with all weapons available, Kraken Slayer completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with ultimate critical damage, completed Collector

Jhin.early_game: Trade when his attack animations are long. Focus on safe farming and avoiding his trap zones.
Jhin.mid_game: Position to engage on his longer attack animations. Group and use numbers advantage.
Jhin.late_game: In team fights, avoid his ultimate angles by staying behind cover. Persistent damage out-values his burst.
Jhin.win_condition: Survive early laning, scale into team fights where your sustained dps exceeds his burst potential.
Jhin.scaling: Aphelios slightly outscales — sustained dps beats burst setup
Jhin.positioning: Position to avoid his R ultimate angles by staying near cover or teammates. Never group closely for his W.

Jinx.ability_to_dodge: Jinx E (Flame Chompers) — avoid her trap zones and never jump into them without proper setup.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with Chakram, Kraken Slayer first item
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with rocket form, completed IE

Jinx.early_game: Trade with Chakram poke when she switches to rocket form slow attack. Maintain spacing.
Jinx.mid_game: Group with team and leverage Aphelios' flexibility. Jinx can't duel late game scaling.
Jinx.late_game: In extended team fights, your sustained damage and range flexibility let you kite her and trade favorably.
Jinx.win_condition: Survive laning phase, scale harder, and position well in team fights where range and sustained dps matter.
Jinx.scaling: Aphelios outscales — sustained dps beats burst potential
Jinx.positioning: Stay spread to avoid her W and E. Never fight in tight chokepoints where she can trap your entire team.

Kai'Sa.trading_pattern: Kai'Sa has stronger early trades but Aphelios matches her as weapons unlock. Use Severum to sustain through her poke.
Kai'Sa.ability_to_dodge: Kai'Sa E (Supercharge) — dodge her follow-up trades by moving away from where she lands.

power_spikes.you: Level 5-6 with access to all weapons, Kraken Slayer completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3-4 with Manamune spike, evolved E

Kai'Sa.early_game: Play safe and sustain with Severum. Let her push and look for jungler ganks on overextension.
Kai'Sa.mid_game: Aphelios scaling equals Kai'Sa's scaling at this point. Group and leverage team fights.
Kai'Sa.late_game: With proper positioning, you out-dps her in extended fights. Use Chakram and Infernum to control space.
Kai'Sa.win_condition: Survive early lane poke, reach mid game on par, then scale with flexibility of weapon switching.
Kai'Sa.scaling: Even — Both scale similarly after 2-3 items
Kai'Sa.positioning: Space horizontally in lane to make her E repositioning less valuable. Group as 5 in mid/late for team fight control.

Kalista.trading_pattern: Kalista dominates early trades with rapid attacks and hop mechanics. Aphelios wins extended fights with sustain.
Kalista.ability_to_dodge: Kalista W (Sentinel) — avoid grouping with teammates, deny her W information by spreading.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with Chakram root, Kraken Slayer completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 2-3 with hop stacking, completed Spear

Kalista.early_game: Play extremely safe and focus on farming. Don't engage her hops as she wins those trades.
Kalista.mid_game: Group with team and leverage numbers. Kalista struggles without early lead.
Kalista.late_game: Massive advantage to Aphelios. You can kite and out-dps her, especially with Infernum and Chakram.
Kalista.win_condition: Survive laning without falling behind, then outscale massively where she has no late game damage.
Kalista.scaling: Aphelios heavily outscales — Kalista is an early game champion
Kalista.positioning: Stay spread to deny her W value. Keep maximum distance during fights to make her hops less effective.

Kog'Maw.trading_pattern: Kog'Maw has poke and armor shred but Aphelios trades evenly with Severum sustain and Chakram utility.
Kog'Maw.ability_to_dodge: Kog'Maw Q (Bio-Arcane Barrage) — avoid getting marked, stay spread to reduce shred efficiency.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with Chakram slow, Kraken Slayer completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with true damage scaling, completed Nashor's Tooth

Kog'Maw.early_game: Trade evenly and use positioning to minimize his Q shred. Focus on farming safely.
Kog'Maw.mid_game: Group with team, your weapons give flexibility to poke and engage safely.
Kog'Maw.late_game: Kog'Maw slightly outscales in extended fights due to true damage, but you can kite and position better.
Kog'Maw.win_condition: Scale evenly and position better in late game fights. Use Chakram and Infernum to maintain kiting distance.
Kog'Maw.scaling: Kog'Maw slightly favored — true damage and range armor shred scale well
Kog'Maw.positioning: Stay spread and mobile to avoid his Q shred stacking. Position at max range where his true damage is less impactful.

Lucian.trading_pattern: Lucian strong early with duelist stats but Aphelios sustain matches. Use Severum to heal through his poke.
Lucian.ability_to_dodge: Lucian E (Relentless Pursuit) — position away from walls so you have room to kite his reposition.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with all weapons, Kraken Slayer completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 2-3 with double attacks, early crit mythic

Lucian.early_game: Play safe and sustain with Severum. Let Lucian push if he wants to, focus on farming.
Lucian.mid_game: Aphelios weapons give flexibility to trade and scale. Group and let your utility shine.
Lucian.late_game: Lucian falls off hard. You out-dps and out-range him with proper positioning.
Lucian.win_condition: Survive early lane, scale with flexibility, then dominate late game where Lucian has no impact.
Lucian.scaling: Aphelios heavily outscales — Lucian is early/mid game champion
Lucian.positioning: Keep distance and use terrain to limit his E repositioning. Never let him get inside you.

Miss Fortune.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune out-trades with Q bounces and E slow. Aphelios' sustain mitigates some damage but she dictates early trades.
Miss Fortune.ability_to_dodge: Miss Fortune R (Bullet Time) — dodge by moving perpendicular to ultimate direction, never stand still.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with Chakram, Kraken Slayer completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 1-2 with Q bounce harass, early crit items

Miss Fortune.early_game: Play extremely safe. Her early poke is oppressive. Focus on surviving and farming under tower.
Miss Fortune.mid_game: Avoid her ultimate by positioning behind cover. Group and use team to block her engage.
Miss Fortune.late_game: Aphelios scales better but MF's ultimate is always a threat. Proper positioning around cover wins fights.
Miss Fortune.win_condition: Survive oppressive early game, scale into mid-game strength, position perfectly to avoid ultimate in fights.
Miss Fortune.scaling: Aphelios scales better late game but Miss Fortune's early aggression can snowball
Miss Fortune.positioning: Hug cover to block Miss Fortune's ultimate. Never stand in open during team fights where she can channel freely.

Nilah.trading_pattern: Nilah wins early extended trades with healing and burst. Aphelios must respect her all-in potential and keep distance.
Nilah.ability_to_dodge: Nilah E (Splitter) — dodge by moving perpendicular to her dash, position away from walls where she can trap you.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with full weapon rotation, Kraken Slayer completed

Nilah.early_game: Play extremely safe. Don't let her get near you. Use Chakram to maintain distance.
Nilah.mid_game: Group with team to reduce her effectiveness. Aphelios' flexibility helps against her aggression.
Nilah.late_game: You out-dps her in group fights but never get isolated. Nilah punishes single targets.
Nilah.win_condition: Survive her early aggression, never get caught alone, leverage team in group fights.
Nilah.scaling: Even in pure stats but Nilah's early lead can snowball — focus on surviving
Nilah.positioning: Stay near teammates at all times. Never push forward alone. Use minion wave as spacing tool against her.

Samira.ability_to_dodge: Samira R (Inferno Trigger) — dodge by moving out of her attack range entirely, maintain distance.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 2-3 with style stacking, completed Collector

Samira.early_game: Play safe and don't engage her trades. Let her push if she wants, focus on farming.
Samira.mid_game: Group with team. Samira struggles in group fights where she can't all-in single targets.
Samira.late_game: Aphelios scales better. You out-dps her in proper team fights with positioning.
Samira.win_condition: Survive her oppressive early game, scale without dying, execute proper spacing in team fights.
Samira.scaling: Aphelios outscales — Samira is early/mid burst champion
Samira.positioning: Keep maximum distance from Samira at all times. Use terrain to limit her engagement angles.

Sivir.ability_to_dodge: Sivir E (Spell Shield) — save burst combos for after her shield is down. Use auto-attacks to bait blocks.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with ultimate engage, completed RFC

Sivir.late_game: You out-dps her significantly. Proper positioning ensures you win extended fights.

Sivir.scaling: Aphelios outscales — more sustained dps in late game
Sivir.positioning: Space back against her range advantage. Use minion wave for protection during early laning.

Tristana.ability_to_dodge: Tristana E (Explosive Charge) — position where the bomb range can't stack properly, use range to kite.

Tristana.early_game: Space back from her all-in range. Don't let her stack E charges. Farm safely under tower if needed.
Tristana.mid_game: Aphelios scaling equals or exceeds Tristana's. Group and use team to control fights.
Tristana.late_game: You out-range and out-dps her in proper team fights. Position to avoid her all-ins.

Tristana.positioning: Keep distance from Tristana to prevent E stacking. Never let her get inside your effective range.

Twitch.positioning: Dodge his R spray by moving perpendicular. Use terrain to block his attacks in team fights.

Qiyana.trading_pattern: Qiyana has burst and CC. Respect her element advantage and avoid close trades early.

Gwen.trading_pattern: Keep Gwen at max range and use W (Astral Flight) to continually poke with your empowered Q (Breath of Light) from outside her melee range. Never let her close the gap — if she dashes in, use R (Falling Star) or E (Singularity) to knock her back and re-establish distance immediately.
Gwen.ability_to_dodge: Gwen's E (Needlework) — her multi-needle ult that fires in rapid succession. Sidestep the first needle to avoid being slowed, which makes subsequent needles much harder for her to hit. Stay mobile with Astral Flight to make tracking difficult.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Falling Star gives you hard CC and a global presence to influence other lanes) and Shadowflame + Rabadon's Deathcap (damage spikes dramatically)
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Gwen's Needlework gives her reliable ranged poke) and Trinity Force + Nashor's Tooth (massive DPS spike if she reaches you)

Gwen.early_game: Levels 1–5 are Gwen's window — she can match your damage if she gets inside your Q range. Stay at max Q distance, harass with Breath of Light procs, and avoid letting her activate Hallowed Mist to block your poke. Do not fight unless she's significantly poked down.
Gwen.mid_game: Once you hit 6, prioritize roaming with Comet of Legend to snowball side lanes — Gwen has no way to follow globally. Use your stacked Starsurge to land a CC chain in skirmishes and avoid 1v1 extended trades where her Hallowed Mist can shield her from your damage.
Gwen.late_game: In teamfights, position at the backline and unleash Falling Star into the enemy cluster — do not frontline against Gwen. Let your team engage while you deal poke damage from a safe angle; Gwen's Hallowed Mist only follows her once, so if she repositions she loses the protection.
Gwen.win_condition: Out-roam Gwen after level 6 to build a lead elsewhere, then outscale her with AP items in the mid-to-late game. Deny her from ever reaching melee range in lane by using Astral Flight's mobility to reposition constantly — if she can't touch you, she can't win lane.
Gwen.scaling: Aurelion Sol outscales Gwen significantly past 2–3 items; Gwen has a narrow melee-range threat window in the mid game but falls off relative to Aurelion Sol's late-game burst and AoE once he completes Rabadon's Deathcap.
Gwen.positioning: Hug the outer edge of your minion wave so Gwen cannot dash onto you through minions, forcing her to path around and telegraphing her engage. When she activates Hallowed Mist (W), walk perpendicular and out of its radius — it only follows her once — then re-enter poke range after it's displaced.

Vel'Koz.ability_to_dodge: Vel'Koz E (Tractor Beam) — dodge sideways or back immediately. This is his only CC.
Vel'Koz.early_game: Play safe and let him push. His range advantage is overwhelming early.
Vel'Koz.mid_game: Look for flanking ult opportunities. Vel'Koz has low mobility if caught off-guard.
Vel'Koz.late_game: He outscales in poke damage. Win through flanks or teamfights where his range is negated.
Vel'Koz.win_condition: Get a roaming lead or flank with ult. Don't fight him directly in long-range exchanges.
Vel'Koz.scaling: Vel'Koz outscales — his sustained poke damage is overwhelming late game
Vel'Koz.positioning: Use your soldiers as damage barriers. Don't overextend without soldiers in range.

Brand.ability_to_dodge: Brand Q (Sear) — dodge by stepping sideways or behind minions.
Brand.early_game: Play defensively. Brand wins early poke wars. Farm safely.
Brand.mid_game: Use soldiers to farm from distance. Look for flanking ult plays.
Brand.late_game: Brand scales better into teamfights. Avoid direct engagements unless you're flanking.

Brand.scaling: Brand outscales — his sustained burn and teamfight damage is stronger
Brand.positioning: Keep distance and use soldiers for trading, not direct poke.

Xerath.trading_pattern: Xerath outranges you. Play around minion waves and poke when he's vulnerable.

Xerath.mid_game: Look for flanking opportunities. Xerath lacks mobility when caught.

Xerath.positioning: Use minions for cover. Try to close distance gradually.

Annie.trading_pattern: Annie has early burst with Tibbers. Trade when her stun is down, retreat when it's up.

Annie.early_game: Trade when her stun counter is low. Respect her all-in when she has 4+ stacks.
Annie.mid_game: Position away from walls where she can trap you. Use soldiers to poke safely.
Annie.late_game: Teamfights favor positioning. Keep distance and use ult to escape her Tibbers engage.
Annie.win_condition: Win through soldier positioning in teamfights. Avoid her stun engagement.
Annie.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
Annie.positioning: Stay away from walls and keep distance when her stun is about to come up.

LeBlanc.early_game: Play defensively. LeBlanc has superior all-in damage.
LeBlanc.mid_game: Use soldiers to farm from safety. Look for ult escape plays.
LeBlanc.late_game: Both scale similarly. Teamfight position determines winner.

Ekko.trading_pattern: Use your soldiers to poke Ekko from max range before he can proc Z-Drive Resonance (passive). Position soldiers between his minions and his body so you can last-hit and project threat simultaneously — if he steps up to CS, auto-attack through your soldier immediately. Never let him land a full W+E+Q combo; retreat behind your wave the moment his W circle appears.
Ekko.ability_to_dodge: Ekko's W (Parallel Convergence) — the stun zone takes 3 seconds to arm after cast; watch for the ghost image on cast to predict where the zone landed and sidestep immediately, or use your R (Emperor's Divide) to knock him away before the stun detonates.

power_spikes.you: Level 9 (soldiers max) + Shadowflame first item — sustained poke damage peaks and you can threaten Ekko at range before he can safely engage
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 + Hextech Rocketbelt — Ekko gains his ult safety net and enough AP to burst you; this is when he will aggressively look for all-ins

Ekko.early_game: Levels 1-5 are your best window — Ekko deals low damage pre-6 and has limited mobility, so poke him with Q-W-soldier autos every time he steps up to farm. Keep your soldiers active near the wave so you can threaten him out of CS; a 20-30 HP lead pre-6 makes all-ins much riskier for him.
Ekko.mid_game: After Ekko backs and buys Rocketbelt, respect his all-in potential at all times. Farm safely with soldiers at max range, avoid standing on the same side as his W ghost, and only commit to fights if he has burned his E or R. Prioritize pushing and roaming to generate a global lead rather than fighting him 1v1.
Ekko.late_game: Azir becomes a teamfight monster with fully stacked soldiers and Shurima Shuffle (R), but Ekko can one-shot you if he flanks. Always stay behind your frontline in teamfights — never stand adjacent to your teammates as a cluster target. If Ekko uses R (Chronobreak) to engage your team, immediately use your R to knock him and his team back before they regroup.
Ekko.win_condition: Survive the mid-game power spike (levels 6-11) by not giving Ekko free kills, accumulate a CS lead via long-range soldier farming, and hit your two-item spike (Shadowflame + Rabadon's Deathcap or Void Staff) to outscale. Force Ekko to use his R defensively or waste Zhonya's, then clean up with your own R to create winning teamfight angles.
Ekko.scaling: Ekko wins the mid-game power curve (levels 6-14); Azir outscales hard in the very late game (3+ items) with superior sustained DPS, wall utility, and teamfight control — but only if he avoids getting one-shot by a fed Ekko.
Ekko.positioning: Always stand behind your soldier hitbox, not beside it — this puts a soldier between you and Ekko, forcing him to walk through soldier attacks to reach you. Hug the side of the lane opposite his W ghost at all times, and pre-position your E (Shifting Sands) escape angle toward your tower before he can close the gap with Phase Dive.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Place a soldier between Nasus and the minion wave via W, then Q through it to poke him every time he steps up to last-hit. Weave in auto-attacks through your soldier and immediately back off before he can Wither you.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Wither (E) is the single most dangerous ability — if it lands, you lose all mobility and become a sitting target. Hold your E (Shifting Sands) specifically as a Wither counter; the moment Nasus begins his E animation, dash away immediately so the slow expires before you're in danger.

power_spikes.you: Nashor's Tooth (first item) — your soldier attack speed and sustained DPS ramp up dramatically, letting you shred Nasus whenever he attempts to Q a minion
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 + Trinity Force — Nasus gains his ultimate for tankiness and damage amplification, and Trinity Force gives him enough burst to punish any overextension

Nasus.early_game: From level 1 onward, plant soldiers between your minions and Nasus to deny him safe Q stacks — he must either take free poke or miss CS. Maintain max soldier range at all times; never walk up to melee range as he can Wither-Q you for a devastating short trade.
Nasus.mid_game: After your first item, shove the wave and rotate to contest objectives or assist your jungler — the top lane is shorter with fewer gank routes, so translating your lane lead elsewhere is more valuable than sitting in the 1v1. Use Emperor's Divide defensively if Nasus tries to all-in, and keep denying his stack farming with poke whenever you return.
Nasus.late_game: In teamfights, use Emperor's Divide as a wall to peel Nasus away from your backline or to cut him off from your carries. Stay behind your soldiers and keep poking — never let Nasus freecast his Q in a prolonged fight, as his stacks at this stage can one-shot almost anyone.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny Nasus his Q stacks in the early and mid game by constantly zoning him with soldier poke; even a 150-stack deficit cripples his late-game threat. Coordinate jungle ganks to force him off the wave and translate your lane priority into dragon and Rift Herald control.
Nasus.scaling: Nasus outscales significantly after 3+ items and 300+ Q stacks (~25-30 minutes). Azir must build a meaningful early lead and deny stacks to prevent Nasus from ever reaching his hyperscaling window.
Nasus.positioning: Always stand at max soldier range (roughly 2 turret-widths away from Nasus) so his Wither cannot reach you unless he Ghost-charges in. Avoid hugging the lane wall — keep yourself centered so you have E escape angles in both directions, and never let him push you into a corner where your E has nowhere safe to land.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Farm safely with E poke from range. Land Q when he commits to his own Q combo — he's locked in animation and can't dodge. Don't trade without Q up.

Aatrox.early_game: He bullies you with Q poke. Farm with E and don't overextend. If he wastes his Q combo and misses sweet spots, punish with Q-W-auto. Your passive sustain keeps you healthy for safe farming.
Aatrox.mid_game: You start winning. Your burst with R is massive and he can't sustain through true damage. Look for Q into W silence to deny his combo, then Feast when he's low.
Aatrox.late_game: You outscale as a tank. Your R execute ignores his healing. In teamfights your Q knockup disrupts his entire team. Stack Feast on minions/objectives to become unkillable.
Aatrox.win_condition: Survive lane, farm passively, and stack Feast. Post-6 you can kill him with a Q-W-R burst combo. Don't let him poke you below Feast execute range without retaliating.
Aatrox.scaling: Cho'Gath favored — infinite health stacking and true damage execute outscale Aatrox's sustain-based kit
Aatrox.positioning: Stay behind your minion wave to block his W chain. When he winds up Q, walk toward him to avoid the sweet spot zone rather than away from it.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast can contest his ult execute), Hextech Rocketbelt + Riftmaker

Darius.early_game: Very hard lane. He zones you off CS with passive threat. Farm with E and Q from max range. If pulled, Q-W immediately and walk away — never stay for extended trades.
Darius.mid_game: You can start fighting back if even in gold. Your Q-W-R burst can kill him before he stacks passive. Rush Plated Steelcaps to reduce his auto damage.
Darius.late_game: You outscale hard. He gets kited in teamfights while you're a massive frontline with AoE CC. Your Feast outdamages his Noxian Guillotine against tanks.
Darius.win_condition: Don't feed him kills early. Farm safely and outscale. Your teamfight is infinitely better than his if you go even in lane.
Darius.scaling: Cho'Gath heavily favored — Darius falls off in teamfights while Cho'Gath becomes an unkillable CC machine
Darius.positioning: Stay well outside his E range (535 units). Farm with E from safety. If he activates Ghost, immediately back off toward tower — you cannot outrun him without boots.

Garen.trading_pattern: Poke him with E auto-attacks to prevent his passive regen. When he runs at you with Q, drop your own Q under yourself and W to silence him before he silences you.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q — his empowered auto silences you; W him first to silence him before he reaches you, canceling his engage.
Garen.early_game: Even lane. Your E poke prevents his passive regen. When he runs at you with Q, use W silence to cancel his engage. Farm normally and don't let him freely regen.
Garen.mid_game: You match his sustain with passive and outburst him with Feast. His ult executes based on missing HP — don't sit at low HP thinking you're safe.
Garen.late_game: You outscale. Your CC, true damage execute, and tank stats are more valuable in teamfights. He becomes a splitpusher but you're a better teamfighter.
Garen.win_condition: Keep poking with E to deny passive regen. At level 6, the ult execute race favors you because yours deals true damage and scales with stacks.
Garen.scaling: Cho'Gath favored — better teamfight CC and true damage scaling outvalue Garen's simple kit
Garen.positioning: Keep attacking him periodically with E empowered autos to prevent his passive regen. Stand slightly behind minions so his Q run has a longer path.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: Dodge his E pull and poke with E. Don't let him auto-attack you three times — his passive deals heavy sustained magic damage. Short trades only, Q under yourself and walk away.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser E — Death's Grasp pull sets up his full combo; sidestep it and punish with Q while it's on cooldown.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast true damage ignores his shield), Hextech Rocketbelt
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Realm of Death steals your stats), Riftmaker

Mordekaiser.early_game: He wins extended trades with passive DoT. Dodge his E and poke with your own E. Farm passively and don't let him proc passive three-hit. Short trades favor you if you land Q.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: His Death Realm steals your stats which is devastating when you have Feast stacks. Consider rushing QSS to escape his ult. Your Q-W-R burst can kill him before he accumulates passive damage.
Mordekaiser.late_game: His ult removing you from teamfights is a huge threat. Stay near carries so if he ults you, he loses access to the rest of your team. Build QSS if his R is the deciding factor.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Dodge his E, land your Q, and burst with R. Don't let him drag you into extended trades. QSS his ultimate if it's causing problems.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Mordekaiser slightly favored — his Death Realm stat steal becomes more punishing as Cho'Gath stacks Feast
Mordekaiser.positioning: Stay offset from his E line — it's a straight pull. Keep at E auto-attack range and don't walk into his passive proc range unless landing Q first.

Sett.trading_pattern: Poke with E and auto from range. When he walks at you, Q behind yourself to knock him up and W to silence, then walk away. Never stand in the center of his Haymaker.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W — Haymaker center deals true damage; sidestep left or right to only take the reduced outer damage.
Sett.early_game: He's stronger in melee early. Poke with E and farm. If he E pulls you, immediately Q under yourself and W to disengage. Don't let him build grit for a big Haymaker.
Sett.mid_game: You start outscaling. Your burst combo deletes him before he can build grit. Land Q, W silence, and Feast — he dies before getting a big Haymaker off.
Sett.late_game: You heavily outscale. He's a mediocre frontline while you're a massive tank with true damage execute. Your teamfight is far superior.
Sett.win_condition: Survive early, farm safely, and burst him post-6. Don't let him build grit — short burst trades deny his Haymaker value.
Sett.scaling: Cho'Gath heavily favored — Sett falls off hard while Cho'Gath becomes an unkillable frontline
Sett.positioning: Always sidestep his Haymaker. When he E pulls you, don't panic — Q-W immediately and walk sideways out of his W center.

Renekton.trading_pattern: Let him push into you and farm under tower. His empowered W stun is brutal — Q under yourself when he dashes in to trade knockup for stun.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W — empowered stun locks you down for a full combo; when you see him dash in with fury, immediately Q yourself and W to silence.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (empowered W + Q trade), Blade of the Ruined King

Renekton.early_game: He zones you hard. Watch his fury bar — empowered W is devastating. Farm with Q and E from range. Let him push and farm under tower where you're safer.
Renekton.mid_game: You outscale from here. He falls off and your Feast stacks make you unkillable. Start looking for all-ins when his fury is low.
Renekton.late_game: You massively outscale. He's a mediocre bruiser while you're a massive tank. Your teamfight CC is worth ten of his.
Renekton.win_condition: Survive lane without dying. You outscale him hard — just don't feed him early kills that let him snowball.
Renekton.scaling: Cho'Gath massively favored — Renekton falls off a cliff while Cho'Gath scales infinitely
Renekton.positioning: Stay far back and farm with E. When he dashes in, Q under yourself immediately. Don't stand near low HP minions he can dash through.

Jax.trading_pattern: When Jax activates Counter Strike, don't auto him — use Q under him and W silence. Your abilities go through his E dodge. After stun ends, walk away.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E — Counter Strike dodges your autos but NOT your Q or W; use abilities through his E and back off before the stun.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast true damage ignores his ult resistances), Hextech Rocketbelt

Jax.early_game: You actually win this lane. Your Q goes through his Counter Strike and your E poke chunks him. When he jumps on you, Q under yourself, W silence, and walk away.
Jax.mid_game: You outburst him with Feast. He can't dodge your true damage execute. Silence him with W when he jumps in to prevent his E recast.
Jax.late_game: He outscales in pure 1v1 duels, but your teamfight is far superior. Group and use your AoE CC instead of trying to match him sidelane.
Jax.win_condition: Bully him in lane with abilities that go through Counter Strike. Don't try to auto-attack trade — use Q and W exclusively.
Jax.scaling: Even — Jax outduels late but Cho'Gath teamfights better with CC and tankiness
Jax.positioning: When he leaps onto you, immediately Q + W. Never auto-attack when his E is spinning. Walk away after landing abilities.

Fiora.trading_pattern: Hug walls to hide vitals. Farm with E from range. If she lunges in, Q under yourself and W to silence — don't try to trade autos against her vital procs.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W — Riposte blocks everything including your Q knockup and Feast; bait it with Q first, then Feast after Riposte is down.
Fiora.early_game: She bullies with vital procs. Stand against walls to deny vital spawns. Farm with E and Q from max range. Don't trade autos — she wins every auto trade.
Fiora.mid_game: She's a constant threat in duels. Never Feast predictably — she Ripostes it for a free stun. Bait W with Q, then R after it's down.
Fiora.late_game: She hard outscales in duels. Her Grand Challenge healing is massive. Don't try to match her sidelane. Group for teamfights where she's weaker.
Fiora.win_condition: Land Q-W silence into Feast when her Riposte is on cooldown. Force her to Riposte your Q, then Feast in the 24-second Riposte CD window.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora heavily favored — true damage vitals shred tanks and Grand Challenge makes her unkillable in duels
Fiora.positioning: Hug walls to deny vital procs on exposed sides. Stand at max E range and poke. Never use Feast when her Riposte is available.

Ornn.trading_pattern: He outtrades you with Brittle passive. Dodge his Q pillar and avoid standing near it when he W's for the Brittle proc. Farm safely and don't engage unless his W is down.
Ornn.ability_to_dodge: Ornn W — Bellows Breath applies Brittle which amplifies his next auto; dodge sideways out of the flame cone and avoid the Brittle proc auto.
Ornn.early_game: He outtrades you surprisingly hard with Brittle procs. Play safe and farm. Dodge his Q-W combo. Your Q is your best trading tool — land it when he walks up.
Ornn.mid_game: Both are tanks but he provides item upgrades for his team. Push for objectives before his team gets upgraded items. Your R execute helps in neutral objectives.
Ornn.late_game: He outscales through item upgrades. His R is a massive teamfight engage. Try to end before his team gets all upgrades. Dodge his R in teamfights — it's game-changing.
Ornn.win_condition: Go even in lane and look for picks with Q-W-R burst. His value scales with game time through item upgrades, so push for early objectives.
Ornn.scaling: Ornn heavily favored — item upgrades for the whole team make him the best scaling tank in the game
Ornn.positioning: Stand away from his Q pillar — he combos W through it for Brittle. Dodge sideways out of his W cone.

Camille.trading_pattern: When she E hookshots toward you, drop Q where she'll land. Your Q-W combo catches her post-dash before she can follow up. Silence denies her Q2 true damage hit.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E — Hookshot stun; place Q on her landing spot to knock her up before she can combo you.
Camille.early_game: Even lane. Respect her passive shield when it's up. When she hookshots in, Q her landing spot and W silence. She can't combo you if silenced.
Camille.mid_game: Her R locks you in place — be careful near walls where she can corner you. Your burst with Feast can catch her off-guard if she overstays.
Camille.late_game: She outscales in sidelane. Group for teamfights where your AoE CC is more impactful. Her R isolating you can be problematic, but your tankiness makes you survive it.
Camille.win_condition: Catch her with Q-W silence when she commits to hookshot. Feast her when she's locked in melee range. Don't try to match her sidelane late.
Camille.scaling: Camille favored — her true damage and split push outvalue Cho'Gath in side lanes
Camille.positioning: Keep distance from walls so her hookshot travel is longer and easier to react to. Q her landing spot every time she commits.

Gnar.trading_pattern: Farm safely against Mini Gnar — he kites you. When he transforms to Mega, respect his R near walls but you can trade into him since he's melee and slower.
Gnar.ability_to_dodge: Mega Gnar R — stay away from walls when his rage is full so his ult can't slam you into terrain for the stun.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast burst on Mini Gnar), Hextech Rocketbelt

Gnar.early_game: Mini Gnar kites you endlessly. Take Doran's Shield and farm. When he wastes boomerang, walk up for an E trade. Track his rage bar — he's vulnerable during Mini form.
Gnar.mid_game: You can one-shot Mini Gnar with Q-W-R combo. Wait for Mega to time out and burst him in Mini form. He's squishy and your combo deletes him.
Gnar.late_game: Mega Gnar teamfight is one of the best. Avoid grouping near walls. Your own teamfight is strong — use Q on clumped enemies and Feast priority targets.
Gnar.win_condition: Burst Mini Gnar with Q-W-R when Mega form is on cooldown. He's squishy in Mini and your full combo one-shots.
Gnar.scaling: Even — both are strong teamfighters with different strengths; Mega Gnar engage vs Cho'Gath sustain and execute
Gnar.positioning: Never fight near walls when his rage is full. Track his rage bar and engage when Mega is unavailable.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Don't fight her with full passive stacks. Q under yourself when she dashes in. W silence prevents her from using Q to dash and E to stun. Without her combo she's helpless.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia Q — her Bladesurge dash resets on kills; don't stand near dying minions or she'll build a dash highway into you.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast burst on her squishy base stats), Hextech Rocketbelt
power_spikes.enemy: Level 4-5 (full passive stacking), Blade of the Ruined King

Irelia.early_game: Respect her stacked passive. Farm safely and don't let her build a dash chain through your minions. W silence shuts down her entire combo.
Irelia.mid_game: Your silence is her worst nightmare. Q-W-R burst kills her fast since she's squishy despite being in melee. Time Feast for when her W damage reduction is down.
Irelia.late_game: Both scale decently. Your silence and knockup shut down her dive. In teamfights, save Q-W for when she dashes into your backline.
Irelia.win_condition: Silence her when she commits and she can't dash, stun, or damage reduce. Feast her during W downtime.
Irelia.scaling: Even — Irelia is a strong duelist but Cho'Gath's silence shuts down her kit in teamfights
Irelia.positioning: Stand away from dying minions so she can't dash through them to reach you. When she commits with E stun, buffer Q on yourself.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Punish his stacking with Q knockup and E poke. Zone him off CS whenever your Q is available. Your R stacking outpaces his if you deny him farm.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W — Wither slow is crippling; rush Mercury's Treads and use Q-W from range rather than trying to auto-attack him through the slow.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R stat boost + stacking threshold), 200+ stacks + Sheen

Nasus.early_game: Bully him off CS. Your Q interrupts his stacking and your E poke is free damage. He can't fight back early — zone him completely.
Nasus.mid_game: Keep denying him. Your Feast stacks grow while his Q stacks stay low. Push tower and roam when he's too weak to contest.
Nasus.late_game: If he's stacked, his Wither + Q hurts. But your true damage execute and tankiness match his. In teamfights your AoE CC is more useful than his single-target damage.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny his stacks early and outscale with Feast. You both want to scale, but you scale faster if you bully him properly.
Nasus.scaling: Even — both scale infinitely, but Cho'Gath's Feast is unconditional while Nasus needs to farm Q stacks
Nasus.positioning: Stand between Nasus and the dying minion wave. Zone him off every last hit with Q threat.

Riven.trading_pattern: Q under yourself when she dashes in. W silence cancels her entire combo — she can't Q, E, or R while silenced. She's helpless without her ability chain.
