Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven Q3 — the third Q knockup is her key engage tool; Q under yourself or walk backward to dodge it.
Riven.early_game: You counter her. W silence prevents her from comboing. When she dashes in, Q under yourself, W silence, auto with E, and walk away. She can't do anything while silenced.
Riven.mid_game: You hard-counter her. She can't combo through your silence. Look for Q-W-R kills when she overextends.
Riven.late_game: She has high CDR combos but your silence still shuts her down. In teamfights, save W for when she dives your backline.
Riven.win_condition: Silence her every time she engages. Without her ability chain, Riven is a minion. Feast her when she's locked out of abilities.
Riven.scaling: Cho'Gath favored — silence completely negates Riven's ability-dependent kit at every stage of the game
Riven.positioning: When she Q3's onto you, immediately W. The silence locks her out of E shield, second R, and further Q casts.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Your Q goes through his Wind Wall. Silence him with W when he dashes in. He can't dash, windwall, or ult while silenced. Your E poke shreds him.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo E — he dashes through minions to dodge Q; place Q slightly behind where he's dashing to, not on his current position.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast kills through his shield), Hextech Rocketbelt
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R combo off tornado), Infinity Edge

Yasuo.early_game: You counter Yasuo hard. Your Q-W silence shuts down his entire kit. When he dashes in, Q-W and auto with E. His windwall doesn't block any of your abilities.
Yasuo.mid_game: Continue bullying. He can't fight you. Your silence removes his ability to dash and windwall. Feast him when he overextends.
Yasuo.late_game: He outscales with crit but your CC still shuts him down. In teamfights, save Q-W for when he dashes into your team.
Yasuo.win_condition: Silence him in every trade. Your abilities bypass windwall. He literally cannot play the game when silenced.
Yasuo.scaling: Yasuo slightly favored late — his crit scaling outpaces tank builds, but your CC still disrupts him in teamfights
Yasuo.positioning: Fight away from minion waves to limit his E dashing. Place Q where he's dashing to, not where he currently is.

K'Sante.trading_pattern: Dodge his Q3 dash and punish with your own Q-W combo. He's weak early and your poke chunks him. Respect his ult form — he trades tankiness for damage.
K'Sante.ability_to_dodge: K'Sante Q3 — his dash knockup is his main engage; sidestep it and he has no follow-up.
K'Sante.early_game: You win early. His damage is low and your E poke plus Q knockup zone him. Dodge his Q3 and punish with a full combo.
K'Sante.mid_game: Respect his ult form — he deals much more damage but is squishier. Feast him during ult form when he has less health.
K'Sante.late_game: Both are tanks but your true damage execute is more impactful. His utility increases but your Q-W teamfight CC is comparable.
K'Sante.win_condition: Bully him early and stack Feast. When he ults into his damage form, that's your window to Feast — he's squishier.
K'Sante.scaling: Even — both are strong tanks with CC; K'Sante has more mobility while Cho'Gath has more burst
K'Sante.positioning: Sidestep his Q3 and immediately punish. When he ults and becomes squishy, that's your Feast window.

Sion.trading_pattern: Interrupt his Q charge with your own Q knockup. He's a tank you can freely poke with E and combo with Q-W. Your Feast outscales his health stacking.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q — cancel his Decimating Smash charge with your Q knockup before it completes.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R engage), Sunfire Cape + Titanic Hydra

Sion.early_game: Free lane. Your Q interrupts his Q charge every time. Poke with E and combo freely. He can't threaten you at all.
Sion.mid_game: Continue dominating. Stack Feast and outpace his health stacking. You win the tank vs tank matchup because of true damage execute.
Sion.late_game: Battle of the scaling tanks. Your Feast true damage wins the health war. In teamfights, both have good CC but your execute is more impactful for securing kills.
Sion.win_condition: Stack Feast and deny his farm. You both scale but your true damage execute gives you the edge in the tank mirror.
Sion.scaling: Cho'Gath slightly favored — true damage Feast execute outscales Sion's raw health stacking
Sion.positioning: Stay near him to interrupt Q charges. Don't let him fully channel from fog of war. Sidestep his R charge path.

Dr. Mundo.trading_pattern: Dodge his Q cleavers and poke with E. Don't try extended trades — he out-regens you. Burst him with Q-W-R combo when he's at Feast execute range.
Dr. Mundo.ability_to_dodge: Dr. Mundo Q — his cleaver slow; sidestep it and he loses his poke and engage setup.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast true damage ignores his regen), Hextech Rocketbelt

Dr. Mundo.early_game: Even lane. Dodge his Q and poke with E. His passive regen is annoying. Farm up and wait for level 6 where your Feast execute cuts through his healing.
Dr. Mundo.mid_game: Your Feast true damage is one of the best tools against his regen. Wait for him to ult and burn through HP, then Feast when he drops to execute range.
Dr. Mundo.late_game: He becomes extremely tanky. Your Feast is still relevant because it's true damage. In teamfights, focus his carries — Mundo just soaks damage.
Dr. Mundo.win_condition: Feast's true damage is the key. Wait for execute range and ignore his healing. Don't try to poke him down — he regens it all.
Dr. Mundo.scaling: Even — both are unkillable tanks; Cho'Gath's true damage execute is the deciding factor
Dr. Mundo.positioning: Sidestep his Q cleavers. Farm passively and wait for Feast execute range. Don't chase him through his R regen.

Urgot.trading_pattern: Dodge his E flip at all costs. If he flips you, you eat all his shotgun knees. Poke with Q from max range and E from safety. Don't walk into flip range.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot E — Disdain flip sets up his full damage rotation; sidestep it and punish while it's on a long cooldown.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast execute contests his R execute), Hextech Rocketbelt
power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (all shotgun knees active), Black Cleaver

Urgot.early_game: Respect his E flip. If it lands, you eat massive damage from his passive shotgun knees. Farm from range with Q and E. Don't walk into his engage range.
Urgot.mid_game: He's a strong brawler. Your Feast execute and his Fear Beyond Death compete — make sure you Feast first before he chains you with R.
Urgot.late_game: He shreds tanks with percent health damage. Your Feast stacks make you tankier but his passive scales against you. Focus on teamfight CC rather than dueling him.
Urgot.win_condition: Dodge his E and poke from range. Your Q-W-R burst can kill him if you avoid his flip. Don't sit below 25% HP or his R chains you.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot slightly favored — his percent health damage scales against Cho'Gath's health stacking
Urgot.positioning: Stay outside his E range and poke with Q. If he flips you, Q-W immediately and disengage — don't try to fight while surrounded by his shotgun knees.

Ambessa.trading_pattern: She's mobile with passive dashes. Land Q when she commits to a trade — she has to walk up to auto-attack. W silence stops her dash chains.
Ambessa.ability_to_dodge: Ambessa R — her ult dash engage; flash or Q under yourself to knock her up when she arrives.
Ambessa.early_game: She's aggressive with short dashes. Farm safely and punish when she overextends. Your Q-W combo catches her mid-dash.
Ambessa.mid_game: You start matching her. Your tankiness and silence neutralize her dash-heavy playstyle. Feast when she overcommits.
Ambessa.late_game: Both are decent teamfighters. Your CC and tankiness are more consistent while she's more mobile. Save W for her dive attempts.
Ambessa.win_condition: Silence her when she dashes in. Without her passive dashes she can't trade effectively. Feast her when she overextends.
Ambessa.scaling: Even — both have strong teamfight presence with different strengths
Ambessa.positioning: Let her come to you. When she dashes in, Q her landing spot and W silence to cancel her follow-up dashes.

Volibear.trading_pattern: He runs you down early. Q under yourself when he charges at you with his Q stun. Silence him with W to deny his E shield. Farm from distance.
Volibear.ability_to_dodge: Volibear Q — Thundering Smash stun; Q under yourself when he runs at you to knock him up before he reaches you.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R tower disable + HP), Nashor's Tooth

Volibear.early_game: He's stronger in melee. Q under yourself when he charges and W silence to deny his combo. Don't let him auto-stack his passive. Farm from range with E.
Volibear.mid_game: He can tower dive you with R. Be careful when low HP under tower — his ult disables it. Back off or have your jungler nearby.
Volibear.late_game: You outscale. He falls off while your Feast stacks and teamfight CC become more valuable. Group and play front-to-back.
Volibear.win_condition: Survive his early aggression and outscale. Your Q-W combo disrupts his engage pattern. Don't let him freely tower dive you.
Volibear.scaling: Cho'Gath favored — Volibear falls off while Cho'Gath scales infinitely with Feast stacks
Volibear.positioning: When he runs at you with Q, place your own Q between you and him. The knockup interrupts his charge before he reaches you.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Your Q AoE clears his ghouls fast. W silence prevents him from commanding Maiden. If trapped in his W cage, auto the nearest wall — it takes 4 hits to break.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick W — Dark Procession cage; auto-attack the wall to break out or use Q-W to kill ghouls while trapped.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Maiden of the Mist), Trinity Force

Yorick.early_game: Even lane. Your Q AoE clears his ghouls efficiently. Don't let him land E on you — ghouls jump to the target. If he traps you in W, auto the walls out.
Yorick.mid_game: Respect Maiden damage. He split pushes hard. Match his push or roam for teamfights where your CC is more valuable.
Yorick.late_game: He takes towers extremely fast. Match his split or force teamfights. Your teamfight is much stronger but he'll take your base if left alone.
Yorick.win_condition: Clear his ghouls with Q AoE and deny his E landing. Don't fight him surrounded by Maiden and ghouls. Force teamfights where he's weaker.
Yorick.scaling: Yorick slightly favored — his split push with Maiden is nearly impossible to match
Yorick.positioning: Kill ghouls with Q before fighting Yorick directly. If he lands E, ghouls jump to you — dodge E at all costs.

Jayce.trading_pattern: Take Doran's Shield and Second Wind. Farm under tower with E and Q. He pokes you down from range but can't kill you if you don't walk into his Q+E combo.
Jayce.ability_to_dodge: Jayce Q+E — Shock Blast through Acceleration Gate deals massive poke; stand behind minions to block it.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast burst on his squishy build), Hextech Rocketbelt

Jayce.early_game: He pokes you hard. Take Doran's Shield, Second Wind, and farm with E from safety. Don't walk up for CS when his Q+E is ready.
Jayce.mid_game: Once you have items, you can burst him. He's squishy and your Q-W-R combo one-shots him. Look for a Flash-R when he oversteps.
Jayce.late_game: You hard outscale. He's a squishy ranged champion who can't survive your burst. One Feast and he's gone.
Jayce.win_condition: Survive lane and scale. Post-Rocketbelt you can flash-R combo him for a guaranteed kill. He's squishy and you have true damage.
Jayce.scaling: Cho'Gath heavily favored — Jayce falls off while Cho'Gath's true damage execute one-shots him late
Jayce.positioning: Stand behind minions to block his Q+E poke. When he switches to hammer form, that's your window to Q-W-R him.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Poke with E and farm. When he spins in, Q under yourself and W silence — he can't auto-attack effectively while silenced. Back off when his ult is up.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere R — Undying Rage; when he ults, walk away for 5 seconds. Your Feast does NOT work during his ult immunity.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast execute after his R ends), Hextech Rocketbelt
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R unkillable for 5 sec), Kraken Slayer

Tryndamere.early_game: Trade evenly. His early crits are RNG. Farm safely and use Q-W when he engages. Don't gamble on all-ins when he has fury — his crits hurt.
Tryndamere.mid_game: He splits hard. Match him or force teamfights. Your Q-W silence denies his auto-attack DPS. Save Feast for AFTER his R expires.
Tryndamere.late_game: He outduels you in split push. Group for teamfights where your CC makes him useless. Never try to solo him late.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Disengage when he ults, re-engage after 5 seconds and Feast. In teamfights, your CC completely shuts him down.
Tryndamere.scaling: Tryndamere favored in duels — his ult and crit scaling make split pushing uncontestable; Cho'Gath favored in teamfights
Tryndamere.positioning: When he ults, immediately disengage. Count to 5, then re-engage and Feast. Never Feast into his Undying Rage.

Poppy.trading_pattern: She's short range. Farm with E poke and Q knockup. She can't reach you without dashing, and her W only blocks dashes — which you don't have.
Poppy.ability_to_dodge: Poppy E — she charges at you to slam into a wall; don't stand near walls or she gets a free stun.
Poppy.early_game: You win. She can't engage on you effectively — your Q zones her, your W silences her combo, and her W dash-block is useless against you since you have no dashes.
Poppy.mid_game: Continue dominating. She's a tank but your Feast true damage chunks her. Her anti-dash W is completely wasted in this matchup.
Poppy.late_game: Both are tanks but your execute and damage are higher. Her R can displace you in teamfights but she can't kill you.
Poppy.win_condition: Stay away from walls to deny her E stun. Farm and scale — her W is a dead ability against you.
Poppy.scaling: Cho'Gath favored — Poppy's key anti-mobility tool is worthless against Cho'Gath's immobile kit
Poppy.positioning: Stay away from walls at all times. If she can't stun you with E wall slam, she has very limited trading power.

Kayle.trading_pattern: Bully her pre-6. Q-W-E combo whenever she walks up to CS. She's melee until level 6 and useless in trades. Zone her completely.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle R — her ult makes her invulnerable; wait out the 2-3 seconds of invulnerability then Feast immediately after it ends.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (becomes ranged), Level 11 (waves on auto), Level 16 (permanent range + waves)

Kayle.early_game: Destroy her. She's melee and pathetic early. Q-W-E every time she walks forward. Freeze near your tower and zone her off all CS and XP.
Kayle.mid_game: She's getting stronger. Push her in and roam to impact the map before she reaches level 11. Don't let her free-farm.
Kayle.late_game: She massively outscales everyone. If she reaches level 16, she's a ranged hypercarry. End the game or she wins. Feast her if you can catch her without ult.
Kayle.win_condition: Crush her early, deny XP, and end the game before level 16. Every CS you deny delays her apocalyptic scaling.
Kayle.scaling: Kayle massively favored — she becomes a ranged hypercarry at level 16 that no tank can survive against
Kayle.positioning: Stand between her and the minion wave. Zone her off every CS and XP point early.

Malphite.trading_pattern: His Q poke hurts with Comet. Pop his passive shield with an auto then combo with Q-W-E. Don't waste abilities into his shield — break it first.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite R — Unstoppable Force is fast; flash sideways if you see him winding up. There's a brief animation before the dash.
Malphite.early_game: Even lane. Break his passive shield before committing abilities. His Q poke is annoying but your passive sustain keeps you healthy. Farm and stack.
Malphite.mid_game: Tank vs tank. He can't kill you and you can't easily kill him. Both scale for teamfights. Focus on stacking Feast on minions and objectives.
Malphite.late_game: His R is a better teamfight engage but your sustained damage and execute are more valuable. Both are strong — focus on positioning.
Malphite.win_condition: Farm, stack Feast, and match his teamfight presence. Break his shield before comboing. Neither dies in lane — it's a scaling race.
Malphite.scaling: Even — both are strong teamfight tanks with different strengths; Malphite has better engage, Cho'Gath has better sustained threat
Malphite.positioning: Break his passive shield with an auto before using abilities. Stand behind minions so his Q targeting favors a minion path.

Singed.trading_pattern: Do NOT chase him. Q where he's running toward and W silence him. Your E poke hits from range without chasing. Farm and wait for Feast execute range.
Singed.ability_to_dodge: Singed E — Fling throws you behind him into poison; never walk into melee range. Hit Q and W from max range only.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Feast to punish his proxying), Hextech Rocketbelt

Singed.early_game: Don't chase him. Ever. Stand in lane, farm with E and Q, and let him come to you. If he proxies, freeze under your tower and wait for your jungler.
Singed.mid_game: He roams and proxies. You can't chase him. Focus on stacking Feast and impacting teamfights. Your AoE CC is very strong against his team.
Singed.late_game: His teamfight is annoying but your CC is better. Q his team when they follow his poison trail. Feast whoever he flings into your team.
Singed.win_condition: Don't chase. Farm, scale, and outperform in teamfights. You're a better frontline tank than he is.
Singed.scaling: Cho'Gath slightly favored — more reliable teamfight CC and true damage execute
Singed.positioning: Stand your ground. Q where he's running. Never follow him into poison trail.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Walk into his Q to avoid sweet spots and auto-attack him while his Q is on cooldown. Stack passive during his Q animation — he's locked in place.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — walk inside his model to dodge the sweet spot edge damage where the knockup and bonus damage land.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (World Ender sustain), Spear of Shojin

Aatrox.early_game: You win hard if you can get on top of him. Walk into his Q sweet spots to deny the edge damage, then auto-W-auto to stack passive. E pull him when he tries to kite.
Aatrox.mid_game: He starts matching you with sustain. Build anti-heal early. Don't let him hit you with Q edges from range — close the distance and fight inside.
Aatrox.late_game: He has better teamfights. You need to flank and get on top of carries. If you can't get five-stack resets in teamfights, you're outvalued.
Aatrox.win_condition: Kill him early by closing the gap and stacking passive. Build Bramble Vest to reduce his healing. Use Ghost to stick to him.
Aatrox.scaling: Aatrox slightly favored — better teamfight AoE and sustain make him more valuable late
Aatrox.positioning: Walk INTO his Q, not away. Stay inside his model during his Q combo to deny the sweet spot damage.

Garen.trading_pattern: Pull him with E when he walks up for CS. Auto-W-auto to stack passive. He can't outtrade you if you get five stacks. Don't let him disengage with Q movespeed.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q — his empowered auto gives movespeed; pull him with E before he reaches you to deny the gap close.
Garen.early_game: You destroy Garen. Pull him whenever he walks up, auto-W for the slow, and stack passive. He can't match your sustained damage. Zone him off every CS.
Garen.mid_game: Continue bullying. Push and roam or freeze and deny. He has no way to fight back unless he's significantly ahead.
Garen.late_game: He becomes tanky and his R execute is point-click. Respect his damage threshold and don't duel below 30% HP or his ult finishes you.
Garen.win_condition: Crush him levels 1-5 and build a massive CS lead. Freeze the wave and zone him. He can never trade into you if you manage passive correctly.
Garen.scaling: Even — both fall off in different ways; Garen gets tankier while Darius needs resets
Garen.positioning: Stand between Garen and the minion wave. Pull him with E the instant he walks forward. Keep passive stacking with autos during his Q animation.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: Walk into him and auto-W-auto stack passive. Dodge his E pull by sidestepping. In his Death Realm you still win if you get five stacks.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser E — Death's Grasp pull is slow; sidestep it and engage with your own E pull for a free trade.
Mordekaiser.early_game: You hard-win. Stack passive on him and he melts. His E is easy to dodge and without it he can't kite you. Zone him off CS repeatedly.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: In his Death Realm, you still beat him 1v1 if you stack passive. Pop Ghost if needed to stay on top of him. His stat steal matters less when you have five stacks.
Mordekaiser.late_game: His Death Realm removing you from teamfights can be annoying. Stay near your team so his ult wastes his own positioning.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Kill him early and often. You win the 1v1 at every stage as long as you stack passive. Ghost through his Death Realm if needed.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Even — Mordekaiser's Death Realm becomes more impactful but Darius still wins the 1v1 inside it
Mordekaiser.positioning: Dodge his E pull and immediately pull him with your E. Stay on top of him and auto-W to stack. Never let him kite with passive DoT.

Renekton.trading_pattern: He short trades — you want extended trades. When he E-W-Q combos and tries to E away, pull him back with your E to force an extended fight.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton E2 — his second dash escape; pull with E immediately after his combo to catch him before he dashes out.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (empowered W burst), Blade of the Ruined King

Renekton.early_game: He wants short trades, you want long ones. When he dashes in, let him combo, then E pull him when he tries to disengage. Five stacks = you win.
Renekton.mid_game: You outscale him. He falls off hard while your ult resets are still threatening. Look for splitpush kills.
Renekton.late_game: You massively outscale. He's useless while you can still pentakill in teamfights with resets. Don't let him end early.
Renekton.win_condition: Extend every trade. Pull him back when he tries to dash away. Once you hit five stacks, he's dead.
Renekton.scaling: Darius favored — Renekton falls off a cliff while Darius maintains ult reset potential
Renekton.positioning: Save E pull for when he tries to dash away after his combo. Pull him back into the fight and stack passive.

Fiora.trading_pattern: Hug walls to deny vitals. Short trades with E pull into W slow. Don't let her proc all four ult vitals — back off if she pops R.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W — Riposte blocks everything; don't use W or R predictably. Bait her W with an auto then use W after it's gone.
Fiora.early_game: Tough matchup. She parries your W or R for a free stun. Keep vitals against walls. Short trade with E pull-auto-W, then back off before she procs vitals.
Fiora.mid_game: She outduels you in sidelane. Don't try to match her split push. Group for teamfights where she's less useful.
Fiora.late_game: She hard counters you. Grand Challenge heals her through everything. Never 1v1 her. Force teamfights where your AoE is better.
Fiora.win_condition: Get early kills by baiting her Riposte. If you use W and she blocks it, you lose the trade. Mindgame her W timing.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora heavily favored — she's the premier anti-juggernaut duelist and Darius can't match her late
Fiora.positioning: Hug walls to deny vital spawns. Never R when her W is up — the riposted R stuns you and she gets a free kill.

Camille.trading_pattern: Pull her with E when she hookshots in. She has to commit to the dash — punish with E pull into auto-W stack combo. Respect her passive shield timing.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E — Hookshot stun; pull her with your E after she arrives to cancel her disengage.
Camille.early_game: You win if you pull her when she hookshots in. She can't trade into five stacks. Respect her passive shield — wait for it to fade before committing damage.
Camille.mid_game: She starts matching you with Trinity Force. Her R locks you in place for ganks. Keep vision and don't overextend.
Camille.late_game: She outscales in sidelane. Group for teamfights where your resets are more impactful than her isolated picks.
Camille.win_condition: Punish her hookshot commits with E pull. Five stacks kills her every time. Don't let her short trade and escape.
Camille.scaling: Camille favored — true damage scaling and split push pressure outpace Darius late
Camille.positioning: Stand away from walls to give her less hookshot angles. When she dashes in, pull immediately and stack.

Sett.trading_pattern: Extended trade king vs extended trade king. Stack passive while dodging his Haymaker center. If you dodge the true damage zone, you win the slugfest.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W — Haymaker center is true damage; sidestep left or right, never dodge backwards through it.
Sett.early_game: Classic juggernaut mirror. Both want extended trades. The difference is you MUST dodge his W center. If you eat the true damage, you lose. Sidestep it and you win with five stacks.
Sett.mid_game: You slightly outscale. Your ult execute is more reliable than his W burst. Keep forcing fights and using Ghost to stick.
Sett.late_game: You outscale with ult resets. His W becomes predictable. In teamfights, get resets on low-HP targets while he tries to R your tank into your team.
Sett.win_condition: Dodge Haymaker center every single time. It's the entire matchup. Five stacks with Haymaker dodged = you always win.
Sett.scaling: Darius slightly favored — ult resets provide more teamfight value than Sett's W burst late game
Sett.positioning: Sidestep his W every time. After he wastes it, engage hard and stack passive. The true damage center is the only way he wins.

Jax.trading_pattern: Auto-W immediately when he jumps on you. When he activates Counter Strike, keep auto-attacking — your passive stacks through it. Pull with E when he tries to stun and run.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E — Counter Strike blocks your autos but NOT your passive bleed damage. Keep stacking and pull him when he tries to walk away.
Jax.early_game: You win early. When he jumps on you, auto-W immediately and start stacking. His Counter Strike blocks autos but your W damage and passive bleed still apply.
Jax.mid_game: He starts matching you. Respect his Counter Strike timing — if he stuns you at five stacks and disengages, you waste your window.
Jax.late_game: He massively outscales. Don't try to sidelane against him. Group for teamfights where your AoE and resets matter more.
Jax.win_condition: Get early kills before he scales. Your W slow lets you stick through Counter Strike. Ghost is essential for chasing after his E stun.
Jax.scaling: Jax heavily favored — one of the hardest scaling duelists who eventually stomps Darius
Jax.positioning: Stay on top of him and keep auto-attacking through Counter Strike. Pull with E after his stun to prevent escape.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Pull her with E when she Q-dashes onto you. Auto-W and stack passive. She's squishy and can't survive five stacks. Don't fight near dying minions — she gets Q resets.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia Q — her dash resets on kills; manage your minion wave so she can't build a highway of resets into you.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 4-5 (full passive stacks), Blade of the Ruined King

Irelia.early_game: You win if you manage the wave. Don't let her build passive stacks off minions. Pull her when she dashes in and stack passive. Five stacks = dead Irelia.
Irelia.mid_game: Respect her BOTRK spike. She can outplay you with dashes if your E is down. Hold E for when she commits.
Irelia.late_game: She outscales in extended fights. Group for teamfights where your AoE and resets are stronger than her single-target dive.
Irelia.win_condition: Manage the wave, pull her when she dashes in, and five-stack her. Don't let her build passive off dying minions before engaging.
Irelia.scaling: Even — Irelia outduels but Darius has better teamfight resets
Irelia.positioning: Keep the wave pushing slightly toward you. Stand away from dying minions so she can't dash-chain into you.

Malphite.trading_pattern: His armor scaling hard-counters you. Try to break his passive shield with an auto, then Q him for the bleed. Short trades with Q-auto-W.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite R — Unstoppable Force knockup; flash sideways to avoid being CC'd and killed in a gank setup.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-5 (before he stacks armor), Trinity Force

Malphite.early_game: You need to kill him early before he stacks armor. His Q slows you and prevents you from reaching him. Push hard and try to get plates before he becomes unkillable.
Malphite.mid_game: He stacks armor and you can't kill him. Black Cleaver is essential. Push sidelane and roam — you can't solo kill him anymore.
Malphite.late_game: He hard-counters you late. Full armor build negates your entire kit. Don't waste time trying to kill him — go for his squishier teammates.
Malphite.win_condition: Kill him before he stacks armor, or give up on the matchup and roam. He's your worst type of opponent — a stacking armor tank.
Malphite.scaling: Malphite heavily favored — armor stacking completely neutralizes Darius's physical damage kit
Malphite.positioning: Try to engage on him early before his passive shield refreshes. Pop shield with auto, then Q-W for bleed stacks.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Zone him off every CS. Pull with E when he walks up to Q stack. Auto-W and stack passive. He literally cannot fight you levels 1-8.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W — Wither is a crippling slow; pop Ghost immediately when Withered to maintain kill pressure.
Nasus.early_game: Freeze near your tower and zone him off every single CS. Pull him whenever he walks forward. He cannot fight you at any point pre-6. Deny every stack.
Nasus.mid_game: If you denied enough stacks, he's useless. If he somehow farmed, respect his Wither + Q damage. Ghost through his slow.
Nasus.late_game: He outscales if he stacked. His Wither makes you useless. End the game before he becomes a raid boss. If you're ahead, force objectives.
Nasus.win_condition: Freeze and deny. If he has under 200 stacks at 15 minutes, you've won. Don't let the game go past 30 minutes or he wins regardless.
Nasus.scaling: Nasus massively favored — infinite Q scaling and Wither make him unstoppable past 25 minutes
Nasus.positioning: Freeze the wave near your tower. Stand between Nasus and the minion wave. Pull him the instant he walks forward for a stack.

Gangplank.trading_pattern: He removes your W slow with his W oranges. You need Ghost to stick to him. Pull with E and all-in before he can W cleanse and run.
Gangplank.ability_to_dodge: Gangplank W — Remove Scurvy cleanses your W slow and heals him; bait it before committing to an all-in with Ghost.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 13 (barrel tick speed), Trinity Force + Collector

Gangplank.early_game: You bully him hard. Pull and stack passive. His W removes your slow but it costs mana. Bait W first, then Ghost and all-in — he can't escape without cleanse.
Gangplank.mid_game: Push him in and look for roams. His barrel combos get dangerous. If you can't kill him, impact the map.
Gangplank.late_game: He massively outscales with barrel crits. Force fights before three items or you lose. His barrels one-shot squishies.
Gangplank.win_condition: Bait his W orange cleanse, then Ghost-E-pull and five-stack him. End the game before his crit scaling takes over.
Gangplank.scaling: Gangplank heavily favored — barrel crit combos and global ult give him far more late game value
Gangplank.positioning: Stand on his barrels to deny chains. Auto them at 1 HP to disarm. Zone him off barrel setups.

Gnar.trading_pattern: Mini Gnar kites you. Wait for Mega Gnar to expire and then Ghost onto him in Mini form — he's squishy and can't escape.
Gnar.ability_to_dodge: Mega Gnar R — fight away from walls so his ult knockback doesn't stun you into terrain.
Gnar.early_game: Mini Gnar kites you all day. Take Second Wind and Doran's Shield. Don't waste Ghost — save it for when he's in Mini form and vulnerable.
Gnar.mid_game: Ghost onto Mini Gnar and five-stack him. He's squishy and dies fast. Avoid Mega Gnar near walls at all costs.
Gnar.late_game: Mega Gnar teamfight is insane. In Mini form he's free kills. Track his rage bar and engage when Mega is unavailable.
Gnar.win_condition: Save Ghost for Mini Gnar windows. Five-stack him when he's squishy and Mega is on cooldown.
Gnar.scaling: Gnar favored — Mini form kiting and Mega form teamfight make him a constant threat Darius can't pin down
Gnar.positioning: Track his rage bar. Ghost onto him the moment Mega expires and he's stuck in Mini form.

K'Sante.trading_pattern: Dodge his Q3 dash and pull him with E. He's weak early and can't match your passive stacking. Auto-W-auto and overwhelm him.
K'Sante.ability_to_dodge: K'Sante Q3 — sidestep the dash knockup and immediately E pull him for a free five-stack combo.
K'Sante.early_game: You stomp him. He has no damage and your passive shreds him. Pull and stack freely. Dodge Q3 and he has zero trading tools.
K'Sante.mid_game: Continue bullying. When he ults into damage form, he's squishier — five-stack and execute him even faster.
K'Sante.late_game: He becomes a useful tank but you still win the 1v1. Group for teamfights where your resets compete with his engage utility.
K'Sante.win_condition: Abuse his weak laning and build a massive lead. He's one of the easiest matchups for Darius.
K'Sante.scaling: Even — K'Sante becomes more useful as a tank while Darius needs resets to stay relevant
K'Sante.positioning: Sidestep Q3 dash. Pull him immediately after and stack passive. In his ult form, he's squishier — punish it.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Pull him with E when he dashes through minions near you. Auto-W and stack passive. He's squishy and dies to five stacks every time.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo E — he dashes through minions; don't stand in the minion wave where he can dash through freely.

power_spikes.you: Level 1 (passive chunks him hard), Trinity Force

Yasuo.early_game: Free lane. Pull him whenever he dashes near you. He's squishy and five stacks kill him instantly. Zone him off CS with pull threat.
Yasuo.mid_game: He gets crit items but you still one-combo him. Respect his tornado knockup into R combo though.
Yasuo.late_game: He outscales with full crit. In teamfights, try to pull him when he dashes in and five-stack before he can ult your team.
Yasuo.win_condition: Kill him repeatedly in lane. He can't survive five stacks at any point. Snowball the lead and end before crit scaling takes over.
Yasuo.scaling: Yasuo favored — crit scaling eventually overwhelms Darius, but only if he survives laning
Yasuo.positioning: Pull him when he dashes near you. Don't stand in the minion wave where he can freely dash around you.

Gwen.trading_pattern: Pull her and stack passive. Her W mist zone doesn't block your melee attacks — it only blocks ranged. Stay inside her W and auto-W-auto.
Gwen.ability_to_dodge: Gwen W — Hallowed Mist makes her immune to attacks from outside the zone; step inside the mist and fight her there.
Gwen.early_game: You win hard. Her Hallowed Mist doesn't help against melee. Pull her and five-stack. She's squishy and can't escape your damage.
Gwen.mid_game: She scales with AP and sustain. Build anti-heal and keep fighting her. You still win 1v1 if you get five stacks.
Gwen.late_game: She outscales with AP items and true damage from passive. Don't let the game go too long. Force teamfights before she's full build.
Gwen.win_condition: Kill her early and snowball. Her W is useless against you in melee. Five stacks destroy her every time.
Gwen.scaling: Gwen slightly favored — her AP scaling and true damage eventually outpace Darius in duels
Gwen.positioning: Walk inside her mist zone and auto-attack her. Her W doesn't protect against melee damage — it only blocks ranged.

Ornn.trading_pattern: Pull him and stack passive. He's a tank — extended fights favor you. Watch for his W unstoppable and his Brittle auto proc.
Ornn.ability_to_dodge: Ornn W — Bellows Breath makes him unstoppable during cast; don't try to pull him mid-W. Wait for it to end then E.

power_spikes.you: Level 1 (passive stacking), Trinity Force + Black Cleaver

Ornn.early_game: You win early. Pull and stack passive. He's tanky but your passive damage shreds him. Respect his W unstoppable — time your E pull after it ends.
Ornn.mid_game: He's getting tanky. Black Cleaver is mandatory. You can still kill him but it takes longer. Push and look for better targets.
Ornn.late_game: He outscales through team item upgrades. His R is a massive engage. Force fights before his team gets all upgrades. You can't solo kill him late.
Ornn.win_condition: Build a lead early and push objectives. He outscales through team utility — end the game before item upgrades matter.
Ornn.scaling: Ornn heavily favored — item upgrades for the team make him the best scaling tank
Ornn.positioning: Wait for his W to end before pulling with E. He's unstoppable during Bellows Breath.

Sion.trading_pattern: Interrupt his Q with your E pull. He's a free lane — stack passive and kill him repeatedly. His Q charge is easy to cancel with any of your abilities.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q — pull him with E to cancel his Decimating Smash charge before it completes.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R engage), Sunfire Cape + Titanic Hydra

Sion.early_game: Free lane. Pull his Q charges with E every time. He can never charge Q safely against you. Stack passive and kill.
Sion.mid_game: He proxies and avoids you. Let him push and take farm — you can't always chase him. Impact teamfights instead.
Sion.late_game: He becomes a massive tank. Black Cleaver helps but he's hard to kill. Focus on his squishier teammates in fights.
Sion.win_condition: Kill him repeatedly in lane and build a massive lead. Force objectives while he's dead.
Sion.scaling: Sion slightly favored — infinite health scaling eventually makes him unkillable, but Darius's early lead should close the game
Sion.positioning: Stay in E pull range to cancel his Q at any time. Sidestep his R charge and pull him after he passes.

Riven.trading_pattern: When she Q-combos into you, auto-W immediately for the slow. If she E-dashes away, save E pull for when her shield expires. Don't let her short trade and escape.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven E — Valor dash shield; wait for the shield to expire before committing damage. Pull with E after shield fades.
Riven.early_game: You win extended trades, she wins short ones. When she combos in, auto-W and try to hold E pull for when she tries to escape. Five stacks with Ghost = dead Riven.
Riven.mid_game: She gets CDR and her trades get faster. Respect her burst combos. Hold E pull for when she commits to fighting rather than poking.
Riven.late_game: She outscales with CDR. Her burst combos can kill you before you stack passive. Group for teamfights rather than dueling.
Riven.win_condition: Pull her when she tries to disengage. Force extended trades where your passive shines. Ghost is mandatory to stick through her dashes.
Riven.scaling: Riven slightly favored — CDR scaling lets her burst faster than Darius can stack passive
Riven.positioning: Hold E pull for after her shield expires. Auto-W when she Q3's onto you. Don't chase her through three Q dashes — wait for her to come to you.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Kill his ghouls before fighting him. If trapped in his W cage, auto-attack the wall out. Don't fight surrounded by Maiden and ghouls.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick W — Dark Procession cage; auto-attack the nearest wall to break free. Don't fight inside the cage with ghouls.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Maiden of the Mist), Trinity Force

Yorick.early_game: Kill his ghouls first. If he traps you with W and has four ghouls plus E mark on you, you take massive damage. Fight him only when ghouls are dead.
Yorick.mid_game: He splits hard with Maiden. You can't easily match him once he has Maiden. Look for teamfights instead.
Yorick.late_game: He's one of the best split pushers. He takes towers faster than almost anyone. You need to either match him or force fights elsewhere.
Yorick.win_condition: Kill ghouls with Q AoE, break his cage immediately, and fight him without his minion army. Never fight in his cage with Maiden.
Yorick.scaling: Yorick favored — his split push with Maiden is nearly impossible for Darius to match
Yorick.positioning: Kill ghouls before engaging. Auto his cage walls immediately to break free. Don't fight in his W with Maiden active.

Vayne.trading_pattern: She kites you endlessly. You need Ghost and Flash to reach her. If she condemns you into a wall, you're dead. Close the gap by any means.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E — Condemn knocks you back and stuns against walls; never fight near walls where she can pin you.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult execute if you reach her), Trinity Force + Ghost
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R stealth + tumble), Blade of the Ruined King

Vayne.early_game: She kites you. Take Doran's Shield and farm. Ghost is MANDATORY. Look for an all-in with Ghost when she wastes her E condemn.
Vayne.mid_game: Flash-Ghost onto her for kills. If both are down, you can't touch her. Her percent health true damage shreds you.
Vayne.late_game: She hard-counters you. Percent health true damage means your tankiness is meaningless. You need to Flash-E-Ghost her for any chance.
Vayne.win_condition: Catch her with Ghost-Flash-E pull when her condemn is on cooldown. If you can touch her, she dies. If you can't, you lose.
Vayne.scaling: Vayne massively favored — percent health true damage counters all forms of tankiness
Vayne.positioning: Never fight near walls where she can condemn-stun you. Approach from open areas and Ghost-E pull.

Quinn.trading_pattern: She blinds you and kites. Ghost is mandatory. Wait for her E vault and immediately pull with E — she vaults backwards, so E her landing spot.
Quinn.ability_to_dodge: Quinn E — Vault kicks off you; pull her with E the instant she vaults back to catch her at landing.
Quinn.early_game: She zones you from CS. Take Doran's Shield and farm what you can. Save Ghost for all-in attempts. Pull when she E-vaults and five-stack her.
Quinn.mid_game: She roams with R. Push tower when she leaves lane. Your teamfight is far stronger than hers.
Quinn.late_game: You outscale in teamfights. She's a roaming assassin who falls off. Group and use your presence in fights.
Quinn.win_condition: Survive lane, let her roam, take tower. Your teamfight impact is massively higher than hers.
Quinn.scaling: Darius favored late — Quinn is an early game bully who gets outscaled in teamfights
Quinn.positioning: Save Ghost for all-ins. When she vaults off you, immediately E pull. She lands at a predictable distance after vault.

Dr. Mundo.trading_pattern: Dodge his Q and all-in. He has no CC or escape. Pull him with E and stack passive. He's free kills all lane phase.
Dr. Mundo.ability_to_dodge: Dr. Mundo Q — sidestep his cleaver and engage. Without Q slow he can't kite or trade.
Dr. Mundo.early_game: Free lane. Dodge his Q and pull him. He has no way to escape and your passive shreds him. Zone him off all CS.
Dr. Mundo.mid_game: He gets tanky with R regen. Build anti-heal. Your ult execute ignores his healing since it's true damage.
Dr. Mundo.late_game: He becomes an unkillable meatshield. Don't waste time trying to kill him — go for squishier targets. He offers nothing but being tanky.
Dr. Mundo.win_condition: Kill him repeatedly early and snowball. Even when he gets tanky, your ult true damage chunks him.
Dr. Mundo.scaling: Even — Mundo becomes unkillable but Darius's true damage execute is always relevant
Dr. Mundo.positioning: Sidestep his Q cleaver and walk at him. E pull and stack passive. He has no escape and no CC.

Volibear.trading_pattern: Both want to all-in. When he runs at you with Q, auto-W and start stacking. He's strong early but you outscale if you get five stacks.
Volibear.ability_to_dodge: Volibear Q — Thundering Smash stun; auto-W him when he reaches you and start stacking immediately.
Volibear.early_game: Coin flip level 1-3. Both are strong early. If you get five stacks, you win. If he stuns you and combos before you stack, he wins. Ghost helps you stick.
Volibear.mid_game: You outscale. His R tower disable is threatening for dives but you win the 1v1 with five stacks.
Volibear.late_game: You outscale. He falls off while your ult resets keep you relevant. Group and fight.
Volibear.win_condition: Get five stacks in every fight. If you reach five stacks, you always win. Ghost to stick through his disengage.
Volibear.scaling: Darius favored — Volibear falls off while Darius maintains ult reset potential
Volibear.positioning: Stand your ground when he charges. Auto-W the instant he reaches you and start stacking. Don't run — fight.

Urgot.trading_pattern: Dodge his E flip and pull him. He's a juggernaut like you but your passive outscales his. Don't walk circles around him — his shotgun knees proc from all angles.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot E — Disdain flip; sidestep it and E pull him while it's on a long cooldown.
Urgot.early_game: You win if his E misses. Dodge the flip, pull him, and stack passive. Stay on one side of him to minimize shotgun knee procs.
Urgot.mid_game: Respect his R execute. If you're below 25%, his chain pulls you in. Don't duel at low HP — his execute outranges yours.
Urgot.late_game: Both are juggernauts who fall off. Whoever gets ahead in lane usually maintains the advantage. Your teamfight is slightly better with resets.
Urgot.win_condition: Dodge E, pull, and stack. Stay on one side to minimize knee damage. Don't sit at low HP or his R chains you.
Urgot.scaling: Even — both are similar juggernauts who rely on getting ahead in lane
Urgot.positioning: Stay on one side of him so fewer shotgun knees proc. Dodge his E and immediately pull.

Jayce.trading_pattern: He pokes you from range. Take Doran's Shield and farm. Save Ghost for when he switches to hammer form — if he's in melee, pull and five-stack.
Jayce.ability_to_dodge: Jayce Q+E — Shock Blast through Acceleration Gate; stand behind minions to block the combo.
Jayce.early_game: He pokes you endlessly. Take Second Wind and Doran's Shield. Farm safely and wait for Ghost all-in window. If he switches to hammer, pull and five-stack.
Jayce.mid_game: Once you have items, Ghost onto him and five-stack. He's squishy and your ult deletes him. Don't let him kite you.
Jayce.late_game: You outscale in teamfights. He's a squishy ranged champion. One pull and he's dead.
Jayce.win_condition: Survive lane, Ghost-pull him when possible, and outscale. He's squishy late and one pull = death.
Jayce.scaling: Darius favored — Jayce falls off while Darius's ult execute stays relevant
Jayce.positioning: Stand behind minions to block Q+E poke. Save Ghost for when he walks into hammer range.

Singed.trading_pattern: Do NOT chase him. If he runs at you, pull with E and stack passive. Let him come to you. Never follow through poison trail.
Singed.ability_to_dodge: Singed E — Fling throws you behind him into poison; pull him before he reaches fling range.

power_spikes.you: Level 1 (passive if you reach him), Trinity Force

Singed.early_game: If he proxies, freeze under tower and wait for your jungler. If he lanes normally, pull him and stack passive. NEVER chase him through poison.
Singed.mid_game: He roams and proxies. Push tower when he's behind it. Don't waste time chasing — he wants you to follow.
Singed.late_game: He's annoying but not dangerous 1v1. Your teamfight is better. Group and fight his team while he runs around.
Singed.win_condition: Don't chase. Let him come to you or freeze the wave. If he proxies, call your jungler. Your 1v1 is better if you fight in place.
Singed.scaling: Even — both are annoying to deal with in different ways; Singed proxies while Darius teamfights
Singed.positioning: Stand still and let him come to you. Pull when he runs at you. NEVER chase through poison.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Pull him and stack passive. His early crits are RNG. When he ults, stop attacking and walk away for 5 seconds, then re-engage and execute.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere R — Undying Rage; disengage for 5 seconds when he ults. Your R won't kill through his invulnerability.
Tryndamere.early_game: You win early. Pull and stack. His RNG crits can surprise you so don't gamble at low HP. Keep health above 50% to avoid crit deaths.
Tryndamere.mid_game: Respect his ult. Don't use R into his Undying Rage. Disengage for 5 seconds and re-engage after it expires.
Tryndamere.late_game: He outduels you in sidelane with ult and crit scaling. Group for teamfights where he's much weaker than you.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Get early kills, don't waste R into his ult, and force teamfights where his split-push style is weaker.
Tryndamere.scaling: Tryndamere favored — his ult and crit scaling make him an unstoppable split pusher late
Tryndamere.positioning: When he pops R, immediately walk away. Count to 5, then re-engage with E pull and execute with R.

Illaoi.trading_pattern: Dodge her E soul grab. If she pulls your spirit, fight her immediately to reduce the damage. If she misses E, all-in her — she has nothing without it.
Illaoi.ability_to_dodge: Illaoi E — Test of Spirit pulls your soul; dodge it by standing behind minions. If she misses E, she's helpless.
Illaoi.early_game: Dodge her E. If she misses, pull and five-stack her. If she lands E and ults, LEAVE immediately. Do not fight in her ult with tentacles everywhere.
Illaoi.mid_game: She's strong in her tentacle zone. Pull her out of position and fight where there are no tentacles set up.
Illaoi.late_game: Her teamfight is strong if she lands E and ults. Dodge her E and she's useless. Focus on positioning to avoid spirit pull.
Illaoi.win_condition: Dodge E, then all-in. If she lands E and ults, leave immediately. Come back when tentacles despawn.
Illaoi.scaling: Even — both are strong lane bullies who fall off in different ways
Illaoi.positioning: Stand behind minions to block her E spirit grab. When she misses E, immediately engage and five-stack.

Kayle.trading_pattern: Zone her off all CS. Pull her the instant she walks forward. She's melee pre-6 and useless. Kill her repeatedly.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle R — her ult makes her invulnerable; wait it out then immediately pull and execute.
Kayle.early_game: Crush her. She's melee and useless. Freeze near your tower and zone her off every CS and XP. Kill her on repeat.
Kayle.mid_game: She's scaling. Don't let her free farm. Keep pushing objectives and forcing fights before she hits 11.
Kayle.late_game: She hard-outscales everyone. If she reaches level 16, it's over. End the game before she transforms.
Kayle.win_condition: Deny her every CS and XP point early. End the game before level 16 or she wins regardless of your lead.
Kayle.scaling: Kayle massively favored — she becomes a ranged hypercarry at level 16 that destroys everything
Kayle.positioning: Stand between Kayle and the minion wave. Pull her the instant she walks forward. Freeze and zone.

Yone.trading_pattern: Force extended trades by walking up and auto-attacking to stack Hemorrhage (passive) as quickly as possible — Yone cannot out-sustain 5-stack bleed damage. Use E (Apprehend) to interrupt his Q3 dash or pull him back when he tries to disengage after using Soul Unbound (E).
Yone.ability_to_dodge: Yone's E (Soul Unbound) — when he dashes away, his body snaps back to him after a short duration; time your own E (Apprehend) to pull him during or right after the return so he cannot safely retreat and gets caught in melee range.

power_spikes.you: Level 5 (full Hemorrhage stacks hit hardest pre-6) and Stridebreaker (first item — gap-close counters Soul Unbound escapes)
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Fate Sealed R for long-range engage/disengage) and Kraken Slayer (hurts through your early tankiness)

Yone.early_game: At levels 1–3, walk up and auto-attack aggressively to stack Hemorrhage — Yone has no reliable sustain or pre-Q3 escape to dodge your W slow and E pull. At level 6, look to all-in the moment he uses Soul Unbound, using E to cancel his retreat and R to execute with full bleed stacks.
Yone.mid_game: After Stridebreaker, you can gap-close on demand, negating Yone's primary evasion tool. Shove the wave, back, and look to proxy or dive — Yone needs multiple items to threaten you, so deny his farm and roam to stack your snowball.
Yone.late_game: In teamfights, target Yone specifically: he is squishy even with scaling items and gets deleted under full Noxian Might. Peel priority away from his R engage angle by positioning between him and your backline; a Ghost + Flash Apprehend onto Yone is a teamfight-winning play.
Yone.win_condition: Stack Hemorrhage in every trade, pull Yone with E when his Soul Unbound is on cooldown, and execute with R before he can escape. Deny his farm lead by shoving and roaming — Yone needs several items to outscale, so keep him behind.
Yone.scaling: Darius wins early and mid convincingly; Yone theoretically outscales in a full 6-item teamfight with CC and mixed damage, but Darius snowballs hard enough that Yone rarely reaches that point if played correctly.
Yone.positioning: Stand between Yone and the wave so your melee auto-attacks whittle him while he last-hits; this forces him to either take Hemorrhage stacks passively or disengage and lose CS. Never stand near walls — Yone's Q3 follows terrain and he can extend his dash distance to escape your pull.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Farm with Q from range and don't walk into his combo. He sustains well too but his Q sweet spots chunk you hard. Dodge his W chain by sidestepping.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox W — Infernal Chains pulls you back if you don't leave the zone; walk sideways out of the rectangle immediately.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (R regen sustain), Spirit Visage + Warmog's
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full Q combo), Spear of Shojin

Aatrox.early_game: He bullies you hard. Farm with Q from range and give up CS if needed. Don't walk into his Q combo. Your passive sustain helps but his damage is too high to trade into.
Aatrox.mid_game: Once you have Spirit Visage, you become very tanky. He struggles to burst through your regen. Start fighting back with Q poke and E trades.
Aatrox.late_game: You outscale as an unkillable tank. His healing gets countered by anti-heal but yours comes from raw health regen which is harder to shut down. Be a meatshield for your team.
Aatrox.win_condition: Survive lane, rush Spirit Visage, and become unkillable. You outscale him as a pure tank even if you're behind in lane.
Aatrox.scaling: Dr. Mundo slightly favored — he becomes unkillable while Aatrox's sustain can be cut by anti-heal
Aatrox.positioning: Farm with Q from max range. Don't walk into his Q sweet spot zones. Sidestep his W chain immediately.

Darius.trading_pattern: Farm with Q and never walk into his E pull range. If pulled, pop passive canister to deny CC chain and walk away. You cannot fight him early — his passive shreds you.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius E — Apprehend pull starts his death combo; stay at 600+ range and farm with Q only.
Darius.early_game: He destroys you in melee. Farm with Q from max range and concede CS rather than die. Don't walk into his E pull range under any circumstances.
Darius.mid_game: You start outscaling once you have two tank items. He can't burst through your regen anymore. Start taking fights with R up.
Darius.late_game: You massively outscale. He gets kited in teamfights while you run through the enemy team with R regen. Be the unkillable frontline.
Darius.win_condition: Survive lane without dying. Once you have Spirit Visage + Warmog's, you outscale him completely. Farm safely and scale.
Darius.scaling: Dr. Mundo heavily favored — Darius falls off hard while Mundo becomes an unkillable raid boss
Darius.positioning: Stay at max Q range. Never walk within his E pull range (535 units). If he uses Ghost, immediately retreat to tower.

Garen.trading_pattern: Farm with Q and poke him to deny his passive regen. He outruns you with Q — don't chase. Wait for R to be up before looking for fights.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q — his empowered auto gives movespeed; throw Q at him when he starts running toward you to slow him down.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (R regen sustain), Spirit Visage + Warmog's

Garen.early_game: He outtrades you in melee. Farm with Q and poke him to prevent his passive from regenerating. Don't engage in melee trades.
Garen.mid_game: You start winning once tanky. His ult execute is dangerous though — respect the damage threshold and R early in fights to stay above execute range.
Garen.late_game: You outscale. He can't kill you through your regen. Be the teamfight frontline while his kit is more single-target focused.
Garen.win_condition: Poke with Q to deny passive regen, farm safely, and outscale with tank items. R before his ult execute range.
Garen.scaling: Dr. Mundo favored — his regen scaling outpaces Garen's damage and tankiness late
Garen.positioning: Hit him with Q periodically to deny his passive regen. Stay at range and farm safely.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: His passive DoT and percent health damage shred you. Dodge his E pull and farm from range. Don't fight inside Death Realm unless you're very ahead.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser E — Death's Grasp pull sets up his combo; sidestep it and poke with Q from safety.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (R regen), Spirit Visage + Force of Nature

Mordekaiser.early_game: He wins trades with sustained magic damage. Dodge his E and farm with Q. Don't let him activate passive — three hits triggers the DoT aura.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: His Death Realm steals your stats including your bonus health from passive. Rush MR items. Fight outside Death Realm whenever possible.
Mordekaiser.late_game: His ult is always a threat. Build QSS if his R is deciding fights. Otherwise, you're tankier in normal circumstances.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Dodge his E, build MR early, and outscale. QSS his ult if needed. Your regen makes you unkillable outside Death Realm.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Even — Mordekaiser's Death Realm counters Mundo's health stacking but Mundo outscales in normal fights
Mordekaiser.positioning: Sidestep his E pull and poke with Q. Don't let him auto you three times to proc passive.

Sett.trading_pattern: Dodge his Haymaker center and poke with Q. His early damage is high but yours scales better. Farm safely and trade with Q poke.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W — Haymaker center deals true damage that ignores your tankiness; always sidestep it.
Sett.early_game: He's stronger in melee early. Farm with Q and E from distance. Dodge his W center — the true damage ignores your tankiness. Short trades with Q poke.
Sett.mid_game: You outscale. His damage falls off while your regen keeps climbing. Start taking fights with R.
Sett.late_game: You massively outscale. He becomes a mediocre frontline while you're unkillable. His R can reposition you but can't kill you.
Sett.win_condition: Dodge Haymaker, farm safely, and outscale. You win every fight after two items.
Sett.scaling: Dr. Mundo heavily favored — Sett falls off while Mundo's regen becomes overwhelming
Sett.positioning: Always sidestep Haymaker center. Farm with Q from range early and scale.

Fiora.trading_pattern: Hug walls to deny vitals. Her percent health true damage shreds your tankiness. Farm with Q and don't let her freely proc vitals.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora Q — Lunge procs vitals; stand against walls to deny vital spawns on your exposed sides.
Fiora.early_game: She bullies with vital procs. Hug walls and farm with Q. Her true damage ignores your tankiness so avoid extended trades.
Fiora.mid_game: She still shreds you with vitals. Don't try to 1v1 her. Group for teamfights where she's weaker.
Fiora.late_game: One of your hardest matchups. Her percent health true damage makes your health stacking meaningless. Avoid her sidelane entirely.
Fiora.win_condition: Farm safely, don't feed her kills, and be more useful in teamfights. She's a bad teamfighter — exploit that.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora heavily favored — percent health true damage hard-counters Mundo's entire identity
Fiora.positioning: Hug walls to deny vital spawns. Farm with Q from max range. Never fight her alone past two items.

Camille.trading_pattern: Her E hookshot is telegraphed — Q her when she dashes in to slow her. Your passive canister blocks CC, which helps against her E stun.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E — Hookshot stun; throw Q to slow her approach and walk away from the stun zone.
Camille.early_game: She trades well with short combos. Farm with Q and concede some CS. Her true damage on Q2 ignores your armor.
Camille.mid_game: You start matching her with tank items. Her R locks you in place but you can tank it with R regen.
Camille.late_game: She outduels you in sidelane. Group for teamfights where your frontline presence is more valuable than her isolated picks.
Camille.win_condition: Farm and outscale in teamfights. Don't try to match her sidelane — she has true damage that counters your tankiness.
Camille.scaling: Even — Camille outduels but Mundo outscales in teamfight tankiness
Camille.positioning: Stay away from walls to give her hookshot less options. Q her when she dashes in to slow.

Jax.trading_pattern: He outfights you in melee. Farm with Q and E from range. When he activates Counter Strike, walk away — don't waste abilities into it.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E — Counter Strike; walk away when he activates it and re-engage after the stun window passes.
Jax.early_game: He wins melee fights. Farm with Q and E from distance. Respect Counter Strike and don't fight into it.
Jax.mid_game: You start matching him once tanky. He can't burst through your regen. Look for teamfights where his dueling is less impactful.
Jax.late_game: He outduels you but you're more useful in teamfights as a meatshield. Group and let your carries kill him.
Jax.win_condition: Farm safely and outvalue him in teamfights. He's a duelist — you're a team tank. Play to your strength.
Jax.scaling: Jax favored in duels — his scaling makes him impossible to 1v1; Mundo favored in teamfights
Jax.positioning: Walk away from Counter Strike. Farm with Q and don't engage in melee unless his E is on cooldown.

Irelia.trading_pattern: She outfights you in melee with stacked passive. Farm with Q and E from range. Don't stand near dying minions — she dashes through them.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia Q — don't stand near dying minions; she builds a dash chain through them into you.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 4-5 (full passive stacking), Blade of the Ruined King

Irelia.early_game: She's dangerous with full passive. Farm with Q from range. If she dashes in with stacked passive, just retreat — you can't trade.
Irelia.mid_game: You match her with tank items. Her BOTRK percent health damage is threatening but your regen compensates. Trade with Q poke and E.
Irelia.late_game: You outscale in teamfights as a frontline tank. She has to dive backlines while you soak damage. Let your team deal with her.
Irelia.win_condition: Farm safely and become unkillable. She's a strong duelist but your teamfight presence outvalues her dive.
Irelia.scaling: Even — Irelia outduels but Mundo outscales as a teamfight tank
Irelia.positioning: Stay away from dying minions. Farm with Q and don't let her build dash chains into you.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Poke him with Q to deny stacking. You win early but he outscales if he stacks freely. Freeze and deny.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W — Wither slow is brutal; pop R for movespeed when Withered to avoid being kited.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-5 (Q poke advantage), Spirit Visage + Warmog's

Nasus.early_game: You poke him well with Q. Deny his stacking by harassing whenever he walks up to CS. You're stronger early — use it.
Nasus.mid_game: He's stacking and getting scary. Keep poking and looking for teamfights where he's less useful. Don't let him free-farm.
Nasus.late_game: He outscales with infinite stacks. His Wither makes you useless. Don't try to 1v1 him late — group and force teamfights.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny his stacks early with Q poke. Push objectives before he becomes a raid boss. End before 30 minutes.
Nasus.scaling: Nasus heavily favored — infinite Q scaling eventually overwhelms Mundo's regen
Nasus.positioning: Poke him with Q whenever he walks up to stack. Freeze the wave and zone him off CS.

Gnar.trading_pattern: Mini Gnar kites you. Farm with Q and wait for Mega to expire. In Mini form you can run him down with R movespeed.
Gnar.ability_to_dodge: Mega Gnar R — fight away from walls so his ult knockback doesn't stun you into terrain.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (R movespeed to catch him), Spirit Visage + Warmog's

Gnar.early_game: Mini Gnar kites you. Take Doran's Shield and farm with Q. Don't chase him. Wait for Mega to expire.
Gnar.mid_game: Pop R and run Mini Gnar down when Mega is unavailable. He's squishy and your Q slow catches him.
Gnar.late_game: Mega Gnar teamfight is great but you're unkillable. Both have strong teamfights. Position away from walls.
Gnar.win_condition: Track his rage bar and engage when Mega is on cooldown. Pop R and Q slow to catch Mini Gnar.
Gnar.scaling: Even — both scale well for teamfights with different strengths
Gnar.positioning: Never fight near walls when his rage is full. Track rage bar and engage during Mini Gnar windows.

Renekton.trading_pattern: He bursts you in short trades. Farm with Q from range. His empowered W stun is devastating. Concede CS rather than die.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W — empowered stun; your passive canister drops when CC'd, pick it up for the heal.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (empowered W combo), Blade of the Ruined King

Renekton.early_game: He destroys you in lane. Farm from range with Q and accept being behind. Don't walk up for melee CS when his fury is high.
Renekton.mid_game: You massively outscale. He falls off a cliff. Start taking fights and being the unkillable frontline.
Renekton.late_game: He's useless while you're an unkillable raid boss. His lane bully advantage is completely irrelevant by now.
Renekton.win_condition: Survive lane without feeding. You outscale him so hard it doesn't matter if you're behind in CS.
Renekton.scaling: Dr. Mundo massively favored — Renekton falls off a cliff while Mundo scales infinitely
Renekton.positioning: Stay at max Q range. Don't walk into his E dash + W stun range when fury is above 50.

Ornn.trading_pattern: Tank vs tank. Poke with Q and farm. His Brittle proc hurts but you sustain through it. Long lane, neither dies easily.
Ornn.ability_to_dodge: Ornn W — Bellows Breath applies Brittle; dodge sideways out of the cone to avoid the amplified auto proc.
Ornn.early_game: Even lane. Both are tanks who farm. Poke with Q and dodge his W Brittle proc. Neither kills the other without jungler help.
Ornn.mid_game: Farm lane. Both scale for teamfights. Your regen is better but his team gets item upgrades.
Ornn.late_game: He outscales through item upgrades for the team. Your individual tankiness is better but his team-wide value is higher.
Ornn.win_condition: Farm and scale. Push objectives before his team gets all upgraded items. You're individually tankier but he provides more team value.
Ornn.scaling: Ornn favored — item upgrades for the whole team outvalue Mundo's individual tankiness
Ornn.positioning: Dodge his W Brittle cone sideways. Poke with Q and farm for teamfights.

K'Sante.trading_pattern: You win early with Q poke. He's weak in lane. Farm freely and poke him down. Respect his ult form — he deals more damage but is squishier.
K'Sante.ability_to_dodge: K'Sante Q3 — sidestep his dash knockup. Without it he can't trade effectively.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-5 (Q poke dominance), Spirit Visage + Warmog's

K'Sante.early_game: You win. Poke with Q and zone him. He has no damage to threaten you. Free farm and build your items.
K'Sante.mid_game: You outscale as a tank. His ult form is dangerous but brief. Wait out his ult then fight him in normal form.
K'Sante.late_game: Both are useful tanks. Your regen makes you individually tankier while his utility and engage are better.
K'Sante.win_condition: Bully him early and build a gold lead. You're a better tank 1v1 while he's a better team utility tank.
K'Sante.scaling: Even — both are strong tanks with different strengths
K'Sante.positioning: Sidestep his Q3 and poke with your own Q. Free farm early.

Sion.trading_pattern: Tank vs tank. Neither kills the other. Farm with Q and E. Dodge his Q charge. Both scale for teamfights.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q — sidestep his Decimating Smash charge. It's slow and telegraphed.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R engage), Sunfire Cape + Titanic Hydra

Sion.early_game: Free farm lane. Neither threatens the other. Dodge his Q charge and poke with Q. Both scale.
Sion.mid_game: Both are tanks. You're harder to kill while he has better engage. Push objectives together.
Sion.late_game: Both scale infinitely. Your regen makes you unkillable while his health stacking makes him a wall. In teamfights, position away from his R charge.
Sion.win_condition: Farm and scale. Both are tanks who want to teamfight. You're individually harder to kill.
Sion.scaling: Even — both scale infinitely with different strengths; Mundo regens more, Sion has more CC
Sion.positioning: Dodge his Q charge sideways. Sidestep his R charge. Farm and scale.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Kill his ghouls with E AoE. If trapped in his W cage, auto the wall out. Don't fight surrounded by Maiden and ghouls.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick W — Dark Procession cage; auto the walls to break free immediately.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Maiden of the Mist), Trinity Force

Yorick.early_game: He's strong with ghouls and Maiden. Kill ghouls with E AoE and don't fight in his cage. Farm and scale.
Yorick.mid_game: He splits hard. You can match him if you're tanky enough but he takes towers faster. Group when possible.
Yorick.late_game: He's one of the best split pushers. Match him in sidelane or group for teamfights where you're more useful.
Yorick.win_condition: Kill ghouls, escape his cage, and fight him without his minion army. Group for teamfights when possible.
Yorick.scaling: Yorick slightly favored — his split push pressure is nearly impossible to match
Yorick.positioning: Kill ghouls first. Auto his cage walls immediately. Don't fight in his W with Maiden.

Volibear.trading_pattern: He runs you down early. Q slow him when he charges and walk away. Don't fight him levels 1-5 in melee. Farm from range.
Volibear.ability_to_dodge: Volibear Q — Thundering Smash; Q slow him and walk away before he reaches you.
Volibear.early_game: He's stronger early. Q slow him when he charges and retreat. Don't fight in melee. Farm from range and survive.
Volibear.mid_game: You outscale. His R tower dive is scary but you can tank it with R regen. Start fighting back once tanky.
Volibear.late_game: You massively outscale. He falls off while your regen keeps climbing. Group and be the unkillable frontline.
Volibear.win_condition: Survive early, outscale mid-late. Your regen makes you unkillable while he becomes a mediocre tank.
Volibear.scaling: Dr. Mundo heavily favored — Volibear falls off while Mundo becomes unkillable
Volibear.positioning: Q slow him when he charges and walk away. Don't fight in melee early.

Gangplank.trading_pattern: His Q poke hurts and Grasp procs chunk. Farm with your Q and sustain with passive. Don't chase him — his W cleanses your Q slow.
Gangplank.ability_to_dodge: Gangplank Q — his Parrrley poke is point-click; you can't dodge it. Take Doran's Shield and sustain through it.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 13 (barrel tick speed), Trinity Force + Collector

Gangplank.early_game: His Q poke is annoying. Take Doran's Shield and sustain with passive. Farm with Q from range and don't chase him.
Gangplank.mid_game: You outscale him in durability. His barrels get dangerous but you can tank the damage. Start grouping.
Gangplank.late_game: He massively outscales with barrel crits. End before three items or his barrels one-shot your carries.
Gangplank.win_condition: Outsustain his poke, farm, and force teamfights before his crit scaling takes over. End early.
Gangplank.scaling: Gangplank heavily favored — barrel crit combos and global ult give him far more late game value
Gangplank.positioning: Stand on his barrels to deny chains. Auto them at 1 HP. Farm with Q and sustain with passive.

Malphite.trading_pattern: His Q poke is AP damage — you sustain it well with passive. Poke him with Q and farm freely. He can't kill you and you can't easily kill him.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite R — Unstoppable Force knockup; flash sideways when you see the animation. Your passive canister drops but you can pick it up.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-5 (Q poke advantage), Spirit Visage + Warmog's

Malphite.early_game: You win this lane. His armor stacking doesn't matter because your Q and E deal magic damage. Poke him with Q and farm freely.
Malphite.mid_game: Free farm lane. Neither kills the other but you sustain better. Push and roam for teamfight impact.
Malphite.late_game: His R engage is a better teamfight tool but you're individually unkillable. Both are strong — position to avoid his R.
Malphite.win_condition: Farm freely and outscale. His armor stacking is irrelevant against your magic damage kit.
Malphite.scaling: Even — Malphite has better engage, Mundo has better sustain
Malphite.positioning: Farm freely. Break his passive shield then poke with Q. Position away from teammates so his R doesn't hit multiple.

Kayle.trading_pattern: Bully her pre-6 with Q poke and E trades. She's melee and weak. Zone her off CS and XP. Use your early advantage to build a lead.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle R — invulnerability for 2-3 seconds; walk away and wait for it to end, then re-engage.
Kayle.early_game: Bully her. She's melee and pathetic. Q poke and E trade whenever she walks forward. Deny all CS.
Kayle.mid_game: Push objectives before she scales. She's getting stronger but you're tankier. Force teamfights.
Kayle.late_game: She hard-outscales. Level 16 Kayle destroys everything. End the game or she wins.
Kayle.win_condition: Crush her early and end before level 16. Every CS you deny delays her power spike.
Kayle.scaling: Kayle massively favored — she becomes a ranged hypercarry that melts even tanks
Kayle.positioning: Zone her off minions. Q poke and E trade every time she walks forward.

Riven.trading_pattern: She combos you hard in short trades. Farm with Q and E from range. Your sustain outlasts her burst. Take Doran's Shield and survive.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven Q3 — the third Q knockup is her engage; walk backwards when you see her start the Q chain.
Riven.early_game: She outbursts you in short trades. Farm with Q and sustain with passive. Take Doran's Shield. Don't fight in melee.
Riven.mid_game: You outscale with tank items. Her burst can't get through your regen. Start taking fights with R up.
Riven.late_game: You're unkillable. She can't burst through your regen no matter how much CDR she has. Be the teamfight frontline.
Riven.win_condition: Survive her burst combos, farm, and outscale. Your regen makes her burst irrelevant after two items.
Riven.scaling: Dr. Mundo favored — Riven's burst can't keep up with Mundo's regeneration scaling
Riven.positioning: Farm with Q from range. Walk backwards when she starts Q combo. Sustain through her damage with passive.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: His crits chunk you and his ult makes him unkillable. Farm with Q and don't gamble on all-ins. When he ults, walk away for 5 seconds.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere R — Undying Rage; walk away for 5 seconds and re-engage after it expires.
Tryndamere.early_game: His crits hurt and your tankiness doesn't matter when he crits. Farm with Q and don't fight in melee early.
Tryndamere.mid_game: He splits hard. Match him if tanky enough or group for teamfights. His ult makes 1v1s unreliable.
Tryndamere.late_game: He outduels you with crit scaling and ult. Don't try to match his sidelane. Group and be the teamfight tank.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Farm safely and group for teamfights. He's a split push monster — don't try to match him alone.
Tryndamere.scaling: Tryndamere favored — his ult and crit scaling make him impossible to duel
Tryndamere.positioning: When he ults, walk away for 5 seconds. Don't waste abilities trying to kill through Undying Rage.

Urgot.trading_pattern: Dodge his E flip. His shotgun knees proc percent health damage which shreds you. Farm with Q and don't let him circle around you.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot E — Disdain flip; sidestep it and poke with Q while it's on cooldown.
Urgot.early_game: Dodge his E flip. His shotgun knees deal percent health damage that shreds your tankiness. Farm from range with Q.
Urgot.mid_game: He's a strong anti-tank. Your regen helps but his percent damage is consistent. Don't extend fights with him alone.
Urgot.late_game: He shreds tanks with percent health. You're more useful in teamfights as a frontline but avoid extended 1v1s with him.
Urgot.win_condition: Dodge E, farm safely, and outvalue in teamfights. His percent health damage is a problem but your team value is higher.
Urgot.scaling: Even — Urgot's percent health counters Mundo's stacking but Mundo provides better team tankiness
Urgot.positioning: Dodge his E flip. Stay on one side of him to minimize knee procs. Farm with Q.

Singed.trading_pattern: Do NOT chase him. Farm with Q and let him come to you. If he flings you, you take poison damage — avoid melee range.
Singed.ability_to_dodge: Singed E — Fling throws you into poison; never walk into melee range. Poke with Q from max distance.
Singed.early_game: Don't chase him. Farm with Q and let him come to you. If he proxies, freeze under tower and wait for your jungler.
Singed.mid_game: He roams and proxies. Push tower when he's gone. Your teamfight presence is better.
Singed.late_game: Both are annoying tanks. You're unkillable and he's unchaseable. Group for teamfights where your frontline is more useful.
Singed.win_condition: Don't chase. Farm with Q. Group for teamfights. You're a better teamfight tank than he is.
Singed.scaling: Even — both are annoying unkillable tanks with different strengths
Singed.positioning: Never chase through poison. Stand still and Q him. Let him come to you.

Ambessa.trading_pattern: She has lots of short dashes. Farm with Q and E from range. Don't let her weave autos and dashes around you. Your sustain outlasts her burst.
Ambessa.ability_to_dodge: Ambessa R — ult dash engage; Q slow her and walk away to avoid the follow-up combo.
Ambessa.early_game: She's aggressive with dashes. Farm safely with Q and E from distance. Your passive sustain keeps you healthy. Don't fight in melee early.
Ambessa.mid_game: You start outscaling with tank items. Her burst can't keep up with your regen. Take fights with R up.
Ambessa.late_game: You outscale as a teamfight tank. She's a diver who needs to commit — your tankiness soaks her damage for your team.
