Ambessa.win_condition: Survive early, outscale mid-late. Your regen makes her burst irrelevant after two items.
Ambessa.scaling: Dr. Mundo favored — Ambessa's burst can't keep up with Mundo's regen scaling
Ambessa.positioning: Stay at range and Q poke. Don't let her weave dashes around you in melee.

Vel'Koz.trading_pattern: Vel'Koz outranges you significantly. Respect his poke and farm safely with abilities.
Vel'Koz.ability_to_dodge: Vel'Koz E (Tractor Beam) — dodge sideways or retreat immediately.
Vel'Koz.early_game: Play defensive and farm with W poke. His range is overwhelming.
Vel'Koz.mid_game: Use ult to engage when he overextends. He has low mobility.
Vel'Koz.late_game: He outscales. Win through teamfight engagement.
Vel'Koz.win_condition: Engage with ult to negate his range. Win teamfights through coordination.
Vel'Koz.scaling: Vel'Koz outscales — his sustained poke damage is overwhelming
Vel'Koz.positioning: Use W to farm from range. Never trade close range.

Hwei.trading_pattern: Hwei has versatile poke. Play defensively and farm with W.
Hwei.ability_to_dodge: Hwei E (variants) — dodge depends on form, vary your positioning.
Hwei.early_game: Play defensively. Hwei's versatility makes him difficult.
Hwei.mid_game: Use ult to engage when he overextends. Group with team.
Hwei.late_game: He outscales. Win through teamfight coordination.
Hwei.win_condition: Engage with ult during teamfights. Win through coordination.
Hwei.scaling: Hwei outscales — his utility increases with items
Hwei.positioning: Use W to farm from range. Keep distance from his poke zones.

Brand.trading_pattern: Brand has stronger early poke. Farm with abilities and respect his all-in threat.
Brand.ability_to_dodge: Brand Q (Sear) — dodge by stepping behind minions or moving back.
Brand.early_game: Play defensively. Brand wins early poke trades.
Brand.mid_game: Use ult to engage when he's out of position. Group with team.
Brand.late_game: Brand scales better. Win through teamfight engagement.
Brand.win_condition: Engage with ult to negate his poke. Win teamfights through coordination.
Brand.scaling: Brand outscales — his sustained burn and teamfight damage is superior
Brand.positioning: Keep distance from his flame zone. Use W to farm safely.

Veigar.trading_pattern: Veigar stacks damage from minions. Trade early before he stacks, avoid late game extended trades.
Veigar.ability_to_dodge: Veigar E (Event Horizon) — dodge by stepping out of the box immediately.
Veigar.early_game: Abuse him early before stacks. Poke with W and farm.
Veigar.mid_game: Use ult to engage before he cages. Don't fight in his E box.
Veigar.late_game: He outscales significantly. Win through early-game advantage or teamfight engagement.
Veigar.win_condition: Get an early lead. Use ult to lock him down in teamfights.
Veigar.scaling: Veigar significantly outscales — his stacking makes him stronger
Veigar.positioning: Respect his cage range. Don't walk into obvious trap zones.

Ahri.trading_pattern: Ahri has poke and mobility. Trade when her Q is down, respect her all-in range.
Ahri.ability_to_dodge: Ahri Q (Orb of Deception) — dodge by stepping sideways or back.
Ahri.early_game: Trade when her abilities are on cooldown. Farm safely with W.
Ahri.mid_game: Both roam well. Use ult to engage or protect teammates.
Ahri.late_game: Both scale similarly. Teamfights determine outcome.
Ahri.win_condition: Win through ult engagement in teamfights. Protect team from her engage.
Ahri.scaling: Ahri slightly favored — her mobility and CC provide utility advantage
Ahri.positioning: Use W to farm and poke. Keep distance from her all-in range.

Singed.trading_pattern: Singed is melee tank-focused. Avoid trading close range; use W poke and farm.
Singed.ability_to_dodge: Singed E (Fling) — dodge by staying out of his melee range.
Singed.early_game: Play defensively. Singed wins close trades.
Singed.mid_game: Use ult to engage when he overextends. Don't chase him.
Singed.late_game: He becomes very tanky. Win through teamfight coordination.
Singed.win_condition: Use ult to engage on his mistakes. Win teamfights through coordination.
Singed.scaling: Singed outscales — his tankiness and poison increase in value
Singed.positioning: Keep distance and use W to farm. Never walk into his poison range.

Sett.trading_pattern: Sett is all-in focused early. Trade at range with W, respect his True Damage potential.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett E (Flay) — dodge by stepping back or to the side.
Sett.early_game: Play at range with W poke. Avoid all-in trades.
Sett.mid_game: Use ult to engage when he overextends. Group with team.
Sett.late_game: He becomes tanky. Win through teamfight engagement.
Sett.win_condition: Engage with ult during his overextension. Win teamfights through coordination.
Sett.scaling: Sett slightly favored — his tankiness scales well
Sett.positioning: Keep distance with W poke. Never trade close range.

Tristana.trading_pattern: Tristana is weak in laning. Use W to trade and farm freely. She scales into late.
Tristana.ability_to_dodge: Tristana E (Explosive Charge) — dodge by stepping away from her stack location.
Tristana.early_game: Dominate early with W poke and trading. Farm freely.
Tristana.mid_game: Maintain pressure. Don't let her scale.
Tristana.late_game: She becomes strong. Finish before she gets 2-3 items.
Tristana.win_condition: Get an early lead and snowball. Finish before Tristana scales.
Tristana.scaling: Tristana outscales — her ADC scaling dominates late game
Tristana.positioning: Abuse early game with W. Push her off CS.

Cassiopeia.trading_pattern: Cassiopeia is weak early. Use W to trade and farm freely.
Cassiopeia.ability_to_dodge: Cassiopeia W (Miasma) — walk around it or wait for dissipation.
Cassiopeia.early_game: Dominate with W poke and farming. She outscales so finish early.
Cassiopeia.mid_game: Maintain pressure and roam. Don't let her free-farm.
Cassiopeia.late_game: She becomes very strong. Finish before her teamfight becomes oppressive.
Cassiopeia.win_condition: Get an early lead and roam. Finish before Cassio scales.
Cassiopeia.scaling: Cassiopeia significantly outscales — you need a major lead
Cassiopeia.positioning: Use W to farm and trade. Stay safe from her poison zones.

Azir.trading_pattern: Azir is weak early. Use W to trade and farm freely. He scales so finish early.
Azir.ability_to_dodge: Azir soldiers have limited range early; poke safely with W.
Azir.early_game: Completely dominate with W poke. Azir struggles early.
Azir.mid_game: Maintain pressure. Don't let him free-farm and scale.
Azir.late_game: He becomes strong. Finish before he gets 2-3 items.
Azir.win_condition: Get an early lead and snowball. Finish before Azir scales.
Azir.scaling: Azir outscales — his teamfight control improves late game
Azir.positioning: Abuse early game with W. Push him off farm.

Ryze.trading_pattern: Ryze is weak early. Use W to trade and farm freely. He scales so finish early.
Ryze.ability_to_dodge: Ryze Q (Overload) — dodge by stepping back or to the side.
Ryze.early_game: Dominate with W poke. Ryze needs time to scale.
Ryze.mid_game: Maintain pressure and roam. Deny his scaling.
Ryze.late_game: He becomes very strong. Finish early.
Ryze.win_condition: Get an early lead and snowball. Finish before Ryze scales.
Ryze.scaling: Ryze significantly outscales — you need a major lead
Ryze.positioning: Abuse early with W. Push him off farm.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Irelia benefits from extended trades. Trade only at range with W, retreat when she commits.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E (Flawless Duet) — dodge by moving away from her chain zone.
Irelia.early_game: Play defensively and farm with W. Avoid extended trades.
Irelia.mid_game: Use ult to lock her down when she engages. Group with team.
Irelia.late_game: Both scale. Teamfights favor whoever engages first.
Irelia.win_condition: Use ult defensively to stop her all-in. Win teamfights through coordination.
Irelia.scaling: Irelia slightly favored — her scaling into extended trades is superior
Irelia.positioning: Keep distance with W poke. Never walk into her extended trade range.

Ekko.trading_pattern: Ekko is mobile with decent early poke. Trade when his abilities are down.
Ekko.ability_to_dodge: Ekko W (Parallel Convergence) — dodge the stun zone or get CC'd.
Ekko.early_game: Trade when his cooldowns are down. Farm safely with W.
Ekko.mid_game: Use ult to engage when he overextends. Group with team.
Ekko.late_game: Both scale similarly. Teamfights determine outcome.
Ekko.win_condition: Engage with ult during his mistakes. Win teamfights through coordination.
Ekko.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
Ekko.positioning: Use W to farm and trade. Keep distance from his all-in range.

Diana.trading_pattern: Diana is melee-focused. Trade only with W at range, retreat when she walks forward.
Diana.ability_to_dodge: Diana Q (Crescent Strike) — dodge by stepping sideways or back.
Diana.early_game: Play defensively at range with W. Diana has strong all-in.
Diana.mid_game: Use ult to lock her down when she engages. Group with team.
Diana.late_game: Both scale. Teamfights favor whoever engages first.
Diana.win_condition: Use ult defensively to stop her all-in. Win through teamfight coordination.
Diana.positioning: Keep distance with W poke. Never trade close range.

LeBlanc.trading_pattern: LeBlanc has burst and deception. Play defensively and farm with W.
LeBlanc.ability_to_dodge: LeBlanc E (Ethereal Chains) — dodge by stepping away or to the side.
LeBlanc.early_game: Play defensively. LeBlanc has superior all-in damage.
LeBlanc.mid_game: Use ult to engage when she overextends. Group with team.
LeBlanc.win_condition: Engage with ult when she's vulnerable. Win teamfights through coordination.
LeBlanc.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
LeBlanc.positioning: Keep distance with W poke. Never walk into her all-in range.

Seraphine.trading_pattern: Seraphine has ranged poke and utility. Trade when her abilities are on cooldown.
Seraphine.ability_to_dodge: Seraphine E (Surround Sound) — dodge by stepping away from the zone.
Seraphine.early_game: Play defensively and farm with W. Respect her poke.
Seraphine.mid_game: Use ult to engage when she's out of position. Group with team.
Seraphine.late_game: Both scale well. Teamfights favor whoever engages first.
Seraphine.win_condition: Engage with ult during teamfights. Win through coordination.
Seraphine.scaling: Seraphine slightly favored — her teamfight utility is superior
Seraphine.positioning: Keep distance with W poke. Don't stand in her poke zones.

Orianna.trading_pattern: Orianna has similar trading patterns. Trade when her ball is far, retreat when it's close.
Orianna.ability_to_dodge: Orianna Q (Command: Attack) — this is her main poke, respect it.
Orianna.early_game: Mirror her playstyle. Farm with abilities.
Orianna.mid_game: Use ult to engage when she's out of position. Group with team.
Orianna.late_game: Both scale into teamfights. Positioning determines winner.
Orianna.win_condition: Win through ult engagement in teamfights.
Orianna.scaling: Orianna slightly favored — her teamfight control is marginally better
Orianna.positioning: Use W to farm from range. Keep distance from her ball.

Pantheon.trading_pattern: Pantheon has early dominance. Play defensively and farm with W.
Pantheon.ability_to_dodge: Pantheon Q (Spear) — dodge by stepping behind minions.
Pantheon.early_game: Play extremely defensively. Pantheon dominates early.
Pantheon.mid_game: Use ult to engage when he's on cooldown. Group with team.
Pantheon.win_condition: Survive early, use ult to engage in teamfights.
Pantheon.scaling: Even — Pantheon early, scaling equalizes late
Pantheon.positioning: Keep maximum distance. Farm with W safely.

Cho'Gath.trading_pattern: Cho'Gath is weak early. Use W to trade and farm freely.
Cho'Gath.ability_to_dodge: Cho'Gath Q (Rupture) — dodge by moving perpendicular.
Cho'Gath.early_game: Dominate early with W trades. Farm freely.
Cho'Gath.mid_game: Maintain pressure. Don't let him scale.
Cho'Gath.late_game: He becomes tanky. Finish before he stacks up.
Cho'Gath.win_condition: Get an early lead. Use ult to engage before he gets tanky.
Cho'Gath.scaling: Cho'Gath outscales — his stacking makes him stronger
Cho'Gath.positioning: Use W to farm and trade. Keep distance from his Q.

Darius.trading_pattern: Avoid extended trades at all costs — Darius's bleed prevents your passive regen. Look for short Q-E-W trades only when he has no E available, then back off immediately.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius Q — the outer ring does the most damage; hug him tightly or stay far back to avoid the full hit.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (execute threat), Sunfire Cape first item
power_spikes.enemy: Level 5 (full bleed stacks), Stridebreaker first item

Darius.early_game: Start Doran's Shield and play defensively. Don't let him stack bleed — disengage after each trade. Farm safely and wait for jungle pressure.
Darius.mid_game: Avoid 1v1s unless you're ahead. Shove and rotate; you lose most side lane duels once he has Stridebreaker.
Darius.late_game: Teamfights are your friend — his ult only hits one target but yours executes the lowest HP target. Peel away from him in fights.
Darius.win_condition: Get early jungle help to deny his lane dominance. Rush Steelcaps + Bramble Vest to reduce his bleed sustain. Win via objective play, not dueling.
Darius.scaling: Darius favored throughout — Garen slightly better in late teamfights but loses sidelane all game
Darius.positioning: Don't stand in the outer ring of his Q. Hug the wave so minions chip him when he engages. Never let him proc 5 bleed stacks.

Fiora.trading_pattern: Short trades with Q-E-W only. She will Riposte your Q silence, so delay Q slightly after she uses W. She wins extended trades due to Vitals healing.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W (Riposte) — if she uses it proactively, wait it out before using Q. Never walk into her when W is available.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (max Riposte cooldown reduction), Ravenous Hydra

Fiora.early_game: She can't all-in you easily at level 1-2, so farm and poke with Q. Once she hits level 3, respect her full kit. Don't let her proc 2+ Vitals in one trade.
Fiora.mid_game: You lose most duels after she has Hydra. Shove wave and go to teamfights — she scales better in split push.
Fiora.late_game: Avoid her in side lane entirely. Group with your team; she's strong 1v1 but less threatening in 5v5s unless she can isolate carries.
Fiora.win_condition: Win before she gets two items. Use ignite to cancel her Vital healing. Get your jungler to gank early and often to deny her CS.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora outscales significantly — she wins all duels past 2 items
Fiora.positioning: Keep track of which direction her active Vital is — reposition to deny it. Stand to the side of her, never directly in front.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Walk to the sides of his Q sweetspots to dodge the knockup. Trade during his Q animation windows when he's committed. Your W negates a chunk of his burst.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — sidestep the outer edge zones; standing between the two Q zones avoids most damage.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (Q cooldown reduction), Goredrinker / Eclipse first item

Aatrox.early_game: Farm and short trade. Walk to the safe zone of his Q and punish him when he misses. Avoid full combos — his passive revive at level 1 is tricky.
Aatrox.mid_game: You can match him in side lane if ahead. Respect his ult healing; don't fight him when it's up unless you can burst him before he casts it.
Aatrox.late_game: He outscales in duels but you're better in teamfights. Stick to mid and support objectives.
Aatrox.win_condition: Get an early kill to deny his snowball. Build Bramble Vest second to reduce his healing. Win through objective control rather than dueling.
Aatrox.scaling: Aatrox outscales in side lane; even in teamfights
Aatrox.positioning: Stay between his Q hitboxes — the safe zone is just inside each Q circle edge. Don't stand directly in front of him.

Camille.trading_pattern: She wins all extended trades with her passive shield and E true damage. Only short Q-E-W trades when she has no E available. Disengage before her W heal triggers.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E (Hookshot) — if she misses, punish immediately. Pre-position away from walls so she can't get a clean angle.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full combo available), Trinity Force first item

Camille.early_game: She outtraded you levels 1-5. Farm under tower if needed. Conqueror into her means you must look for extended trades but her kit prevents it.
Camille.mid_game: She outduels you and can use ult to isolate you 1v1. Don't fight near walls. Consider teleporting to other fights.
Camille.late_game: She dives your carries — peel them or die to her ult isolation. She's a bigger threat in side lane all game.
Camille.win_condition: Need jungle assistance early. Play for teleport plays and team fighting where her ult isolation is less impactful.
Camille.scaling: Camille outscales in duels all game; Garen slightly better in pure teamfight chaos
Camille.positioning: Stay away from walls — she needs wall geometry for her E angle. Hug the center of the lane.

Jax.trading_pattern: Abuse the early levels before he has E — trade hard at level 1 and 2. Once he has Counterstrike (E), only trade while E is on cooldown. Never trade into his E.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E (Counterstrike) — don't autoattack him while it's spinning. Trade with Q and E then back off during his E stun.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult passive stacks), Trinity Force + Steelcaps

Jax.early_game: Play extremely aggressive at level 1-2 before he has E. Force him to burn flash or take heavy damage. Back off once he hits level 3.
Jax.mid_game: He starts winning trades after Trinity Force. Match his shove and roam; avoid prolonged 1v1s.
Jax.late_game: He outscales hard. Group and fight in teamfights — his 1v1 is far stronger than yours late game.
Jax.win_condition: Snowball the early levels 1-2 advantage into a kill or major CS lead. Keep him behind before Trinity Force comes online.
Jax.scaling: Jax outscales significantly past first item — Garen only wins early
Jax.positioning: Stand at max Q range to proc it without walking into his E. Don't autoattack when his E is glowing.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Short trades with Q before he gets fury stacked. His crits become devastating — never fight him at full fury. W before his spinning slash hits.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere E (Spinning Slash) — he uses it to gap close and escape. Pre-W when you see him wind up an E-Q trade.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult makes him unkillable), Phantom Drive first item

Tryndamere.early_game: Abuse him pre-6 when he can't make fights 'free'. Poke with Q and deny CS. He can't really all-in you before ult.
Tryndamere.mid_game: He starts winning duels after first crit item. His split push becomes oppressive — you must match him or he takes towers.
Tryndamere.late_game: Never fight him 1v1 in side lane. Group for objectives. His ult makes 1v1 kills impossible during the timer.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Get ahead early and roam to snowball other lanes. You can't reliably kill him at 6+ in side lane — win by objective control.
Tryndamere.scaling: Tryndamere outscales hard — Garen only relevant early
Tryndamere.positioning: Deny his fury generation by not standing near him at low fury. Kite sideways during his E so he can't immediately follow up.

Vayne.trading_pattern: All-in early before she gets items — she can't escape your E. But her Silver Bolts true damage shreds you in extended fights. Short all-ins only.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — never stand near walls or she'll stun you. Stay center of lane at all times.

power_spikes.enemy: Guinsoo's Rageblade first item, Level 6 (ult stealth)

Vayne.early_game: She's squishy and vulnerable early. Charge her with Q and try to all-in at level 2-3. Deny her CS with your threat.
Vayne.mid_game: Her Silver Bolts start melting your HP. She becomes very hard to kill and can kite your E. Respect her dueling power.
Vayne.late_game: She counters you fundamentally — true damage bypasses your W. Avoid 1v1s entirely. In teamfights, try to land E on her early to delete her.
Vayne.win_condition: Kill her early or force her off the wave. If you can't kill her before Guinsoo's, roam and win elsewhere. Ignite to deny her potential lifesteal.
Vayne.scaling: Vayne outscales hard — Garen only wins very early
Vayne.positioning: Keep her away from walls to prevent Condemn stuns. Never chase her along wall edges.

Kayle.trading_pattern: All-in windows: level 2, level 5, level 6 if you hit it first, and level 9. Outside of these windows she kites you permanently. Charge in with Q and force all-ins.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle E (Starfire Spellblade) — her ranged empowered autos hurt a lot; engage when she just used E on a minion.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 11 (fully ranged and untouchable), Nashor's Tooth + Rabadon's

Kayle.early_game: Abuse her hard before level 6. She's melee and squishy at levels 1-5. Take Ignite and try to kill her at level 2 with Q-E combo.
Kayle.mid_game: By level 11 she's fully ranged and you literally cannot reach her. Roam mid or group; staying top is a losing proposition.
Kayle.late_game: She's untouchable with her ult and ranged form. Don't attempt to fight her. Win the game through other lanes.
Kayle.win_condition: Kill her before level 6, ideally twice. If you can't get a kill early, ask for jungle pressure and roam. She wins the game if she's left alone.
Kayle.scaling: Kayle outscales astronomically — Garen only wins levels 1-5
Kayle.positioning: Force her under tower early so she loses CS. Don't let her farm safely — she needs levels more than most champions.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: Never fight inside his W passive zone — it gives him bonus stats. Poke with Q and back off. He wins prolonged trades due to his shield generation.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser R (Realm of Death) — when he ults you, Flash out if possible, otherwise W immediately and fight. Never panic in the realm.
Mordekaiser.early_game: He builds shield fast with his passive. Don't trade into his E slow. Farm and harass with Q but don't commit to extended fights.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: He outduels you once Riftmaker is online. Match his shove and roam. Don't fight him in 1v1 scenarios.
Mordekaiser.late_game: His ult is deadly for isolating carries. Peel your backline. His teamfight isn't as strong as his dueling.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Get ahead early and build Grievous Wounds to cut his Riftmaker healing. Win through objective play, not dueling.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Mordekaiser outscales in duels; even in teamfights
Mordekaiser.positioning: Don't cluster inside his W passive range. Stand outside the golden ring to deny his passive procs.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Only fight her when her passive has 0-2 stacks. At max stacks she destroys you. Keep minions low HP near you to prevent her from resetting Q on them.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E (Flawless Duet) — sidestep the two blades; if they both hit you they stun and she wins the trade completely.

power_spikes.you: Level 2-4 (before she maxes passive stacks), Level 6
power_spikes.enemy: Level 5 (max passive stacks accessible), Trinity Force first item

Irelia.early_game: Freeze the wave away from her tower so she can't stack passive on minions. Fight her at 0 stacks. She can't reliably all-in early if you respect her E.
Irelia.mid_game: She outduels you with Trinity and max passive. Don't fight 1v1. Shove and roam.
Irelia.late_game: Her teamfight is strong. Peel carries and use your E on her if she dives in.
Irelia.win_condition: Deny her passive stacks by keeping wave off her. Poke with Q when she has 0 stacks. Get kills early or it becomes unwinnable.
Irelia.scaling: Irelia outscales in duels past first item
Irelia.positioning: Stand between the two E blades' expected path to sidestep. Keep low HP minions away from her so she can't stack passive.

Renekton.trading_pattern: Never fight at level 3 — he wins decisively with Q-W-E combo and fury. Wait until levels 6+ when your ult threat evens the lane. Use W to absorb his W stun.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W (Ruthless Predator) — when you see him jump with E, immediately W to reduce the stun/damage. Never trade while W is down.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult threat and stats), Sunfire Cape
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full combo), Level 9 (Q max), Black Cleaver

Renekton.early_game: He dominates levels 1-6 hard. Farm and survive. Use W proactively when you see E incoming. Don't fight without jungle backup.
Renekton.mid_game: He falls off mid-game relative to early. Match his shove — you can fight more even footing once you have Sunfire.
Renekton.late_game: Your teamfight is stronger. He's best as a lane bully — his late game isn't impressive. Group and win.
Renekton.win_condition: Survive the early game, get jungle help, and out-scale. Buy Plated Steelcaps ASAP. His power fades after level 10-12.
Renekton.scaling: Renekton favored early, even mid, Garen slightly better late
Renekton.positioning: Don't stand in the center of lane where he can E in from either side. Hug the side closest to your tower for easier retreat.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Harass him constantly with Q every time he goes for CS. He has no early damage without stacks. Don't let him free farm — every denied Q stack matters.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W (Wither) — don't chase him into it; it will slow you to zero and he can kite or turret dive with his ult.

power_spikes.enemy: 300+ Q stacks, Level 6 (ult), Frozen Heart + Hullbreaker

Nasus.early_game: Be aggressive and deny stacks. Every Q he gets on a canon minion is a problem late game. Push and deny CS with E-Q poke.
Nasus.mid_game: Once he has Frozen Heart and stacks he becomes hard to kill. Don't fight him at 6 unless you can burst before ult.
Nasus.late_game: 500+ stacks Nasus can kill you in 2-3 Qs. Avoid side lane duels late game. Force objectives while he's split pushing.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny as many Q stacks as possible early. Win the game before he hits 400 stacks. Push and roam to snowball other lanes.
Nasus.scaling: Nasus outscales massively — Garen wins early, Nasus wins past 20 minutes
Nasus.positioning: Stand between him and the CS he's trying to last-hit with Q. Force him to choose between taking poke or getting stacks.

Sett.trading_pattern: Avoid his W (Haymaker) — if he has full grit, back off immediately. He wins W-empowered trades decisively. Short Q-E trades work well.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W (Haymaker) — the true damage center is extremely deadly; sidestep to avoid the center of the explosion when he throws it.
Sett.early_game: Fairly even. Don't let him auto you too freely — he generates grit from taking damage. Short trades while his W grit is low.
Sett.mid_game: He wins 1v1s once Heartsteel is up. Shove and roam. Match his teleport pressure.
Sett.late_game: His teamfight initiations are powerful. Stay out of his ult grab range. You're both better in brawls but he's stronger.
Sett.win_condition: Get ahead early and deny his grit generation. Rush Bramble Vest to reduce his auto-attack trading power. Win through objective play.
Sett.positioning: Don't stand still while he's auto attacking — move sideways to reduce grit accumulation. Stay away from his W explosion center.

Malphite.trading_pattern: Charge Q and E on him repeatedly. He has no sustain and takes big damage from your E. His armor scaling makes trades less effective late but you win early.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite Q (Seismic Shard) — it applies a movement slow; avoid face-tanking it by staying at max range and sidestepping slightly.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Unstoppable Force), Sunfire Aegis + Randuin's Omen

Malphite.early_game: Abuse him heavily before he gets armor items. Your E ignores armor via its spin damage and you out-trade him at all pre-6 levels.
Malphite.mid_game: His armor makes your physical damage less effective. Build Black Cleaver if ahead. He'll roam for ult combos — push and take plates.
Malphite.late_game: His ult is a game-changer in teamfights — be ready to W when he lands on your team. You win sidelane duels.
Malphite.win_condition: Get a big early lead and deny his gold income. Push your advantage before he completes his armor items.
Malphite.scaling: Garen favored early; Malphite better in teamfights late via ult
Malphite.positioning: Don't let him chunk you with Q at range. Stand outside Q range and all-in when he walks up to CS.

Olaf.trading_pattern: He ignores slows with his ult and can't be CC'd. Your W silence is useless against him. He wins extended trades with lifesteal and axes. Only fight when his axes are on cooldown.
Olaf.ability_to_dodge: Olaf Q (Undertow) — dodge the axe throw. He needs to walk to pick it up, so bait him into throwing it away from him.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult, CC immunity), Stridebreaker first item

Olaf.early_game: His axes hurt early. Don't try to match him in extended trades. Farm safely and call for jungle assistance.
Olaf.mid_game: He's hard to stop once he has Stridebreaker and ult. Shove and rotate — don't duel him.
Olaf.late_game: His ult into your team is devastating. Try to poke him before he activates ult. Your execute is useful if he goes low.
Olaf.win_condition: Get jungle help early. Survive his power window and wait for a gank. Your ult execute threat prevents him from ignoring you at low HP.
Olaf.scaling: Olaf favored throughout; Garen slightly better in pure execute scenarios
Olaf.positioning: Stand to the side of his axe throw trajectory. Force him to retrieve axe in a bad position.

Riven.trading_pattern: She wins short trades with her shield and mobility. Trade only when her shield (E) is on cooldown. Use W silence to interrupt her combo mid-sequence.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven E (Valor/Shield) — track its cooldown. When it's down she's vulnerable. Her entire kit becomes less effective without the shield.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (max CDR on combo), Black Cleaver + Ravenous Hydra

Riven.early_game: Fairly even at level 1. Be aggressive and match her trade at level 2. Don't let her land full 3-Q combo — move between each Q.
Riven.mid_game: She outduels with full build. Match her shove and avoid straight 1v1s.
Riven.late_game: Her execute ult timing can delete your carries. Your W silence disrupts her burst if timed right.
Riven.win_condition: Punish her when E is down. Take Ignite for early kill pressure. Win the game before she completes two damage items.
Riven.scaling: Riven outscales in duels; Garen better in teamfight brawls
Riven.positioning: Move sideways between each of her Q casts to reduce Q3 knockup reliability. Don't stand still.

Shen.trading_pattern: Bait out his W (Spirit's Refuge) before committing to a full trade. He wins trades during his dodge window. Hit Q before he activates W.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: Shen W (Spirit's Refuge) — don't autoattack or use E during the dodge zone. Q him instead since it's not a basic attack.
Shen.early_game: You out-trade him early. Spam Q and E to deny his passive proc. Keep him off the wave to prevent energy regen.
Shen.mid_game: Respect his global ult — push wave then back when it's available so he can't ult and win a fight elsewhere while you're overextended.
Shen.late_game: His teamfight is good. Don't autoattack into his W zone. You can brawl with him but he has better utility.
Shen.win_condition: Build early CS lead and roam. Track his ult cooldown and push objectives during its downtime.
Shen.scaling: Even — both fall into teamfight utility roles late
Shen.positioning: Step out of his W dodge zone when he activates it. Retreat the sword slightly then re-engage after the window expires.

Sion.trading_pattern: Interrupt his Q charge with your Q silence — if he's charging Q, sprint at him and Q to cancel it. Short trades win for you.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q (Decimating Smash) — run toward him perpendicular to his charge direction. Don't run directly away as you'll get hit by the knockup.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult roam), Sunfire + Heartsteel (passive HP stacks)

Sion.early_game: You out-trade him significantly early. He can't really fight you if you interrupt his Q. Shove hard and deny HP stacks.
Sion.mid_game: His roam ult is dangerous — push and create pressure rather than following him blindly.
Sion.late_game: His HP becomes massive late game. Your execute hits harder on high-HP targets. Land ult on Sion for a satisfying delete.
Sion.win_condition: Deny his Heartsteel passive stacks by trading and winning early. Build your own lead and roam with advantage.
Sion.scaling: Even — Garen slightly better early, Sion better late via HP scaling
Sion.positioning: Move toward him at an angle when his Q is charging to reduce travel time and cancel it with Q.

Trundle.trading_pattern: Avoid standing in his W (Frozen Domain) — it gives him massive attack speed and movement speed. Draw him off his pillar before trading.
Trundle.ability_to_dodge: Trundle E (Pillar of Ice) — it blocks paths; don't get cornered against it. He'll use it to slow you and keep you in his W zone.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult steals resistances), Sunfire Aegis + Sterak's

Trundle.early_game: You win early trades significantly. Harass with Q constantly. Don't let him free farm to enable his ult pillar plays.
Trundle.mid_game: His ult stealing your armor and MR is massive. Avoid 1v1s at 6+. Roam to where you're more useful.
Trundle.late_game: His ult on a tanky frontliner is devastating. Keep your distance in teamfights — don't be the one he chains ult on.
Trundle.win_condition: Snowball early lead before his ult and items come online. Get kills and roam — once he's behind, his ult is less impactful.
Trundle.scaling: Trundle outscales in 1v1s; Garen better in 5v5
Trundle.positioning: Never fight while standing in his W zone. Sidestep his pillar immediately when it drops.

Urgot.trading_pattern: He wins almost all trades with his passive shotgun knees empowered autos. Only fight when he's out of passive stacks. His W passive makes him reduce your damage.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot E (Disdain) — if he runs at you with shield up, respect the engage. His E grab is point-click — don't get too close.
Urgot.early_game: He out-trades you at all stages with his passive. Play under tower and farm. His range means you can't freely Q him.
Urgot.mid_game: He wins 1v1s after first item. Match his shove and roam where you're more effective.
Urgot.late_game: His teamfight ult execute is terrifying. In teamfights, watch for his ult on carries. You can sometimes race-condition ult him if he's sub-40% HP.
Urgot.win_condition: Need jungle presence to survive. Take Ignite for kill threat. Win through team play — he dominates side lane.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot outscales in duels; Garen slightly better in group fights
Urgot.positioning: Stay outside his E range (short-medium). Don't stand in front of his knees — angle so you take fewer passive shots.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Kill his ghouls quickly — they deal significant damage if left alive. Short trades work; avoid trading while his Maiden is active as it adds significant DPS.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick E (Mourning Mist) — it marks you for Maiden and Ghoul attacks; sidestep it to avoid the empowered harassment.
Yorick.early_game: Pretty even early. Kill ghouls with E so he can't last-hit with them. Deny him Mist charges to delay Maiden.
Yorick.mid_game: His split push with Maiden is dangerous. You have to match him in side lane or he takes towers. He wins 1v1 with Maiden.
Yorick.late_game: His Maiden and ghouls can siege and backdoor. Match his split or send someone else while you group.
Yorick.win_condition: Deny Mist charges by killing his ghouls and standing away from his E. If you can deny Maiden long enough, your early lead matters.
Yorick.scaling: Yorick outscales via split push; even in direct fights
Yorick.positioning: Don't let ghouls free-hit you. Clear them with E spin. Sidestep his E to deny Maiden empowerment.

Jayce.trading_pattern: He wins all poke trades with hammer-cannon combo. Look to all-in when he's in cannon form — he's weaker up close. Use Q to close the gap on his cannon form.
Jayce.ability_to_dodge: Jayce Q (Shock Blast / Thundering Blow) — in cannon form, dodge his Q arc; in hammer form, dodge the knockback by sidestepping.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 7 (Q max in cannon), Trinity Force first item

Jayce.early_game: He chunks you at range constantly. Farm with Doran's Shield and Fleet Footwork. Don't overextend. Call for jungle help.
Jayce.mid_game: Once he has Trinity he roams and dominates. Push wave fast and match his roams with teleport.
Jayce.late_game: He falls off a bit — his teamfight is primarily poke damage. Your W reduces burst if you engage on him.
Jayce.win_condition: Force all-ins in his hammer form. Take Flash-Ignite and look for a kill when he's melee. Get jungle help to punish his squishy early game.
Jayce.scaling: Jayce favored early-mid; Garen better in brawls
Jayce.positioning: Hug minions to reduce his cannon Q poke angles. Force him to be in melee range to CS.

Gnar.trading_pattern: In mini form, only trade when he's thrown his boomerang. In mega form, W his ult stun and go ham. His mega form is actually disadvantaged vs you.
Gnar.ability_to_dodge: Gnar Q (Boomerang Throw) — sidestep it on the way out; it returns and can hit twice. Don't stand in a straight line from him.

power_spikes.enemy: Mega form rage stacks, Trinity Force + Heartsteel

Gnar.early_game: He pokes you down from range safely in mini form. Farm under tower with Doran's Shield. Don't chase him at range.
Gnar.mid_game: Respect mega form — it's a huge power window but then he's back to mini. Fight during mini form when he's low rage.
Gnar.late_game: His GNAR! ult into walls is devastating in teamfights. Peel from walls and don't cluster near terrain.
Gnar.win_condition: Wait out his mega rage and punish the transition back. Get jungle help to catch him in mini form. Your execute hurts his high-HP mega form.
Gnar.scaling: Gnar outscales in teamfights via GNAR! utility; even in 1v1
Gnar.positioning: Don't stand near walls when he's in mega form — GNAR! wall bounce is how he wins teamfights. Stay in open areas.

Illaoi.trading_pattern: If her E misses, go in immediately — she's weak without it. Never fight while tentacles are up. Short Q trades between E cooldowns.
Illaoi.ability_to_dodge: Illaoi E (Test of Spirit) — this is everything. Dodge it or the fight is over. Sidestep and watch her telegraph the throw.
Illaoi.early_game: You win early before she gets tentacles set up. Harass with Q and deny her E. Don't let her plant tentacles near your half of the wave.
Illaoi.mid_game: Her tentacles make trading into her suicidal. Build Grievous Wounds. Fight only in open areas without tentacles.
Illaoi.late_game: She's a powerful split pusher. Match her or she takes towers. Her ult in a chokepoint is devastating.
Illaoi.win_condition: Dodge her E consistently. Build Bramble Vest to reduce tentacle healing. Win before tentacles are everywhere.
Illaoi.scaling: Illaoi outscales in split push; even in open teamfights
Illaoi.positioning: Never stand directly in front of her during her E wind-up animation. Move laterally.

Varus.trading_pattern: He pokes you down from extreme range and you have no gap close except Q. Wait for him to waste Q then run him down. His W stacks are a problem for extended trades.
Varus.ability_to_dodge: Varus Q (Piercing Arrow) — dodge the charged shot by moving laterally; it's a slow projectile when fully charged.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (execute if he's low), Sunfire Cape
power_spikes.enemy: Kraken Slayer + Guinsoo's (W stacks), Level 6 (ult CC)

Varus.early_game: He pokes freely from range. Doran's Shield and Fleet Footwork. Try to all-in when his Q is charging and he's committed.
Varus.mid_game: His W blight stacks will melt your HP. Once he has Kraken Slayer his true damage hurts through W.
Varus.late_game: His ult chains your whole team. Flank from a side angle to deny his ult line. Your W won't help vs his damage.
Varus.win_condition: You need jungle help to close the gap. He's very kitable — find flanks to get on him. He's squishy early, punish hard with ignite.
Varus.scaling: Varus outscales significantly in ranged kite pattern
Varus.positioning: Hug minions to reduce his poke angles. Approach from diagonal to avoid his Q line.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Your Q silence prevents him from using Wind Wall, E, or R for its duration. Time Q when he's in an unfavorable position. Don't chase him through minions.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo Q (Steel Tempest) — the tornado is telegraphed. Sidestep when he's on third stack with a glow on his sword.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult air combo), Infinity Edge + Kraken Slayer

Yasuo.early_game: Fairly even. He needs crit to really hurt you. Your silence disrupts his combos. Trade when his E is on cooldown.
Yasuo.mid_game: He duels you with crits. Your W is massive here — use it to tank his ult combo. Build Plated Steelcaps.
Yasuo.late_game: His crits hurt but your W negates a chunk. In teamfights your E does great damage. Keep him away from your backline.
Yasuo.win_condition: Deny him early crits to slow his snowball. W his ult combo and then fight back. Silence prevents his E-Q dash combos.
Yasuo.scaling: Even early; Yasuo slightly better with full crit build
Yasuo.positioning: Don't fight near minion waves where he can E between minions to dodge. Force open field fights.

Yone.trading_pattern: Similar to Yasuo. Your Q silence prevents his E soul dash return combo. Punish after he uses E — he's committed during the dash.
Yone.ability_to_dodge: Yone E (Soul Unbound) — when he dashes forward, he'll return to his body. Trade hard and then disengage to reset before he returns.
Yone.early_game: Even matchup early. He has better poke with Q1-Q2-Q3 but your Q silence disrupts him. Short trades favor you.
Yone.mid_game: His damage ramps with items. W into his ult combo then fight back. Silence prevents his ult return.
Yone.late_game: His ult in teamfights is devastating. Use W to survive his burst window then all-in.
Yone.win_condition: Silence his combo at the right time. Get early kills before his items come online. Your execute is reliable on a high-damage dealer who overextends.
Yone.scaling: Even early; Yone outscales in sustained fights
Yone.positioning: When he uses E, back away slightly — when he returns to his body he takes bonus damage based on damage dealt. Trade then disengage during his E window.

Galio.trading_pattern: Poke with Q and E consistently. His passive shield requires him to take magic damage to proc — your mostly physical kit makes the shield harder to use.
Galio.ability_to_dodge: Galio W (Shield of Durand) — if he taunts you, immediately W to reduce the damage and move away from his Q center.
Galio.early_game: You out-trade him solidly early. His passive needs magic damage to activate. Your all-physical trading bypasses his passive shield often.
Galio.mid_game: He roams with global ult. Push wave and deny him free roams. Your teleport can match his ult on other lanes.
Galio.late_game: His ult is incredible for engaging teamfights. Peel and use W when he taunts. Your execute on his high-HP build hurts.
Galio.win_condition: Build an early lane lead and roam before he gets his ult items. Track his ult CD and push mid when it's down.
Galio.scaling: Even — Galio better teamfight, Garen better 1v1
Galio.positioning: Stay out of his W taunt range. Don't stand still when he winds up W.

Darius.trading_pattern: Poke from range in mini form and never let him stick to you. His bleed denies your passive regen window between forms. Trade with boomerang poke then disengage.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius Q — stay outside the full ring or hug him; the outer ring is the most dangerous part. Use Hop (E) to escape after poking.

power_spikes.you: Mega Gnar form with walls nearby, Trinity Force + Black Cleaver
power_spikes.enemy: Level 5 (full bleed stacks), Stridebreaker first item

Darius.early_game: Poke safely from range in mini form. Never let him grab you with E. His bleed prevents your regen between forms so kite perpetually.
Darius.mid_game: Match his shove with wave clear. Your Mega form can punish his engage attempts if you GNAR! him into a wall.
Darius.late_game: Your GNAR! into a wall is fight-winning in teamfights. Avoid side laning vs him 1v1 — you lose that duel.
Darius.win_condition: Poke him down from range and threaten Mega form all-ins near walls. Build Black Cleaver to shred his armor during Mega form.
Darius.scaling: Darius wins 1v1; Gnar better in teamfights via GNAR! utility
Darius.positioning: Keep max range in mini form at all times. Never stand near walls unless YOU are in Mega form initiating.

Malphite.trading_pattern: Abuse his mana early with Fleet Footwork autos and Q poke. Force him to use spells for CSing. He doesn't have sustain. Win on Mega form timings with Black Cleaver.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite Q (Seismic Shard) — sidestep it; losing the movement speed from it is a big deal in mini form.
Malphite.early_game: Poke his mana down. He struggles without mana to sustain through your harass. Hit level 6 healthy and threaten Mega form.
Malphite.mid_game: He wants to roam for ult engages. Push fast and counter-rotate. Your GNAR! can match his ult as an initiator.
Malphite.late_game: Both of you want to be the engage tool. He's better in closed spaces, you're better when you can GNAR! into walls.
Malphite.win_condition: Drain his mana early, get ahead, build Black Cleaver to shred him in Mega form. Deny him roam pressure by pushing.
Malphite.scaling: Malphite outscales via ult teamfight; Gnar even in laning
Malphite.positioning: Sidestep his Q poke. Keep lane pressure to prevent roaming. Don't fight near walls when you're in mini form.

Irelia.trading_pattern: She dashes through you at max passive stacks. Only fight her at 0-2 stacks. In mini form she destroys you — save Hop for escape when she engages.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E (Flawless Duet) — sidestep the two blades. If both hit it's a stun and you lose the trade badly.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 5 (full passive stacks), Trinity Force first item

Irelia.early_game: She bullies you early. Use Hop to escape her all-ins. Farm safely and avoid giving her passive stacks from minions.
Irelia.mid_game: She outduels you in both forms once Trinity is up. Shove and rotate — avoid prolonged 1v1s.
Irelia.late_game: She's dangerous on carries. Your GNAR! can peel for your team if you land it on her.
Irelia.win_condition: Survive laning phase with CS lead. Play for teamfights where your GNAR! has more impact than her split push.
Irelia.scaling: Irelia outscales in duels; Gnar better in 5v5
Irelia.positioning: Never stand between her two E blades. Hop away from her engage immediately — don't try to out-trade her in mini form.

Renekton.trading_pattern: He bullies you especially in Mega form — you can't kite him well when slow. In mini form, use range to poke and Hop to escape his E engage.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W (Ruthless Predator) — when he uses E to gap close, immediately Hop backwards to escape the stun.

power_spikes.you: Mega Gnar form near walls, Trinity Force
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full combo), Black Cleaver + Plated Steelcaps

Renekton.early_game: He dominates early. Poke from range and never commit to fights at levels 1-5. His burst shreds your HP before you can Mega.
Renekton.mid_game: He falls off mid-game relative to early. Match his shove and play for objectives. Your Mega form GNAR! can punish his engage.
Renekton.late_game: His late game isn't impressive. Group and let your GNAR! be the teamfight initiator.
Renekton.win_condition: Survive the early lane. Get jungle help. Once past level 10-12, his power fades and your teamfight matters more.
Renekton.scaling: Renekton favored early; Gnar better in teamfights late
Renekton.positioning: Keep range in mini form. When you see his E, Hop backwards immediately. Never stand still in his auto range.

Urgot.trading_pattern: His shotgun knees destroy mini Gnar in extended trades. Stay at max boomerang range and avoid his passive proc range. He wins most all-ins.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot E (Disdain) — don't position in front of him when his shield is up. His E grab leads to a full kill combo.
Urgot.early_game: He out-ranges mini Gnar's effective fight range. Farm safely, poke with Q from max range. Avoid getting kneecapped.
Urgot.mid_game: He wins duels. Rotate and play for teamfights where your GNAR! has more value.
Urgot.late_game: His ult on carries is lethal. Your GNAR! into walls disrupts his team — land it on him before he can ult.
Urgot.win_condition: Play for macro and teamfighting. Avoid 1v1s in side lane. Get jungle help if forced to fight him.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot outscales in duels; Gnar better teamfight initiator
Urgot.positioning: Stay outside his passive knee range (roughly 490 units). Approach from angles where his knees aren't facing you.

Sett.trading_pattern: Poke him down in mini form. Don't engage in Mega form without your E jump available — you can't escape if he W's you. Trade then hop out.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W (Haymaker) — sidestep the center true damage. Hop away if he's about to throw it.

power_spikes.you: Mega form near walls, Trinity Force

Sett.early_game: Even early. Poke with boomerang. He can't easily punish your range. Avoid his W grit explosions.
Sett.mid_game: He wins sustained duels. Match his shove and keep poke pressure in mini form.
Sett.late_game: Your GNAR! into a wall can flip teamfights as hard as his ult. Both have great engage tools.
Sett.win_condition: Poke him down before all-ins. In teamfights, GNAR! him into a wall before he can get his ult off.
Sett.scaling: Even throughout; Sett slightly better in 1v1, Gnar better in team engage
Sett.positioning: Maintain range in mini form. In Mega form, only engage if walls are nearby for a GNAR! bounce.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Poke from range in mini form and dodge his Q sweet spots. His kit is designed for melee trades — your range makes his Q harder to land.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — sidestep the outer edge hitboxes. The safe zone between the two Q zones is your friend.
Aatrox.early_game: Your range advantage is huge here. Poke with Q and E autos. Don't let him land full Q combo in Mega form.
Aatrox.mid_game: He outduels you in melee range with Goredrinker. Keep range and poke. Match his shove.
Aatrox.late_game: His teamfight is good but your GNAR! into walls disrupts his combo. Peel and kite.
Aatrox.win_condition: Abuse your range early. Build lead before his items come online. Land GNAR! on him before he can ult.
Aatrox.scaling: Even early; Aatrox slightly better in duels mid-late
Aatrox.positioning: Stay at max range in mini form. Position between his Q hitboxes when forced to fight.

Jax.trading_pattern: Bully him pre-3 with range advantage. Once he has E, poke during his E cooldown. Never trade into active Counterstrike.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E (Counterstrike) — stop attacking when it's glowing. Poke with boomerang only during cooldown.
Jax.early_game: You win hard early with range. Poke him down and deny CS. Don't let him farm safely.
Jax.mid_game: He catches up with Trinity. Maintain range advantage and play for objectives.
Jax.late_game: He outduels in side lane. Group and play for teamfights via GNAR! utility.
Jax.win_condition: Snowball early range advantage into a kill. Keep him behind before Trinity Force spikes.
Jax.scaling: Gnar wins early; Jax outscales past first item
Jax.positioning: Stay at max range and poke freely. Don't auto him when his E is up.

Camille.trading_pattern: Your range advantage matters here. Poke from max range in mini form. When she uses E to engage, hop backwards and fight only if Mega form is available.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E (Hookshot) — stay away from walls so she can't get clean angles. Pre-emptive Hop when she lines up an E.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full kit), Trinity Force first item

Camille.early_game: Range advantage makes this manageable early. Don't let her E-True Grit trade on you. Keep distance.
Camille.mid_game: Her ult isolates you in Mega form. Avoid fighting near walls — she flips the GNAR! wall advantage against you.
Camille.late_game: She dives carries. Your GNAR! can interrupt her dive if you land it on her.
Camille.win_condition: Poke her down and avoid isolated 1v1s. Never fight near walls in Mega form unless you're initiating.
Camille.scaling: Camille outscales in duels; Gnar better in open teamfights
Camille.positioning: Stay center of lane away from walls. Her Hookshot needs wall geometry — deny it.

Fiora.trading_pattern: In mini form you can poke but she can Riposte your boomerang. In Mega form she parries your moves. Trade carefully and don't commit when her W is available.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W (Riposte) — never commit full combo when W is up. Bait it out first with a basic attack then use boomerang.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (Riposte CDR), Ravenous Hydra first item

Fiora.early_game: Mini form lets you poke safely. She can't easily engage on you. Maintain range and deny her Vitals.
Fiora.mid_game: She outduels you in melee. Avoid prolonged engagements in Mega form — she'll proc Vitals and heal off you.
Fiora.late_game: She dominates side lane. Group and use GNAR! for teamfight value.
Fiora.win_condition: Play for objectives and teamfights. Don't give her the extended 1v1 she wants. Use Ignite to cancel Vital healing.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora outscales in duels; Gnar better teamfight initiator
Fiora.positioning: Use range to avoid her Vitals. Reposition when a Vital spawns on the side facing you.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Deny every Q stack with boomerang poke. Your range is perfect for harassing him off CS. Make every Q last-hit cost him HP.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W (Wither) — don't chase him when he activates it; you become slow enough that he can stick to you in Mega form.

power_spikes.enemy: 300+ Q stacks, Level 6 (ult), Frozen Heart + Hullbreaker

Nasus.early_game: Absolutely bully him. Hit him every time he walks up for Q stacks. Make laning painful for him.
Nasus.mid_game: He starts getting stacks despite your harassment. GNAR! him into walls when he tries to all-in.
Nasus.late_game: 500 stacks Nasus threatens even your Mega form. Avoid 1v1s late. Group and force objectives.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny as many Q stacks as possible. Win the game before 25 minutes. Your range makes his laning miserable.
Nasus.scaling: Gnar wins early-mid; Nasus becomes unkillable past 400 stacks
Nasus.positioning: Stand at max boomerang range between Nasus and the minion he's trying to last-hit with Q.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: Poke from range before his passive shield activates. His kit needs melee range — your range makes his kit less effective. Kite his E slow.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser R (Realm of Death) — if ult catches you, immediately Hop away and W in Mega form to survive. You can't Flash out once grabbed.

power_spikes.you: Mega form near walls, Trinity Force

Mordekaiser.early_game: Your range dominates his melee trading style. Poke his passive shield down and harass with Q.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: His ult in Mega form is dangerous — you lose mobility when slow in Mega. Save Hop for ult escapes.
Mordekaiser.late_game: His ult isolates carries. In teamfights, kite and use GNAR! before he can ult.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Poke him down early and build lead. Build Grievous Wounds vs his Riftmaker healing. GNAR! him into a wall before he reaches your backline.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Even — Mordekaiser better 1v1, Gnar better teamfight initiator
Mordekaiser.positioning: Stay outside his E slow range. In Mega form, keep distance so his ult doesn't catch you at low mobility.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Poke him in mini form before he builds fury. He can't stick to you without E. His crits hurt in Mega form. Maintain range and avoid his spinning slash.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere E (Spinning Slash) — hop perpendicular when he dashes so his E doesn't catch you in a straight line.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult unkillable), Phantom Drive + Mortal Reminder

Tryndamere.early_game: Bully him early with range. He has to walk up to CS. Poke freely.
Tryndamere.mid_game: His crits start hurting in mini form. Avoid extended fights.
Tryndamere.late_game: His ult makes him unkillable for 5 seconds. Never try to kill him during ult. In teamfights, GNAR! before his ult.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Poke heavy early and build a big lead. Push objectives when he's split pushing. GNAR! his all-in before he crits you down.
Tryndamere.scaling: Gnar wins early; Tryndamere outscales in 1v1 side lane
Tryndamere.positioning: Keep max boomerang range. Hop away when he uses E. Never fight near walls in mini form.

Sion.trading_pattern: Walk toward him when he charges Q to cancel it easily — your mobility makes dodging his kit simple. Poke with boomerang freely.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q (Decimating Smash) — move toward him to interrupt range, or hop sideways to avoid the knockup direction.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult roam), Sunfire + Heartsteel passive HP stacks

Sion.early_game: You have complete lane dominance. Harass him off every CS. His immobility means you can poke freely.
Sion.mid_game: His ult roams are dangerous. Push and take plates when he roams. Match with teleport.
Sion.late_game: His HP becomes massive. Your GNAR! execute threat on low-HP enemies is great. Use ult when he's out of position.
Sion.win_condition: Dominate laning, get plates and early dragon/rift. He'll fall behind if you deny HP stacks.
Sion.scaling: Gnar wins early; Sion tankier late but weaker teamfighter vs GNAR!
Sion.positioning: Stay at max range and poke. Move toward him when Q charges to minimize its effective range.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Stay mobile to avoid his E mark and Maiden harassment. Poke with boomerang and hop away from his ghoul zones. Never fight surrounded by tentacles/ghouls.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick E (Mourning Mist) — sidestep the slow projectile; being marked causes Maiden and ghouls to prioritize you.
Yorick.early_game: You out-range him and can poke freely before Maiden. Kill his ghouls with AoE to deny his mist charges.
Yorick.mid_game: Maiden makes him dangerous. Avoid fighting with Maiden nearby. Match his split push or he takes towers.
Yorick.late_game: He's a better split pusher. Group and GNAR! for teamfight value while he pushes.
Yorick.win_condition: Deny Maiden by killing ghouls and dodging E. Build a big enough lead that his split push can't outpace your teamfight pressure.
Yorick.scaling: Gnar wins early; Yorick outscales via split push
Yorick.positioning: Move laterally to dodge E. Don't let ghouls surround you. Hop away when Maiden is active.

Olaf.trading_pattern: Your range is your only advantage. Poke with boomerang but his axes hurt even at range. In Mega form he ignores slows so you can't kite him effectively.
Olaf.ability_to_dodge: Olaf Q (Undertow) — dodge the throw and watch where it lands. He has to walk to retrieve it — punish that walk.

power_spikes.you: Mega form near walls, Trinity Force
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult CC immunity), Stridebreaker first item

Olaf.early_game: Risky poke lane. He throws axes and they deal more damage at low HP. Farm safely and avoid his all-in levels.
Olaf.mid_game: His ult makes him ignore your Mega form slows. Avoid 1v1s after level 6.
Olaf.late_game: He ignores your Mega form slow entirely during ult. GNAR! him before he activates ult in teamfights.
Olaf.win_condition: Need jungle help. Try to GNAR! him into walls before he activates his ult. Land GNAR! first = fight won.
Olaf.scaling: Olaf favored throughout during his ult window
Olaf.positioning: Stay at max range. When he throws Q at an angle away from him, punish his retrieve walk with a boomerang + autos.

Riven.trading_pattern: Poke from range in mini form. When her shield (E) is down, she's vulnerable. Hop away from her Q3 knockup. Your range denies her from trading freely.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven Q3 (Blade of the Exile) — move sideways between each Q cast to make her Q3 knockup unreliable.

power_spikes.you: Mega form near walls, Trinity Force
power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (max CDR), Ravenous Hydra + Black Cleaver

Riven.early_game: Range advantage is huge. Poke her while she goes for CS. She needs to E to shield before trading.
Riven.mid_game: She outduels at full items. Maintain range and play for teamfights.
Riven.late_game: Her execute ult timing on carries is dangerous. GNAR! her before she can full combo your backline.
Riven.win_condition: Bully early and maintain CS lead. Use Mega form GNAR! to lock her down before her burst sequence.
Riven.scaling: Even early; Riven outscales in duels
Riven.positioning: Move sideways between her Q casts. Hop away immediately when she uses Q3.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Your range advantage is key. Poke with boomerang while he CS's. He can windwall your boomerang but it's on a long CD. Engage in Mega form when windwall is used.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo W (Wind Wall) — throw boomerang after he uses it, not before. In Mega form, your GNAR! is unstoppable by Wind Wall.
Yasuo.early_game: You bully him with range. He can't freely CS. Poke every time he goes for a minion.
Yasuo.mid_game: His crits hurt in mini form. Avoid trading without Hop ready as his E can close the gap.
Yasuo.late_game: Windwall counters your boomerang poke. Time throws carefully. GNAR! works through Wind Wall.
Yasuo.win_condition: Snowball early range advantage. In teamfights, GNAR! him into a wall — your ult isn't blockable by Wind Wall.
Yasuo.scaling: Gnar wins early; Yasuo scales better with crits
Yasuo.positioning: Throw boomerang at angles where Wind Wall coverage is less effective. Approach from the side.

Yone.trading_pattern: Range poke is strong here. When he uses E (soul dash), he commits forward — trade then Hop back before he returns to his body for the bonus damage.
Yone.ability_to_dodge: Yone E (Soul Unbound) — when he dashes, Hop away. When he returns to his body, move away from where he'll land.
Yone.early_game: Poke him freely with range advantage. His Q-chain poke hurts but your range outpaces his.
Yone.mid_game: His damage ramps. Keep range, avoid his full E-Q3 combo in mini form.
Yone.late_game: His ult can reach your whole team. GNAR! him before his ult channels. He's immobile during the ult cast.
Yone.win_condition: Poke early and snowball. In teamfights, GNAR! him mid-ult to interrupt or displace him.
Yone.scaling: Gnar wins early; Yone outscales in sustained fights
Yone.positioning: When he E dashes forward, Hop backwards. Position away from where his body stands so return damage misses.

Kayle.trading_pattern: Bully her pre-11 constantly. Both you and her are ranged but your all-in threat is way higher. Mega form all-ins at levels 3-6 are devastating.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle E (Starfire Spellblade) — dodge the empowered auto range. Hop away when she winds up an E auto.
Kayle.early_game: She can't fight you. All-in constantly. Your Mega form is a nightmare for pre-11 Kayle.
Kayle.mid_game: She's becoming ranged. Apply pressure and roam. Don't let her hit 11 with a lead.
Kayle.late_game: She's untouchable with ult and full range. Avoid direct duels. Your GNAR! can scatter her team though.
Kayle.win_condition: Kill her before level 6 and roam. If she hits 11 safely, the lane is lost — win elsewhere.
Kayle.scaling: Gnar wins early-mid; Kayle wins late
Kayle.positioning: Invade her space constantly early. Deny CS with Mega form threat. Stay on top of her pre-11.

Jayce.trading_pattern: He out-ranges mini Gnar in cannon form. Only fight when he's in hammer form. Hop to dodge his hammer knockback during melee all-ins.
Jayce.ability_to_dodge: Jayce Q (Shock Blast) — dodge the arc shot in cannon form by sidestepping. In hammer form, dodge Q knockback by sidestepping.

power_spikes.you: Mega form near walls, Trinity Force
power_spikes.enemy: Level 7 (Q max cannon), Trinity Force first item

Jayce.early_game: He pokes you down from safe range. Farm with Doran's Shield. Call for jungle help.
Jayce.mid_game: His roams are strong. Push and counter-rotate. Mega form GNAR! can answer his engage.
Jayce.late_game: He falls off a bit. Group for teamfights where his poke damage is less impactful.
Jayce.win_condition: Survive laning. In teamfights, GNAR! him into a wall during his melee form when he engages.
Jayce.scaling: Jayce favored early-mid; Gnar better teamfight initiator
Jayce.positioning: Hug minion wave to reduce his cannon Q poke angle. Force him into hammer form to fight.

Vayne.trading_pattern: Both have range advantage games. She'll Silver Bolts you in Mega form for massive true damage. Keep fights short. Never stand near walls — she'll Condemn-stun you.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — never stand near walls or she stuns you. In Mega form near walls GNAR! should always be YOUR initiation, not her condemn.
Vayne.early_game: You have all-in threat she doesn't. Force early trades and Mega form all-ins.
Vayne.mid_game: Her Silver Bolts melt your HP. Avoid extended fights. Short trades only.
Vayne.late_game: Her true damage counters both your forms. Avoid dueling her. In teamfights, GNAR! her if she dives.
Vayne.win_condition: Kill her early before Guinsoo's. If you can't, roam and win elsewhere. Avoid isolated 1v1s entirely after first item.
Vayne.positioning: Never stand near walls — she'll Condemn stun you into terrain. Keep center of lane.

Poppy.trading_pattern: In Mini form, poke Poppy with Q (boomerang) and auto-attacks from max range to stack your W passive, then disengage before she can close the gap. Never commit to an extended trade in Mini form — wait until you have Mega Gnar stacked to all-in, as Poppy wins short melee exchanges cleanly.
Poppy.ability_to_dodge: Poppy's W (Steadfast Presence) is the single most important ability to respect — it creates a dome that blocks all dashes, completely neutralizing your Mini E leap and Mega E; bait it out by faking a dash, then re-engage once the ~24s cooldown is spent.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Mega Gnar unlocked) + Black Cleaver first item — gives you the stats and shred to threaten an all-in when Mega meter is full
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 and Sunfire Aegis first item — Poppy's base stats and passive shield generation make her extremely brawl-capable before you have items

Poppy.early_game: Play Mini Gnar at max range, using Q to poke and auto-attacks to proc your W passive (Hyper) for the bonus magic damage and move speed. Avoid trading at levels 1-3 when Poppy's base stats and passive shield are strongest — step on her Buckler (W shield item on the ground) to deny her from picking it up.
Poppy.mid_game: After Black Cleaver, look for Mega Gnar all-ins when your rage bar is nearly full. Position near walls to threaten a wall-bounce GNAR! ultimate, but do not dash with your E while Poppy's W dome is active. Back off immediately after Mega form expires to avoid being caught in Mini form.
Poppy.late_game: In teamfights, save Mega Gnar for initiating or following up on your team's engage — aim GNAR! to bounce multiple enemies into a wall. Stay away from Poppy in Mini form, as she can lock you down with her ult and prevent your escape. Your value is initiating big multi-man ultimates, not dueling.
Poppy.win_condition: Kite Poppy relentlessly in Mini form to keep her from engaging, step on her Buckler to deny the passive shield, bait out her W before committing any dash, then all-in with a wall-bounce GNAR! ultimate when Mega form is fully stacked. Win through superior teamfight initiation and split-push threat — not direct duel.
Poppy.scaling: Gnar outscales Poppy in the mid-to-late game due to superior teamfight initiation and ranged poke in Mini form; Poppy wins the early laning phase and is stronger in close-range skirmishes throughout most of the game.
Poppy.positioning: Always stay at the outer edge of your Q range in Mini form so Poppy cannot gap-close onto you without burning Flash; never stand near walls or the terrain edges when your Mega meter is above 80%, as a committed Poppy will try to force Mega form early and punish your cooldowns before your GNAR! is ready.

Vel'Koz.trading_pattern: As Mini Gnar, use minion bodies to block Vel'Koz's Q (Plasma Fission) by walking behind the wave before throwing your own Q for safe poke. Only go for extended trades when Vel'Koz has already blown his E (Tectonic Disruption) — his knockup is his primary trade extender and going in before it's on cooldown will get you chunked.
Vel'Koz.ability_to_dodge: Vel'Koz's E (Tectonic Disruption) — this knockup into slow is what enables his full combo. Bait it by stepping into short range and then sidestepping, because if you get knocked up while his Q and W are up, he can stack 3 Research Stacks and nearly one-shot you at level 6.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 Mega Gnar + Sunfire Aegis first item — you can all-in through his poke with Mega Gnar's resistances and burst; Gnar also spikes hard at level 9 with max W (Hyper) stacks in Mini form
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 + Luden's Tempest — Vel'Koz gains his full combo (Q-W-E-R) and a fully stacked R (Life Form Disintegration Ray) can delete you in the lane in one channel if you're CC'd

Vel'Koz.early_game: Play Mini Gnar at max Q range (550 units) and do NOT walk up to auto-attack unless Vel'Koz has used his E. Last-hit with Q when possible to minimize time standing in his skillshot cone. Track your Rage bar carefully — when Mega Gnar is ready, that is your window to hard engage before he can kite you back to Mini form.
Vel'Koz.mid_game: Rush Sunfire Aegis or Black Cleaver on first back to get enough tankiness to ignore his poke. After first item, look to proxy or shove the wave and force him away from his comfortable 550+ range. Avoid 1v1 trades when Vel'Koz has his ultimate available — instead, shove the wave and roam or coordinate with your jungler.
Vel'Koz.late_game: In teamfights, use Mega Gnar's GNAR! (R) to slam Vel'Koz into a wall and immediately follow with W (Wallop) to extend the stun lock. Focus on peeling or engaging onto squishy targets; Vel'Koz has no mobility so a good GNAR! into a wall removes him from the fight entirely. Never let him channel Life Form Disintegration Ray uninterrupted on your carries.
Vel'Koz.win_condition: Force all-ins during Mega Gnar windows before Vel'Koz can disengage or kite you back to Mini form. Position near walls in fights so GNAR! can stun him for maximum duration, then follow with W. Build enough early tankiness (Sunfire Aegis) to survive his lane poke, and engage as soon as his E is on cooldown.
Vel'Koz.scaling: Vel'Koz outscales Gnar into the late game due to his true damage passive (Research) and percentage true damage on R; Gnar's best window is mid-game once he has one tank item and can dive before Vel'Koz has full AP build.
Vel'Koz.positioning: Never stand in a straight line in the lane — Vel'Koz's Q splits into two perpendicular bolts on hit, so staying at an angle to the wave and stepping to the side every few seconds makes his Q much harder to land. In Mega Gnar, position near the nearest wall so your GNAR! slam is always threatening a maximum-duration stun.

Darius.trading_pattern: This matchup hinges on landing E. If E lands, you win — his bleed doesn't matter when tentacles are shredding him. If E misses, he out-trades you with bleed. Commit only after landing E.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius Q — the outer ring is most dangerous. Hug him or back off entirely. Don't stand in the outer ring while he charges.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult summons tentacles in teamfight), Sunfire + Hullbreaker
power_spikes.enemy: Level 5 (full bleed stacks), Stridebreaker first item

Darius.early_game: Farm and look for E windows. His bleed counters your tentacle healing. Only trade when E is landed and tentacles can follow up.
Darius.mid_game: You win side lane duels if you can E him consistently. Hullbreaker makes you a split push threat.
Darius.late_game: In teamfights your ult is a huge AoE zone. Avoid his gank trap — your 1v2 potential is actually strong with ult up.
Darius.win_condition: Land E consistently. Get Grievous Wounds removed from your list by securing kills early. His bleed is your main problem — win before GW items appear.
Darius.scaling: Even throughout — E-landing rate determines winner
Darius.positioning: Stand off-angle from his Q sweep. Force him to commit before landing E on him.

Fiora.trading_pattern: She parries your E with Riposte and heals off Vitals through your tentacles. Very hard to trade into. Only look for E after baiting her W.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W (Riposte) — bait it out before throwing E. If she has W available, don't commit your E.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (Riposte CDR), Ravenous Hydra + Trinity Force

Fiora.early_game: Very dangerous matchup. She'll Riposte your E and trade back freely. Farm defensively until level 6.
Fiora.mid_game: She outduels you and heals off your tentacles with Vitals. Avoid extended fights. Split push on the opposite side.
Fiora.late_game: She outdamages you in side lane. Play for objectives with your team. Your ult AoE is better in 5v5.
Fiora.win_condition: Bait her Riposte before committing. Get Bramble Vest early to punish her Vitals healing. Play for teamfight.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora outscales in 1v1; Illaoi better in grouped fights
Fiora.positioning: Don't stand with active Vitals facing you. Reposition to force bad Vital angles.

Jax.trading_pattern: He can't trade into your tentacles extended — his E only blocks basic attacks, not tentacle slams or E grab. Force long trades with tentacles active.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E (Counterstrike) — tentacle slams still deal damage during his E as they're not basic attacks. Trade freely through his E.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult passive), Trinity Force first item

Jax.early_game: Bully him hard. Your tentacles and E crush him in extended fights. He can't dodge your kit the way he dodges basic attacks.
Jax.mid_game: He starts coming back with Trinity. Keep trading into him — you still win with tentacle backup.
Jax.late_game: He scales better in 1v1 sidelane. Group for teamfights with ult.
Jax.win_condition: Get an early kill and snowball. Your E-into-tentacle combo destroys him pre-items. Build Hullbreaker for split push dominance.
Jax.scaling: Illaoi wins early; Jax comes back with items
Jax.positioning: Force fights in areas dense with tentacles. His Counterstrike doesn't block tentacle slam damage.

Renekton.trading_pattern: He's one of your best matchups. His engage gives you free E and tentacle procs. When he E's in, immediately E him and let tentacles slam. He can't disengage from your zone.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W (Ruthless Predator) — W your tentacle activation to survive his burst. Otherwise just let him fight inside your zone.
Renekton.early_game: Bully him extremely hard. His aggressive playstyle plays into your kit. Every time he dashes onto you, E him.
Renekton.mid_game: Keep dominating him. His power fades mid-game and you scale better. Hullbreaker split push.
Renekton.late_game: He falls off. Group and ult teamfights.
Renekton.win_condition: Be aggressive and let him come to you. His dash-engage is a death sentence when tentacles are nearby.
Renekton.positioning: Stand near tentacles. Let him engage into your zone. Never fight away from tentacle positions.

Sett.trading_pattern: His W grit damage cuts through your healing somewhat. Land E before trading — tentacle zone shuts down his ability to trade effectively. Don't trade into his W.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W (Haymaker) — sidestep the center true damage. Use tentacle pressure to force bad W positioning.
Sett.early_game: Even trading. Land E for tentacle backup. Don't face-tank his grit W.
Sett.mid_game: He wins pure 1v1 duels. Force fights near tentacles to even things out.
Sett.late_game: Your ult zone beats his ult in direct teamfights. Good matchup late.
Sett.win_condition: Land E and force fighting in tentacle zones. Your sustained DPS beats his burst when tentacles are up.
Sett.scaling: Even; Illaoi slightly better with tentacle setup
Sett.positioning: Set up tentacles between you and him before trading.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: His ult removes your tentacles from the fight — you lose your main tool. He heals through your damage with Riftmaker and your tentacles don't follow into the Realm.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser R (Realm of Death) — you can't escape this. Inside the realm your tentacles don't exist. Fight inside realm using W slam for burst.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Realm of Death), Riftmaker first item

Mordekaiser.early_game: He out-trades you before tentacles are established. Farm carefully.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: His ult completely negates your kit. Avoid 1v1s when his ult is available.
Mordekaiser.late_game: His ult on your carry or on you solo is devastating. Split push opposite side.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Play around his ult cooldown. Push and take objectives while his ult is down. Build Thornmail to counter his healing.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Mordekaiser wins this matchup due to ult countering tentacles
Mordekaiser.positioning: Don't cluster inside his W passive range. Maintain distance until his ult is on cooldown.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: His Q hitboxes make melee trading tricky. Land E and force him to fight in tentacle zones. His healing competes with yours — build GW-adjacent items.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — stand between the safe zones of his Q hitboxes when he commits, then E him during the Q animation.
Aatrox.early_game: Fairly even. Land E and trade in tentacle zones. His hitbox weaving is harder when tentacles slow him.
Aatrox.mid_game: He starts winning duels with healing. Get Bramble Vest. Keep forcing E-tentacle combos.
Aatrox.late_game: His ult healing competes with yours. Good matchup in teamfights — your ult AoE beats his ult.
Aatrox.win_condition: Land E consistently. Build Bramble Vest to cut his healing. Fight in dense tentacle zones.
Aatrox.scaling: Even throughout; Illaoi slightly better early
Aatrox.positioning: Set tentacles on his side of the wave. Force him to dodge tentacles while also dodging your E.

Camille.trading_pattern: Her mobility makes E harder to land. When she ults you in her zone, your tentacles still function inside it — use W and tentacle slam to fight her.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E (Hookshot) — stay away from walls so she can't angle her E into you. Pre-position tentacles before fights.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full kit), Trinity Force first item

Camille.early_game: She out-trades you early. Farm defensively and set up tentacles patiently.
Camille.mid_game: Her ult isolates you but your tentacles still work inside it. Fighting back in her hexzone is viable.
Camille.late_game: She dives carries. Your ult zone threatens her if she over-engages.
Camille.win_condition: Place tentacles proactively. Her ult inside your tentacle zone is actually not terrible for you. W + tentacle slam in hex zone.
Camille.scaling: Camille slightly favored in pure 1v1; Illaoi better in controlled fights
