Camille.positioning: Stay away from walls to deny her Hookshot angle. Pre-plant tentacles before she can ult you.

Garen.trading_pattern: If E lands, he cannot escape your tentacle zone with his limited mobility. Extended fights heavily favor you. Keep him in tentacle range.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen E (Judgment) — step slightly outside his spin radius after landing E so tentacles hit him while he spins in place.
Garen.early_game: You win this lane. His lack of mobility means he can't escape E. Tentacle zone dominates him.
Garen.mid_game: Keep pressure. His W silence can disrupt you briefly but tentacles ignore silence.
Garen.late_game: You outscale him in side lane. Group or split with Hullbreaker.
Garen.win_condition: Land E and hold him in tentacle zone. His silence doesn't stop tentacles. Extended fights are free wins.
Garen.scaling: Illaoi wins throughout
Garen.positioning: Position tentacles on both sides of him so no matter which direction he goes, tentacles follow.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Bully him hard pre-stacks. Land E and deny every Q stack. Your tentacles make it impossible for him to safely farm. His Wither is annoying late.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W (Wither) — don't chase him when it's active; you'll be slowed to nothing and he'll stack tentacle slams on you.

power_spikes.enemy: 300+ Q stacks, Level 6 (ult stat boost), Frozen Heart

Nasus.early_game: Abuse him constantly. Every time he walks up for a Q stack, E him. His lane is miserable into you.
Nasus.mid_game: He starts becoming tanky with stacks. Keep harassing. Hullbreaker to force his hand.
Nasus.late_game: 500 stacks Nasus hurts even you. Avoid 1v1s late. Group for objectives.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny stacks aggressively early. Win before 400 stacks. Your tentacle harassment makes farming painful for him.
Nasus.scaling: Illaoi wins early; Nasus becomes unkillable past 400 stacks
Nasus.positioning: Stand between him and the CS he's trying to Q-last-hit. Force him to choose between stacks and health.

Malphite.trading_pattern: He's passive and farms to ult. Your aggression disrupts his lane completely. Land E and trade freely — he has no way to outfight you in an extended trade.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite Q (Seismic Shard) — sidestep it. Losing the movement speed slow matters when you're trying to stick to him in tentacle range.
Malphite.early_game: Free lane. Bully him off wave, deny plates, set tentacles everywhere.
Malphite.mid_game: He'll try to roam with ult. Push fast and counter with teleport.
Malphite.late_game: His ult engage in teamfights is strong. Your ult provides a competing initiation tool.
Malphite.win_condition: Dominate lane, take plates, roam or split. Deny him the passive farming game.
Malphite.scaling: Illaoi wins lane; Malphite better teamfight initiator
Malphite.positioning: Force him to fight near tentacles. His Q slow is your only concern.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Tentacles don't care about his ult HP. Force extended fights where tentacles can slam repeatedly. His ult only delays — it doesn't save him if tentacles are active.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere E (Spinning Slash) — reposition tentacles to his new location when he dashes. His ult doesn't stop them.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult makes him unkillable for 5s), Phantom Drive first item

Tryndamere.early_game: You win early. Bully him off wave. Tentacles make his early crit trading risky.
Tryndamere.mid_game: He starts threatening with crits. Keep tentacles active in fights.
Tryndamere.late_game: His crits hurt but tentacles still kill him slowly. Never fight during his ult — wait it out.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Force fights with tentacles active. When he ults, step back and wait 5 seconds — then finish him.
Tryndamere.scaling: Illaoi slightly better; Tryndamere threatens side lane crits
Tryndamere.positioning: Place tentacles to cut off his Spinning Slash escape path.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Both have zone control and summoned minions. Your E is better than his for trading. Land E and fight — his ghouls do less damage than your tentacles per trade.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick E (Mourning Mist) — sidestep the mark; being marked means his Maiden focuses you in fights.
Yorick.early_game: You win before Maiden. Kill his ghouls to deny mist charges. E him when he CS's.
Yorick.mid_game: Maiden + Hullbreaker makes him dangerous in side lane. Match his pressure or he takes towers.
Yorick.late_game: Both of you are split-push threats. One of you should group while the other splits.
Yorick.win_condition: Deny Maiden charges early. Win before he completes Hullbreaker. Your tentacles out-zone his ghouls in a direct fight.
Yorick.scaling: Even; Illaoi slightly better early, Yorick better with Maiden
Yorick.positioning: Kill his ghouls with Q/W AoE to deny mist. Fight in open areas away from his ghoul clusters.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Deny her passive stacks by killing minions before she can reset Q on them. When she dashes in, E her immediately — tentacles slow her dashes indirectly.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E (Flawless Duet) — sidestep the stun blades. If stunned into your tentacle zone it still hurts her.
Irelia.early_game: Deny her passive stacks by fighting at 0 stacks. Your E grabs her when she dashes in.
Irelia.mid_game: She becomes hard to fight at full items. Keep tentacle zones active and trade in them.
Irelia.late_game: Her teamfight is strong. Your ult zone disrupts her too.
Irelia.win_condition: Keep her passive stacks low. E her when she dives in. Tentacle zone punishes her aggressive playstyle.
Irelia.scaling: Even; Irelia outscales in pure 1v1 dueling
Irelia.positioning: Keep low HP minions away from her to deny passive resets. Fight in tentacle-dense areas.

Riven.trading_pattern: Her shield and mobility make E landing unreliable. Look for E when her E shield is on cooldown. Tentacle zone punishes her when she commits to a full combo.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven E (Valor) — don't commit your E when her shield is available. Bait it out with a basic attack first.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (combo CDR), Ravenous Hydra + Black Cleaver

Riven.early_game: She out-trades you short-term. Farm and set tentacles. Look for E when her shield is down.
Riven.mid_game: She outduels at full items. Focus on tentacle zone control and extended fights.
Riven.late_game: Group and use ult for teamfight dominance. Avoid her 1v1.
Riven.win_condition: Catch her with E when shield is down. Drag fights into tentacle zones where extended duration favors you.
Riven.scaling: Riven wins short trades; Illaoi wins extended tentacle fights
Riven.positioning: Pre-plant tentacles around you so when she dashes in, they immediately proc.

Gnar.trading_pattern: His range means E is hard to land. In mini form he kites your tentacle zone easily. In Mega form he's actually better to fight — less mobile.
Gnar.ability_to_dodge: Gnar Q (Boomerang) — sidestep the throw and also the return path. Both hits hurt.

power_spikes.enemy: Mega form near walls, Trinity Force

Gnar.early_game: He pokes you from range and kites your tentacle zone. Farm defensively.
Gnar.mid_game: His Mega form is your opportunity — he's melee and your tentacles can grab him.
Gnar.late_game: His GNAR! into walls is dangerous. Don't cluster near walls in teamfights.
Gnar.win_condition: Force fights during his Mega form. E him when he can't kite. Keep fights away from walls where he can GNAR! you.
Gnar.scaling: Gnar outscales in ranged poke; Illaoi better in forced melee fights
Gnar.positioning: Stand in areas without nearby walls to deny GNAR! stuns. Fight only during his Mega form transition.

Trundle.trading_pattern: His Frozen Domain gives him movement speed but your tentacles have no movement — they hit regardless. Land E and fight in your zone. His resistance steal via ult is the main threat.
Trundle.ability_to_dodge: Trundle E (Pillar of Ice) — walk around it. Never let it cut you off from your tentacle zone.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult steals your armor/MR), Sunfire + Sterak's

Trundle.early_game: You win lane. Bully him constantly. His Frozen Domain doesn't help much against tentacle AoE.
Trundle.mid_game: His ult stealing your resistances is a big power flip in 1v1. Avoid ult range fights.
Trundle.late_game: Group for teamfights. His ult on a tank is devastating — don't be the frontline he targets.
Trundle.win_condition: Build a big early lead. His ult stealing armor and MR is your main counter — get ahead so it matters less.
Trundle.scaling: Illaoi wins early; Trundle's ult limits her mid-late 1v1
Trundle.positioning: Don't let his Pillar cut you off from your tentacle positions.

Sion.trading_pattern: Walk into his Q charge and E him during the charge animation. His kit requires time to execute — your E-tentacle combo is instant.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q (Decimating Smash) — move perpendicular or toward him to cancel the charge range. E him during his Q windup.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult roam), Sunfire + Heartsteel HP stacks

Sion.early_game: Free bully lane. E him whenever he charges Q. His immobility is a gift.
Sion.mid_game: He'll start tanking your tentacles. Build Black Cleaver.
Sion.late_game: His passive zombie form can still deal damage after death — stay away after killing him.
Sion.win_condition: Dominate lane. E every Q charge he attempts. Take plates and roam.
Sion.scaling: Illaoi wins early; Sion becomes tankier late but weaker
Sion.positioning: Approach him at an angle during Q charge so you can interrupt and E simultaneously.

Olaf.trading_pattern: His ult ignores your E slow/displacement effects in a sense — he can run you down regardless. Force tentacle trades before he activates ult.
Olaf.ability_to_dodge: Olaf Q (Undertow) — sidestep the axe. He needs it for his kit — force him to retrieve it in bad positions.
Olaf.early_game: Even early. Both trade well. Land E and get tentacle zone set up before his axes hit.
Olaf.mid_game: He becomes very hard to deal with post-ult. Avoid 1v1 when his ult is up.
Olaf.late_game: Your ult out-zones his ult in grouped fights. Good teamfight matchup for you.
Olaf.win_condition: Fight before his ult is up. E him and get tentacles slapping before he can activate Ragnarok.
Olaf.scaling: Even; Olaf's ult counters your kite/slow tentacle pattern
Olaf.positioning: Place tentacles before engaging. His ult doesn't make him immune to tentacle damage.

Urgot.trading_pattern: His shotgun knees out-damage your early trades. He wins short trades badly. Only fight in extended tentacle zone with E landed.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot E (Disdain) — don't stand directly in front of him at medium range. His grab leads to full combo.
Urgot.early_game: He wins early. Farm safely and avoid his passive knee range. Get tentacles up before trading.
Urgot.mid_game: He dominates 1v1. Avoid unless you have E landed and tentacles ready.
Urgot.late_game: Your ult zone competes with his in teamfights. Group where numbers help.
Urgot.win_condition: Survive laning. Get to 6 and ult with tentacles. Team-fight where your zone matters more than his dueling.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot wins 1v1; Illaoi better in grouped tentacle fights
Urgot.positioning: Don't stand in his knee proc range. Approach from the side where his knees aren't facing.

Vayne.trading_pattern: You win early all-ins before Guinsoo's. Her Silver Bolts true damage shreds you in extended fights. Kill her before items arrive.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — never stand near walls or she stuns you into a wall while tentacles can't save you.
Vayne.early_game: All-in her hard. She's squishy and can't kite your tentacle zone effectively early.
Vayne.mid_game: Silver Bolts melts your HP. Hard to win extended trades.
Vayne.late_game: True damage bypasses your healing. Avoid 1v1s late. Group for teamfights.
Vayne.win_condition: Kill her early before Guinsoo's. E her when she overextends. Your all-in window pre-items is strong.
Vayne.scaling: Illaoi wins very early; Vayne hard outscales
Vayne.positioning: Force fights away from walls. Never stand near terrain when she's nearby.

Pantheon.trading_pattern: Pantheon dominates early all-ins. Play defensive and focus on farming with minion management.
Pantheon.ability_to_dodge: Pantheon Q (Spear) — dodge by stepping behind minions or moving back.
Pantheon.early_game: Play extremely safe. Pantheon will destroy you in short trades. Farm only with minion resets.
Pantheon.mid_game: Use your E to space away from his attacks. Scale with teamfights.
Pantheon.late_game: Both scale. Avoid extended 1v1 trades against him.
Pantheon.win_condition: Survive early game and scale. Win through teamfight positioning.
Pantheon.scaling: Even — Pantheon early, scaling equalizes late game
Pantheon.positioning: Keep maximum distance. Never walk into his spear range.

Vex.trading_pattern: Vex has poke and CC. Trade only when her abilities are down, respect her Q range.
Vex.ability_to_dodge: Vex E (Looming Darkness) — dodge by staying outside the zone.
Vex.early_game: Play defensively and respect her poke. Use Q to farm safely.
Vex.mid_game: Use your mobility to dodge her abilities. Group with team.
Vex.late_game: Both scale similarly. Teamfights determine outcome.
Vex.win_condition: Use your mobility to avoid her CC. Win through teamfight coordination.
Vex.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
Vex.positioning: Stay outside her poke range. Use mobility to dodge when she goes aggressive.

Garen.trading_pattern: Garen has tankiness and high damage. Use Q to farm and keep distance with mobility.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q (Decisive Strike) — move back when he walks forward to avoid it.
Garen.early_game: Play very defensively. Garen wins short trades and sustains.
Garen.mid_game: Use E to kite away. Don't let him stick to you.
Garen.late_game: He becomes very tanky. Avoid 1v1s.
Garen.win_condition: Roam and create advantages elsewhere. Win through teamfight timing.
Garen.scaling: Garen outscales — his tankiness increases significantly
Garen.positioning: Keep distance and use E to kite. Never walk into his all-in range.

Sett.trading_pattern: Sett has True Damage all-in. Trade only at range with Q, avoid close range.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett E (Flay) — dodge by stepping back or to the side.
Sett.early_game: Play at range with Q farming. Avoid all-in trades.
Sett.mid_game: Use E to kite and escape. Group with team.
Sett.late_game: He becomes tanky. Space in teamfights.
Sett.win_condition: Use mobility to avoid his all-in. Win through teamfight coordination.
Sett.scaling: Sett slightly favored — his tankiness scales well
Sett.positioning: Keep distance with Q farming. Never trade close range.

Akali.trading_pattern: Akali is melee-focused. Trade when her abilities are down, use your range advantage.
Akali.ability_to_dodge: Akali E (Shuriken Flip) — dodge by moving away or stepping back.
Akali.early_game: Trade when she's going for CS. Use range to your advantage.
Akali.mid_game: Use mobility to dodge her engage. Both champions scale similarly.
Akali.late_game: Depends on positioning and who engages first.
Akali.win_condition: Use mobility to dodge her abilities. Win through better teamfight positioning.
Akali.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
Akali.positioning: Keep distance with Q farming. Use E to kite when she engages.

Qiyana.trading_pattern: Qiyana has early burst and CC. Play defensively and farm safely.
Qiyana.ability_to_dodge: Qiyana E (Audacity) — dodge by moving away when she dashes.
Qiyana.early_game: Farm defensively. Qiyana has strong all-in potential.
Qiyana.mid_game: Use E to escape her engages. Group with team.
Qiyana.win_condition: Use mobility to escape her all-in. Win through teamfight coordination.
Qiyana.scaling: Qiyana slightly favored — her roam and side lane control is superior
Qiyana.positioning: Keep distance and use E to kite. Avoid narrow angles.

Corki.trading_pattern: Corki is weak early. Use Q to farm and harass. Respect his bomb damage.
Corki.ability_to_dodge: Corki Q (Phosphorus Bomb) — dodge by stepping back.
Corki.early_game: Dominate early with Q farming and trading.
Corki.mid_game: Maintain pressure. Corki needs time to scale.
Corki.late_game: He becomes strong. Finish before he gets 2-3 items.
Corki.win_condition: Get an early lead and snowball. Finish before Corki scales.
Corki.scaling: Corki outscales — his split-push and poke translate better late
Corki.positioning: Use Q to farm and trade. Keep distance from his bomb zones.

Smolder.trading_pattern: Smolder is very weak early. Use Q to farm and harass freely.
Smolder.ability_to_dodge: Smolder Q (Breath) — dodge by stepping back.
Smolder.early_game: Completely dominate. Farm freely and harass with Q.
Smolder.mid_game: Maintain pressure and roam. Don't let him scale.
Smolder.late_game: He outscales significantly. Finish before he gets 2-3 items.
Smolder.win_condition: Get a lead and snowball. Finish before Smolder scales.
Smolder.scaling: Smolder significantly outscales — you need a major lead
Smolder.positioning: Abuse early game with Q. Push him off farm.

Jayce.trading_pattern: Jayce has early range advantage. Use Q to farm, respect his gate-empowered abilities.
Jayce.ability_to_dodge: Jayce Q (Shock Blast) — dodge by stepping back or to the side.
Jayce.early_game: Play somewhat defensively. Jayce dominates early range matchups.
Jayce.mid_game: Use mobility to close distance. Group with team.
Jayce.late_game: Both scale. Teamfights favor whoever engages first.
Jayce.win_condition: Use mobility to close distance. Win through teamfight engagement.
Jayce.positioning: Use Q to farm. Close distance when you can engage.

Ryze.trading_pattern: Ryze has poke and weak early game still. Trade when his abilities are down.
Ryze.ability_to_dodge: Ryze Q (Overload) — dodge by stepping back or to the side.
Ryze.early_game: Play somewhat defensively. Ryze needs time to scale.
Ryze.mid_game: Both scale. Use mobility to dodge his abilities.
Ryze.late_game: He outscales in teamfights. Space properly.
Ryze.win_condition: Get an early advantage before he scales. Use mobility in teamfights.
Ryze.scaling: Ryze outscales — his teamfight control improves late
Ryze.positioning: Use Q to farm. Close distance when possible.

Ekko.trading_pattern: Ekko is mobile with poke. Trade when his abilities are down.
Ekko.ability_to_dodge: Ekko W (Parallel Convergence) — dodge the stun zone.
Ekko.early_game: Trade when he's vulnerable. Both have similar scaling.
Ekko.mid_game: Use mobility to outmaneuver him. Group with team.
Ekko.late_game: Both scale similarly. Teamfights determine outcome.
Ekko.win_condition: Win through better positioning and mobility.
Ekko.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
Ekko.positioning: Use Q to farm. Keep distance from his all-in range.

Taliyah.trading_pattern: Taliyah has strong waveclear and poke. Trade only when her abilities are down.
Taliyah.ability_to_dodge: Taliyah Q (Threaded Volley) — dodge by moving perpendicular.
Taliyah.early_game: Play defensively. Taliyah's waveclear is superior early.
Taliyah.mid_game: Both have roam potential. Use mobility advantage.
Taliyah.win_condition: Use mobility to catch her or escape her engage.
Taliyah.scaling: Taliyah slightly favored — her roaming is stronger
Taliyah.positioning: Use Q to farm. Avoid standing in Q volley lines.

Orianna.trading_pattern: Orianna has poke and teamfight control. Trade when her ball is far.
Orianna.ability_to_dodge: Orianna Q (Command: Attack) — dodge by stepping back.
Orianna.early_game: Play defensively. Orianna has decent early poke.
Orianna.mid_game: Use mobility to dodge her abilities. Group with team.
Orianna.late_game: She outscales in teamfights. Space properly.
Orianna.win_condition: Use mobility to catch her or avoid her R. Win through teamfight coordination.
Orianna.scaling: Orianna outscales — her teamfight utility is superior
Orianna.positioning: Use Q to farm. Keep distance from her ball.

Ahri.trading_pattern: Ahri has poke and CC. Trade only when her abilities are down.
Ahri.ability_to_dodge: Ahri Q (Orb of Deception) — dodge by stepping sideways.
Ahri.early_game: Play defensively. Ahri has decent early poke.
Ahri.mid_game: Use mobility to avoid her abilities. Group with team.
Ahri.win_condition: Use mobility to engage or escape. Win through teamfight positioning.
Ahri.scaling: Ahri slightly favored — her mobility and CC provide utility
Ahri.positioning: Use Q to farm. Keep distance from her all-in range.

Diana.trading_pattern: Diana is melee-focused. Trade when her abilities are down.
Diana.ability_to_dodge: Diana Q (Crescent Strike) — dodge by stepping sideways.
Diana.early_game: Trade when she's vulnerable. Both have similar scaling.
Diana.mid_game: Use mobility to outmaneuver her. Group with team.
Diana.late_game: Both scale similarly. Teamfights determine outcome.
Diana.win_condition: Win through better positioning and engagement.
Diana.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
Diana.positioning: Use Q to farm. Close distance when you can engage.

LeBlanc.trading_pattern: LeBlanc has burst and deception. Play defensively and farm safely.
LeBlanc.ability_to_dodge: LeBlanc E (Ethereal Chains) — dodge by stepping away or to the side.
LeBlanc.early_game: Play very defensively. LeBlanc has superior all-in damage.
LeBlanc.mid_game: Use mobility to dodge her abilities. Group with team.
LeBlanc.win_condition: Use mobility to avoid her combo. Win through teamfight coordination.
LeBlanc.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
LeBlanc.positioning: Use Q to farm. Never walk into her all-in range.

Katarina.trading_pattern: Katarina is all-in burst. Play defensively and farm with Q.
Katarina.ability_to_dodge: Katarina E (Shunpo) — when she dashes in, retreat immediately.
Katarina.early_game: Play defensively. Katarina has strong all-in.
Katarina.mid_game: Use mobility to escape her all-in. Group with team.
Katarina.late_game: Avoid teamfights where she gets resets. Space properly.
Katarina.win_condition: Avoid her reset opportunities. Win through teamfight coordination.
Katarina.scaling: Katarina slightly favored — her reset potential is stronger
Katarina.positioning: Use Q to farm. Never walk into melee range.

Cassiopeia.trading_pattern: Cassiopeia has poke and poison. Trade when her abilities are down.
Cassiopeia.ability_to_dodge: Cassiopeia W (Miasma) — walk around it or wait for dissipation.
Cassiopeia.early_game: Play somewhat defensively. Cassio has decent early poke.
Cassiopeia.mid_game: Use mobility to close distance. Group with team.
Cassiopeia.late_game: She outscales in sustained teamfights. Space properly.
Cassiopeia.win_condition: Use mobility to catch her or avoid poison. Win before she scales.
Cassiopeia.scaling: Cassiopeia outscales — her sustained damage is overwhelming late
Cassiopeia.positioning: Use Q to farm. Keep distance from her poison zones.

Syndra.trading_pattern: Syndra has poke and CC. Trade only when her abilities are down.
Syndra.ability_to_dodge: Syndra E (Scatter the Weak) — dodge by stepping away from her orbs.
Syndra.early_game: Play defensively. Syndra has strong early poke.
Syndra.mid_game: Use mobility to dodge her abilities. Group with team.
Syndra.win_condition: Use mobility to avoid her CC. Win through teamfight coordination.
Syndra.scaling: Syndra slightly favored — her spike potential with stacks is higher
Syndra.positioning: Use Q to farm. Keep distance from her orb zones.

Azir.trading_pattern: Azir has soldiers for poke. Trade when soldiers are down.
Azir.ability_to_dodge: Azir soldiers have range; respect their position.
Azir.early_game: Play somewhat defensively. Azir's early poke is decent.
Azir.mid_game: Use mobility to close distance. Group with team.
Azir.late_game: He outscales in teamfights. Space properly.
Azir.win_condition: Use mobility to catch him before he gets soldiers off. Win before he scales.
Azir.scaling: Azir outscales — his teamfight control improves late
Azir.positioning: Use Q to farm. Close distance to prevent soldier spam.

Mel.trading_pattern: Mel has poke and reflect defense. Trade carefully, angle abilities away from her reflect.
Mel.ability_to_dodge: Mel E (Reflect) — your auto-attacks will reflect damage, so use abilities.
Mel.early_game: Trade carefully. Use abilities primarily to avoid reflect punishment.
Mel.mid_game: Both scale. Use mobility to engage or escape.
Mel.late_game: Both scale similarly. Teamfights determine outcome.
Mel.win_condition: Use abilities to trade, avoid auto-attacks. Win through teamfight coordination.
Mel.scaling: Even — both scale at similar rates
Mel.positioning: Use abilities for trading. Avoid standing in predictable patterns.

Darius.trading_pattern: Only trade at 0 passive stacks. His bleed prevents you from resetting Q reliably since minions die slower under bleed. Stay at range until you have 5 stacks, then all-in fast.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius Q — stay outside the full swing or hug him. The outer ring is the kill zone. Pre-position before engaging.

power_spikes.you: Level 5 (max passive stacks easier), Trinity Force

Darius.early_game: Very dangerous. He out-trades you at nearly all stages without 5 stacks. Farm safely and only short-trade when passive is maxed.
Darius.mid_game: At Trinity Force you can challenge him. Use E to kite after Q resets.
Darius.late_game: He's a stronger frontliner. Play for teamfights where your mobility shines.
Darius.win_condition: Get to 5 passive stacks before engaging. E his bleed to kite between autos. Take Conqueror + Ignite for early kill window.
Darius.scaling: Darius wins early; even mid; Irelia better mobility late
Darius.positioning: Keep minions between you at 0 stacks. Never engage without passive stacked.

Garen.trading_pattern: His Q silence prevents you from Q-resetting during the silence window. Engage only when Q silence is on cooldown. His E doesn't stop your Q resets.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q — when you see him sprint at you, back off immediately. His silence kills your combo entirely.
Garen.early_game: He out-trades you early. Play passive, stack passive on minions, engage only when his Q is on CD.
Garen.mid_game: You win duels with Trinity if you can avoid his silence. Short Q-resets to build and drain his HP.
Garen.late_game: You out-scale him in mobility. Teamfights favor your kit.
Garen.win_condition: Engage after his Q is used. Never let him land a full Q-E-W trade — the silence disables your combo.
Garen.scaling: Garen slightly better early; Irelia outscales with stacks
Garen.positioning: Maintain enough distance to react to his Q sprint. Don't stand still in his face.

Malphite.trading_pattern: He counters your entire kit — his passive armor reduces Q reset damage, his W slows your attack speed, your DPS drops massively. He wins all trades.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite W (Thunderclap) — he activates it before engaging. Try to disengage before he procs it fully.
Malphite.early_game: Avoid all extended trades. He reduces your attack speed which breaks your Q reset rhythm entirely.
Malphite.mid_game: You can't really fight him. Shove and roam. His armor is a permanent wall against you.
Malphite.late_game: Avoid being his ult target. Peel away from his zone before teamfights begin.
Malphite.win_condition: Roam and create leads elsewhere. You almost can't win this lane — play for map pressure.
Malphite.scaling: Malphite counters Irelia throughout
Malphite.positioning: Keep distance. Push fast and roam rather than trying to fight him.

Jax.trading_pattern: You win early before his E. Once he has Counterstrike, your Q resets get blocked. Only trade during E cooldown windows.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E (Counterstrike) — don't autoattack when spinning. Use Q resets carefully during his E.
Jax.early_game: Abuse him hard pre-3. Get a kill at level 2 if possible. Once he has E, the matchup flips.
Jax.mid_game: He outduels you with Trinity + Counterstrike. Match his shove and roam.
Jax.late_game: He outscales in 1v1 significantly. Play for teamfights.
Jax.win_condition: Kill him pre-3 or snowball early lead before he gets items. If he hits Trinity even, it's very hard.
Jax.scaling: Irelia wins early; Jax hard outscales
Jax.positioning: Be highly aggressive levels 1-2. Position for E-Q at level 2 for all-in potential.

Sett.trading_pattern: He counters you due to his auto-attack reliance matching up poorly with your Q resets. His W grit damage shreds through your trades. Avoid extended auto-heavy fights.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W (Haymaker) — sidestep the center true damage or use Q onto a minion to escape the zone.
Sett.early_game: He beats you in almost every trade. Play very passively and stack passive on minions only.
Sett.mid_game: He wins 1v1 consistently. Shove and roam to create leads elsewhere.
Sett.late_game: In teamfights, his ult grabs you if you're out of position. Stay mobile and peel carries.
Sett.win_condition: Roam early and create pressure. You can't win this lane — win through mid or jungle intervention.
Sett.positioning: Don't face-tank his auto attacks. Keep minions between you.

Fiora.trading_pattern: She can Riposte your E stun or your Q engage. Bait her W before committing. Your Q resets are very strong when she has no W. Both have high mechanics requirement.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W (Riposte) — bait it with a basic attack before committing to E-Q combo. Her Riposte on your E stun is devastating.
Fiora.early_game: Both have kill threat. Bait her W at level 1 before engaging at level 2.
Fiora.mid_game: High skill matchup. Both can kill each other. Mechanics decide this entirely.
Fiora.late_game: She outduels you in side lane with full items. Rotate to teamfights.
Fiora.win_condition: Bait her Riposte every fight. Deny her Vitals by repositioning. Use Ignite to cancel healing.
Fiora.scaling: Even; Fiora slightly better in extended 1v1 duels
Fiora.positioning: Reposition away from her active Vital side during fights.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Both have strong all-in damage. His Q hitboxes are your main challenge — sidestep them while maintaining Q reset chains. Your passive stacks beat his passive revive pressure.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — stand in the safe zone between his Q hitboxes. Your E lets you dash through him to avoid Q zones.
Aatrox.early_game: Fairly even. Trade when you have 5 passive stacks. Avoid his Q sweet spots.
Aatrox.mid_game: High skill fight. Both can win depending on execution.
Aatrox.late_game: Your Q resets in teamfights are stronger than his kit in chaos.
Aatrox.win_condition: Maintain 5 stacks. E through him to avoid Q zones. Use Conqueror + Ignite for kill pressure.
Aatrox.scaling: Even; slight Aatrox favor in sustained 1v1
Aatrox.positioning: E through him to get behind him — his Q hitboxes are primarily in front.

Camille.trading_pattern: Mirror of skilled mechanics. Her E true damage hurts during Q reset chains. Trade aggressively when her shield is down. Both have strong all-in threat.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E (Hookshot) — stay away from walls. Pre-emptively Q dash when she lines up wall Hookshot.
Camille.early_game: Skill matchup from level 1. Both are strong 1v1s. Passive stack management is key.
Camille.mid_game: Very similar power levels. Mechanics decide fights entirely.
Camille.late_game: Both are excellent duelists. Play for teamfights where Q resets hit multiple targets.
Camille.win_condition: Keep 5 stacks and engage when her shield E is on CD. Use Ignite to burn her out.
Camille.scaling: Even throughout
Camille.positioning: Stay away from walls to deny her Hookshot angle advantage.

Renekton.trading_pattern: He wins levels 1-5 decisively. His burst combo outdamages your setup. Stack passive on minions and only engage in brief windows after his E.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W (Ruthless Predator) — when he uses E to close, Q onto a low minion immediately to escape his stun radius.
Renekton.early_game: He dominates you early. Farm under tower if needed. His burst combo stops your Q reset chain cold.
Renekton.mid_game: He falls off mid-game. You start trading even. Shove and pick your fights.
Renekton.late_game: You outscale him. Group and dish out Q resets in teamfights.
Renekton.win_condition: Survive laning. Get jungle help. After level 10-12 your power is significantly higher.
Renekton.scaling: Renekton favored early; Irelia outscales mid-late
Renekton.positioning: Farm under tower early. Use minions to block his E engage path.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Abuse him pre-6. Your Q resets are fast enough to proc full passive stacks quickly. His crits can surprise you but your burst is faster.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere E (Spinning Slash) — Q onto a low HP minion when he dashes to maintain distance and reset your own Q.
Tryndamere.early_game: You win early before crits. Go aggressive with Conqueror + Ignite.
Tryndamere.mid_game: His crits start threatening. Keep using Q resets to stay mobile.
Tryndamere.late_game: His ult prevents you from finishing kills. Wait out his ult before committing. Never ult during his ult.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Get kills pre-6. When he ults, Q onto a minion and wait it out — don't commit. Finish after ult expires.
Tryndamere.scaling: Irelia wins early; even mid; Tryndamere better side lane late
Tryndamere.positioning: Maintain Q range onto minions so you can always chain resets to escape or re-engage.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Kill his ghouls with your Q to deny resets and remove his ghoul DPS. Without ghouls, trading favors you heavily. Engage when he has low mist charges.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick E (Mourning Mist) — sidestep the mark. Being marked causes Maiden to focus you.
Yorick.early_game: Win early by denying ghoul resets. Your Q chain through ghouls gives free stacks.
Yorick.mid_game: With Maiden he becomes dangerous. Match his shove.
Yorick.late_game: He out-splits you. Group for teamfight advantage.
Yorick.win_condition: Deny Maiden by killing ghouls constantly. Kill him before Hullbreaker comes online.
Yorick.scaling: Irelia wins early; Yorick outscales in split push
Yorick.positioning: Q between his ghouls to clear them and build stacks simultaneously.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Abuse every time he goes for Q stacks. Your Q chain shreds his HP fast. He has no early damage — engage freely.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W (Wither) — Q onto a nearby minion to escape the slow before it fully stacks on you.
Nasus.early_game: Free bully lane. Deny every Q stack. He cannot fight back at low stacks.
Nasus.mid_game: He becomes tankier. Build Black Cleaver.
Nasus.late_game: 500 stacks Nasus can threaten you. Avoid 1v1s late. Group.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny stacks hard. Build a big lead and snowball before 400 stacks.
Nasus.scaling: Irelia wins early; Nasus outscales past 400 stacks
Nasus.positioning: Step between him and the minion he's trying to Q last-hit.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: His passive shield absorbs your first trade. Burn it with Q procs on minions then go back in. Inside his ult, you have no minions for Q resets — be careful.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser R (Realm of Death) — if caught, Q a nearby ghoul or illusion if available, otherwise W and fight. No Q resets in the realm.
Mordekaiser.early_game: Even early. Poke his passive down with Q procs then trade properly.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: His ult isolated is dangerous with no Q reset targets. Avoid his ult when it's available.
Mordekaiser.late_game: His ult on your carries is devastating. Peel and interrupt with E if he targets backline.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Bait his passive shield before all-ins. Track ult cooldown — fight freely when it's down.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Even; Mordekaiser slightly better in 1v1 due to ult
Mordekaiser.positioning: Don't fight in the golden W passive ring zone — it gives him bonus stats.

Shen.trading_pattern: He wins trades in his W dodge window. Your Q resets don't proc during his dodge window if he activates it right. Poke with E then wait for W to expire before Q chain.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: Shen W (Spirit's Refuge) — stop all-ins when he activates it. Q onto a minion to wait it out safely.
Shen.early_game: He out-trades you. His W blocks your Q reset procs if timed right. Farm safely.
Shen.mid_game: His ult roams are map-deciding. Push wave and track his ult.
Shen.late_game: You outscale in direct duels but his global utility is stronger.
Shen.win_condition: Poke with E first to bait W, then Q chain. Push and force him to stay in lane — deny his ult value.
Shen.scaling: Shen wins early; Irelia outscales in direct 1v1 late
Shen.positioning: Stand far enough back to dash out of W range when he activates it.

Trundle.trading_pattern: Q chain him freely early. His kit doesn't counter your resets. Avoid his Frozen Domain — his movement speed in W makes sticking to him for resets harder.
Trundle.ability_to_dodge: Trundle E (Pillar of Ice) — Q through it using a minion on the other side. Never let it cut off your Q reset chain.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult steals your stats), Sunfire + Sterak's

Trundle.early_game: You win early. Bully him off wave.
Trundle.mid_game: His ult stealing your resistances flips the matchup badly. Avoid 1v1 at level 6.
Trundle.late_game: His ult on you in teamfights is devastating. Stay mobile.
Trundle.win_condition: Build early kill lead. His ult stealing your stats makes extended fights dangerous — get ahead while you can.
Trundle.scaling: Irelia wins early; Trundle's ult limits her mid-late
Trundle.positioning: Use Q jumps to get around his Pillar. Keep it from cutting off your reset chain.

Urgot.trading_pattern: His passive shotgun knees deal massive damage per Q reset attempt. You can't trade through his passive procs. Only fight when he's out of passive stacks.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot Q (Corrosive Charge) — sidestep to avoid E amplification. His W activates knees on nearby enemies — don't approach when W is active.
Urgot.early_game: Very tough. Farm under tower. His passive means your Q resets are getting blasted each time.
Urgot.mid_game: He wins duels. Shove and roam to create leads elsewhere.
Urgot.late_game: His ult execute is a teamfight changer. Group to deny isolated targets.
Urgot.win_condition: Need jungle help to get a kill early. Roam and play for team. You can't win this lane solo.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot outscales in 1v1; Irelia better in teamfight chaos
Urgot.positioning: Stay outside his W knee activation range (medium range). Approach from angles where his Q marker isn't amplifying.

Vayne.trading_pattern: Kill her before Guinsoo's. Your early all-in is strong. Her Silver Bolts true damage shreds your health in extended fights.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — never stand near walls. Q onto a minion near her to approach from odd angles she can't condemn toward walls.
Vayne.early_game: All-in her hard early. She's squishy and can't escape your Q resets.
Vayne.mid_game: Her Silver Bolts and Guinsoo's make extended fights dangerous. Keep fights short.
Vayne.late_game: She counters your tank-stacking with true damage. Avoid 1v1s. Go for her in teamfights with burst.
Vayne.win_condition: Kill her pre-Guinsoo's. Take Ignite. Don't let her stack Silver Bolts in fights — burst her fast.
Vayne.scaling: Irelia wins very early; Vayne hard outscales
Vayne.positioning: Approach from angles away from walls. Q dash toward her to close gap faster than she can reposition.

Varus.trading_pattern: He pokes from extreme range before you can engage. Your only window is when his Q is charging — dash in then. His W blight stacks hurt in extended fights.
Varus.ability_to_dodge: Varus Q (Piercing Arrow) — dash sideways when he's charging a full Q. Move laterally — it's a slow projectile.
Varus.early_game: He pokes you down before you can engage. Farm under tower. Call for jungle.
Varus.mid_game: His W stacks melt you in trades. Hit him fast and get out.
Varus.late_game: His ult chains your team. Approach from flanks to avoid R.
Varus.win_condition: Engage when his Q is charging. Jungle pressure helps. Build speed to close the gap quickly.
Varus.scaling: Varus outscales via range; Irelia needs to kill fast
Varus.positioning: Use minion cover to close distance. Dash in from diagonal angles when his Q charges.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: He can block your E with Wind Wall. Bait Wind Wall with a basic auto first then use E. Your Q resets beat his kit if you get in melee range.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo W (Wind Wall) — bait it before using E. If W is up, don't throw E.
Yasuo.early_game: Even. Both have strong all-ins. Bait Wind Wall early before committing.
Yasuo.mid_game: His crits start dealing massive damage. W his ult combo. Q resets keep you mobile.
Yasuo.late_game: Both are good 1v1 fighters. His crits vs your resets — skill matchup.
Yasuo.win_condition: Bait Wind Wall before using E. Get Plated Steelcaps for his crit damage.
Yasuo.scaling: Even throughout; both scale into good late game fighters
Yasuo.positioning: Approach sideways to reduce Wind Wall coverage angle.

Yone.trading_pattern: When he uses E, trade then disengage before he returns. His return gives bonus damage. Your Q resets are strong when he's committed to E dash.
Yone.ability_to_dodge: Yone E (Soul Unbound) — when he dashes, trade then back off before return. His physical return hits hard.
Yone.early_game: Even early. Trade when he commits E. Disengage before his body returns.
Yone.mid_game: His damage ramps. Use W to survive his ult. Q resets help kite.
Yone.late_game: His ult hits your whole team. Q resets across multiple targets shine here.
Yone.win_condition: Engage when his E soul is away. Deny his body's bonus return damage with E stun.
Yone.scaling: Even; Yone slightly better in sustained fights
Yone.positioning: Move away from where his body stood when he E's — that's where he returns with bonus damage.

Kayle.trading_pattern: Abuse her pre-11 constantly. Your all-in is devastating against early Kayle. Kill her at level 2 with E-Q combo.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle E (Starfire Spellblade) — dodge the ranged empowered auto range. Engage immediately after she uses it on a minion.
Kayle.early_game: Bully her off wave. She can't fight you early. Force kills at level 2-3.
Kayle.mid_game: She gains range progressively. Keep the pressure on before 11.
Kayle.late_game: At 11 you can't reach her. Roam and make plays elsewhere.
Kayle.win_condition: Kill her twice before level 6 and deny CS. Snowball the lead before she hits 11.
Kayle.scaling: Irelia wins early; Kayle wins past level 11
Kayle.positioning: Stay on top of her at all times before level 11. Deny every CS with E-Q aggression.

Darius.trading_pattern: His bleed delays your E cooldown windows. Short trades with E to dodge his Q outer ring, then reset. Don't let him land full W stun into bleed.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius Q — hug him or stay completely outside. His outer ring is the kill zone. E into him when he winds up Q.
Darius.early_game: He out-trades you pre-6. Use E to dodge his Q and trade briefly. Don't let him stick full bleed.
Darius.mid_game: You match him with Trinity. Extended fights now favor you with E and ult stacks.
Darius.late_game: You outscale him significantly. Your ult passive armor + ult shred wins duels.
Darius.win_condition: Play defensive early, scale to Trinity. Use E to dodge his Q and trade hard on the window after it misses.
Darius.scaling: Darius wins early; Jax outscales past first item
Darius.positioning: Hug him when he winds up Q to stay inside the non-lethal zone.

Garen.trading_pattern: His Q silence prevents your E from activating during silence. Trade when his Q is on cooldown. Your E blocks his E spin damage entirely.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q — back off when you see him sprint at you. E is unavailable during silence so dodge it first.
Garen.early_game: You win early. E to dodge his Q then auto-attack him freely. His E deals less than your sustained damage.
Garen.mid_game: Dominate him. Your ult passive gives free armor/MR stacks that outscale his tankiness.
Garen.late_game: You outscale significantly. Avoid his execute ult when low.
Garen.win_condition: Dodge his Q with early positioning and trade freely. His silence window is your only danger.
Garen.scaling: Jax wins throughout
Garen.positioning: Pre-activate E before he sprints so it's already in dodge mode when Q lands.

Fiora.trading_pattern: Your E blocks her W Riposte bait — but she can Riposte your E activation. Both need to bait each other's defensive tools. Trade when her W is down.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W (Riposte) — bait it with a basic attack first, then activate E once it's used.
Fiora.early_game: High skill matchup from level 1. Bait W before committing E. Both have kill threat.
Fiora.mid_game: She wins extended duels with Vitals healing. Keep trades short after E.
Fiora.late_game: She outduels you in split push 1v1. Group for teamfights.
Fiora.win_condition: Bait W every fight. Use Ignite to cancel Vitals healing. Get kills early before she completes Hydra.
Fiora.scaling: Even early; Fiora slightly better in extended 1v1
Fiora.positioning: Reposition away from her active Vital sides.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Stand in his Q safe zones (between the hitboxes) and trade with E. Your E blocks some of his Q follow-up autos. Extended fights favor you.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — sidestep the outer edge hitboxes. The center zone between Q areas is relatively safe.
Aatrox.early_game: Even. Trade when you can position between his Q hitboxes. Your E is strong when he commits.
Aatrox.mid_game: Your Trinity beat matches his Goredrinker. Extended fights favor you.
Aatrox.late_game: You outscale him. His ult healing matters less once you have full items.
Aatrox.win_condition: Position between his Q zones. Extended fights with E cooldown cycling beat his healing.
Aatrox.scaling: Even early; Jax outscales mid-late
Aatrox.positioning: Stand between his Q hitboxes. E in when he commits to Q cast.

Camille.trading_pattern: Her passive shield absorbs your first trade. Burn it with a basic attack then E. Her true damage during E is dangerous — dodge or absorb with E.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E (Hookshot) — stay away from walls. Your E dodge can block her E true damage if timed right.
Camille.early_game: She has an edge early. Farm safely and wait for your E cooldown windows.
Camille.mid_game: Even power levels. Mechanics decide this entirely.
Camille.late_game: You outscale her in direct 1v1 with ult passive stacks.
Camille.win_condition: Burn her passive shield first. Fight in open areas away from walls.
Camille.scaling: Even; Jax slightly better with full ult stacks
Camille.positioning: Stay center of lane. Her Hookshot requires walls.

Renekton.trading_pattern: One of your best matchups. His engage gives you free E windows. When he E's onto you, immediately E to dodge his W stun then trade back freely.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W (Ruthless Predator) — activate E before he lands after his E dash. Dodge the stun entirely.
Renekton.early_game: You dominate. His E-W combo gets blocked by your E. Trade freely after dodging his W.
Renekton.mid_game: You win convincingly. Build snowball lead.
Renekton.late_game: He falls off. You continue to dominate.
Renekton.win_condition: Time E to his W stun. Trade freely after. Build a big early lead and snowball.
Renekton.positioning: Pre-activate E when he winds up E dash so W stun is dodged on landing.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Bully him every time he goes for Q stacks. Your E + sustained damage makes trading very favorable. His Wither is annoying but E windows let you trade through it.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W (Wither) — E when he Withers you; your damage during E window still applies.
Nasus.early_game: Free lane. Deny every Q stack. Trade hard whenever he approaches.
Nasus.mid_game: He becomes tankier. Build Black Cleaver or Lord Dom's to shred.
Nasus.late_game: 500 stacks Nasus hurts even you. Avoid 1v1 late. Avoid Wither-into-extended fight.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny stacks aggressively. Win before 400 stacks. Your harass makes farming miserable for him.
Nasus.scaling: Jax wins early-mid; Nasus threatens past 400 stacks
Nasus.positioning: Stand between him and the minion he's trying to Q last-hit.

Malphite.trading_pattern: His W reduces your attack speed massively. Your E and damage output crumble against his armor. He counters your entire trading pattern.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite Q (Seismic Shard) — sidestep it. The movement speed slow compounds with his W slow against you.
Malphite.early_game: Avoid trading. His armor and attack speed reduction nullify your kit. Farm and roam.
Malphite.mid_game: He wins all duels. Shove and roam aggressively.
Malphite.late_game: His ult is a teamfight changer. You win sidelane with enough items.
Malphite.win_condition: Build magic penetration (Wit's End). His armor makes physical damage useless. Roam to create leads.
Malphite.scaling: Malphite counters Jax throughout laning; Jax better late with mixed damage
Malphite.positioning: Push fast and roam. Don't fight him — you can't.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: His passive shield absorbs early trades. Poke it down then E-trade. Inside his ult you lose the E dodge advantage partially — fight decisively.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser R (Realm of Death) — when ult catches you, immediately E and fight aggressively. You have no escape so fight.
Mordekaiser.early_game: Even. Poke his passive before committing. Avoid his E slow.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: His ult isolated is dangerous. Avoid 1v1 when ult is available.
Mordekaiser.late_game: Even matchup. Your ult passive gives great stats for dueling.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Track his ult cooldown. Fight freely when it's down. Build Thornmail to reduce his healing.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Even; Jax slightly better with full ult stack advantage
Mordekaiser.positioning: Don't stand inside his W passive ring. Stay outside the golden zone.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Your E completely negates her Q reset auto-attacks. She can't trade through your E. Activate E when she goes for Q resets and punish her.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E (Flawless Duet) — sidestep the stun blades. Your E helps absorb some of the damage.
Irelia.early_game: You hard counter her. Your E blocks her Q resets. Trade freely into her.
Irelia.mid_game: Continue dominating. She struggles to proc her kit against you.
Irelia.late_game: She tries to find teamfight value. Your ult is stronger in brawls.
Irelia.win_condition: E when she tries to Q reset. Her entire kit is built around auto-attacks that your E counters.
Irelia.positioning: Position so that when she dashes in, your E is ready to absorb her reset autos.

Riven.trading_pattern: Her shield (E) and your E interact interestingly. Time your E for after her E expires. Both can burst the other — mechanics decide this entirely.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven Q3 (knockup) — sidestep between her Q casts. Move sideways after each Q to make Q3 harder to land.
Riven.early_game: Even. Both can burst each other. Wait for her E shield to expire before going all-in.
Riven.mid_game: High skill matchup. Her full combo vs your E-extended fight.
Riven.late_game: Jax scales better into full items. Your ult stacks beat her burst.
Riven.win_condition: Bait her E shield before activating yours. Extended fights favor you once E is cycling.
Riven.scaling: Even early; Jax outscales late
Riven.positioning: Move sideways between her Q casts to avoid Q3 knockup.

Sett.trading_pattern: His grit generation from taking damage works against your auto-heavy trading pattern. E to dodge his W center then trade hard. Avoid letting him store grit.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W (Haymaker) — sidestep the center true damage when he throws it. Your E can help avoid some of his autos that build grit.
Sett.early_game: He out-trades early. Farm safely and let him come to you.
Sett.mid_game: More even with Trinity. E to dodge W and trade back.
Sett.late_game: Your ult stacks give great free stats for dueling him.
Sett.win_condition: Survive early and scale to Trinity. E his W center explosion. Extended fights favor you with ult up.
Sett.scaling: Sett slightly better early; Jax outscales
Sett.positioning: Sidestep the W center. Don't face-tank his grit storage autos.

Shen.trading_pattern: His W (dodge zone) blocks your basic attacks. Don't autoattack during W — Q-jump and then auto after W expires.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: Shen W (Spirit's Refuge) — stop autoattacking immediately when he activates it. Re-engage after the zone passes.
Shen.early_game: You win early. Bully him when W is on cooldown.
Shen.mid_game: His global ult makes him a map presence. Push and track ult.
Shen.late_game: Even in direct fight. His utility matters more in teamfights.
Shen.win_condition: Bully early and build big CS lead. Track his ult — push objectives when it's down.
Shen.positioning: Stop autoattacking when his W zone activates. Wait a beat then re-engage.

Sion.trading_pattern: Interrupt his Q charge by jumping onto him — you close the distance fast enough to stop it. Then E and trade freely. His immobility is a gift.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q (Decimating Smash) — Q-jump onto him to close the gap and interrupt the charge. Move perpendicular to avoid knockup direction.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult roam), Sunfire + Heartsteel HP stacks

Sion.early_game: Free lane. Bully him constantly. Deny HP stacks.
Sion.mid_game: He becomes tankier but your mixed damage still shreds.
Sion.late_game: His passive zombie form — stay away after killing him.
Sion.win_condition: Dominate lane, deny stacks, snowball. Jump in when he charges Q to interrupt it.
Sion.scaling: Jax wins throughout
Sion.positioning: Gap close with Q leap when he starts Q charge to interrupt at minimum range.

Trundle.trading_pattern: His Frozen Domain speed boost makes kiting him annoying. Trade hard before he sets up W zone. Your E lets you dodge his axe throw harassment.
Trundle.ability_to_dodge: Trundle Q (Chomp) — it reduces your damage output. E to block some of his autos during the chomp window.
Trundle.early_game: You win early. Bully him off wave. Don't fight inside his W zone.
Trundle.mid_game: His ult becomes massive. Avoid 1v1 at level 6+.
Trundle.late_game: His ult steals your armor/MR for 4 seconds — devastating when you have stacked resistances.
Trundle.win_condition: Get a big early lead. His ult stealing your ult passive stacks is brutal — win before items.
Trundle.scaling: Jax wins early; Trundle's ult is a major threat mid-late
Trundle.positioning: Don't fight inside his W zone. Bait him out of it before trading.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Your E blocks his crit autos during Counterstrike window. Trade frequently before his fury stacks and crits scale up. Never fight during his ult.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere E (Spinning Slash) — activate E when he dashes to block his follow-up autos.
Tryndamere.early_game: Win early. Deny fury generation. E to block crit autos.
Tryndamere.mid_game: Crits start threatening. Keep E cycling.
Tryndamere.late_game: His ult makes killing him impossible for 5 seconds. Wait it out. Never extend fights during his ult.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Build pre-6 lead. Wait out his ult before finishing. Your E blocks his crit autos — use it aggressively.
Tryndamere.scaling: Jax wins early; even mid; Tryndamere better sidelane late crits
Tryndamere.positioning: Activate E when he crit-autos you to reduce the burst.

Urgot.trading_pattern: His shotgun knees shred your E-blocked trades. Even with E up, his W passive damages you per auto. Avoid prolonged trades entirely.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot W — when he activates it, kite backwards. His knees fire during W and E blocks only basic attacks.
Urgot.early_game: He wins early hard. Farm under tower.
Urgot.late_game: His ult execute is terrifying. Group to deny isolated ult targets.
Urgot.win_condition: Need jungle help. Roam to create leads elsewhere. Avoid 1v1 in side lane.
Urgot.positioning: Stay outside his W knee range. Don't stand directly in front of his knee arc.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Kill his ghouls with E — your E dodge blocks their basic attacks. Without ghouls he's significantly weaker. Force fights when he has no mist charges.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick E (Mourning Mist) — sidestep the mark. E blocks ghoul auto-attacks just like regular ones.
Yorick.early_game: Win early. Kill ghouls with E. Deny mist charges.
Yorick.mid_game: Maiden + Hullbreaker makes him dangerous. Match his split push.
Yorick.late_game: Your ult passive stacks make you tankier for extended 1v1s. Match his pressure.
Yorick.win_condition: Deny Maiden charges. Kill ghouls with E auto-attack blocking. Win before his Hullbreaker spike.
Yorick.scaling: Jax wins early; Yorick better split pusher with Maiden
Yorick.positioning: E to block ghoul autos. Kill ghouls first to remove his damage advantage.

Vayne.trading_pattern: Kill her early before Guinsoo's. Her Silver Bolts true damage ignores your E dodge — she wins extended trades. Short all-ins only.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — never stand near walls. Q-leap toward her from angles she can't condemn toward terrain.
Vayne.early_game: All-in her early before items. She can't escape your Q leap.
Vayne.mid_game: Her Silver Bolts melt you in extended fights. Quick all-ins only.
Vayne.late_game: True damage counters your ult passive stats. Avoid 1v1s. Burst her in teamfights.
Vayne.win_condition: Kill her pre-Guinsoo's. Take Ignite. Never fight extended after her first item.
Vayne.scaling: Jax wins very early; Vayne hard outscales
Vayne.positioning: Don't stand near walls. Approach from angles she can't condemn toward terrain.

Varus.trading_pattern: He pokes from extreme range before your Q leap reaches him. Engage when his Q is charging. His blight stacks + W explode hurt in extended fights.
Varus.ability_to_dodge: Varus Q (Piercing Arrow) — dash sideways when he's charging. It's a slow projectile at full charge.
Varus.early_game: He pokes you down safely. Farm under tower. Call for jungle.
Varus.mid_game: His W true damage hurts through E. Quick trades only.
Varus.late_game: His ult chains your whole team. Approach from flanks.
Varus.win_condition: Jungle pressure needed. Engage when his Q charges. Build speed to gap close.
Varus.scaling: Varus outscales via range and true damage
Varus.positioning: Use minion wave as cover to approach. Leap in when Q is committed.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Wind Wall doesn't block your Q leap or E. But his crits hurt during your E window if he has them. Trade when his E is on cooldown.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo Q3 tornado — sidestep when his sword glows. Your Q leap gives mobility to dodge.
Yasuo.early_game: Fairly even. Both can trade well. E blocks his auto attacks in his combo.
Yasuo.mid_game: His crits compete with your kit. E to block crit autos.
Yasuo.late_game: Your ult passive gives free stats that his kit doesn't match. You outscale.
Yasuo.win_condition: E to block his crit autos. Your Q leap goes through Wind Wall. Fight when his E is down.
Yasuo.scaling: Even early; Jax outscales past Trinity
Yasuo.positioning: E to block crit window. Q-leap doesn't interact with Wind Wall.

Yone.trading_pattern: When he uses E, trade during his forward dash then disengage before his return. E blocks his return auto-attack damage.
Yone.ability_to_dodge: Yone E (Soul Unbound) — when he dashes, position away from where his body stands so his return misses.
Yone.early_game: Even early. Trade when he commits E. Back off before his body returns.
Yone.mid_game: His damage ramps significantly. E blocks his auto-attack damage during return.
Yone.late_game: Your ult stacks win in extended fights.
Yone.win_condition: Trade during his E forward dash, back off before his return. E to block return phase autos.
Yone.scaling: Even; Jax slightly better in extended fights
Yone.positioning: Move away from his body position when he E-dashes so his return body misses.

Kayle.trading_pattern: Bully her pre-11. Your Q leap closes distance and E blocks her auto-attacks. Kill her before she gets ranged.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle E (Starfire Spellblade) — E blocks her empowered auto. Engage immediately after she procs it on a minion.
Kayle.early_game: Dominate her. She can't fight you early. Force kills at levels 2-4.
Kayle.mid_game: She gains progressive range. Keep pressure before 11.
Kayle.late_game: At 11 you can't reach her effectively. Group or play for flanks.
Kayle.win_condition: Kill her before level 6 and snowball. At 11 she becomes uncatchable.
Kayle.scaling: Jax wins early; Kayle wins past level 11
Kayle.positioning: Stay on top of her at all times pre-11. Deny all CS with aggression.

Jayce.trading_pattern: He out-ranges you in cannon form. Force all-ins when he's in hammer form. Q-leap at him aggressively when he switches to hammer.
Jayce.ability_to_dodge: Jayce Q (Shock Blast) — sidestep the arc in cannon form. In hammer, dodge his knockback with E activation.
Jayce.early_game: He pokes you down from safe range. Farm and wait for hammer form windows.
Jayce.mid_game: Even power at Trinity. Force hammer form fights.
Jayce.late_game: You outscale him. Your ult passive stats beat his kit.
Jayce.win_condition: Force fights in his hammer form. Q-leap aggressively when he melee-engages. Avoid fighting in cannon range.
Jayce.positioning: Hug minions to reduce his cannon Q poke. Leap in when he's in hammer form.

Darius.trading_pattern: Poke him from cannon form to deny CS. If he ever walks up into hammer range, he wins the all-in. Stay ranged and never let him pull you with E.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius E (Apprehend) — always stay outside his grab range. In hammer form, never stand still near him.
Darius.early_game: Poke him with cannon Q-E combo and back off. Never fight melee unless you're way ahead.
Darius.mid_game: His Stridebreaker lets him close your gap. Keep ranged poke and avoid melee form.
Darius.late_game: Avoid side lane duels. Group and use cannon poke in teamfights.
Darius.win_condition: Stay in cannon form, poke from max range, never let him land E. Take Teleport to avoid his tower dive threat.
Darius.scaling: Darius wins melee; Jayce wins at range — Darius wins overall because he forces melee
Darius.positioning: Stay outside his E hook range at all times. Never walk forward into melee range in cannon form.

Garen.trading_pattern: Poke him constantly from cannon form. He can't close the gap on you safely. His Q silence is only dangerous in melee range which you avoid.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q — never walk into melee range to trigger it. Cannon form range means this is largely irrelevant.
Garen.early_game: Free lane. Poke him off every CS attempt. He can't respond from melee range.
Garen.mid_game: Continue to dominate. Build lead and roam.
Garen.late_game: He can catch you in teamfights with W. Avoid getting slowed into melee range.
Garen.win_condition: Maintain cannon range. Never let him get within Q range. Build big CS lead and roam.
Garen.scaling: Jayce wins throughout from range
Garen.positioning: Stay at max cannon Q range. Back off when he tries to close gap.

Fiora.trading_pattern: She can Riposte your cannon Q — bait W before using Q. If she gets into melee range she wins. Her Vitals at close range combined with your melee form weakness is brutal.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W (Riposte) — throw a basic attack to bait it, then commit Q. Never use cannon Q as the first action when her W is available.
Fiora.early_game: She can't all-in easily from range. Maintain cannon form. Danger starts when she forces melee.
Fiora.mid_game: She outduels you in melee. Shove and roam. Never 1v1 in side lane post-level 6.
Fiora.late_game: She hard outduels you. Group and play for teamfight poke.
Fiora.win_condition: Bait her W before using Q. Never fight melee. Cannon poke + Ignite for kill pressure.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora wins all duels; Jayce better in ranged harass
Fiora.positioning: Stay in cannon form. If she gaps close, immediately switch to hammer to at least trade effectively.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Poke him from cannon while sidestepping his Q. When he commits Q, punish with cannon Q-E shot. Switch to hammer only if he's very low.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — sidestep the outer edges. Poke with cannon Q during his Q animation when he can't reposition.
Aatrox.early_game: You win the poke war. Dodge his Q with cannon range advantage.
Aatrox.mid_game: He starts fighting even with Goredrinker. Match his shove and roam.
Aatrox.late_game: His healing makes extended fights difficult. Group for teamfights.
Aatrox.win_condition: Win the poke war early. Avoid letting him all-in at melee range.
Aatrox.scaling: Jayce wins early; even mid; Aatrox better sustained fighter
Aatrox.positioning: Dodge his Q hitboxes while maintaining cannon poke range.

Camille.trading_pattern: Poke from cannon but she can E through walls onto you. She wins all melee engagements. Never stay near walls.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E (Hookshot) — stay center of lane away from walls. She can't get clean angles without wall geometry.
Camille.early_game: Poke from cannon. When she E's onto you, switch to hammer for the fight — cannon form melee is weaker.
Camille.mid_game: She outduels in melee. Shove and roam.
Camille.late_game: She dives backline. Protect yours. Your cannon poke disrupts her setup.
Camille.win_condition: Stay center of lane. Poke from range. Roam and create leads elsewhere.
Camille.scaling: Camille outscales in duels; Jayce better from range
Camille.positioning: Never stand near walls. Center of lane denies her Hookshot angles.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Poke her hard from range pre-6. She needs passive stacks on minions to function — your cannon poke denies her CS and stack generation.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E (Flawless Duet) — sidestep the stun blades. She needs to walk up to throw them.
Irelia.early_game: Poke her heavily. She can't get passive stacks under your harassment.
Irelia.mid_game: She reaches you with Trinity + E. Match her shove and avoid isolated 1v1s.
Irelia.late_game: She outduels you in melee. Group for poke-based teamfights.
Irelia.win_condition: Deny her passive stacks and CS early. Build a big lead and roam.
Irelia.scaling: Jayce wins early; Irelia outscales in direct duels
Irelia.positioning: Stay at cannon poke range. Don't let her get close enough to proc passive stacks on you.

Jax.trading_pattern: Poke from cannon pre-6. At level 6 his ult passive gives him too many free stats to bully. He'll close the gap and win melee fights every time.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E (Counterstrike) — he can't block cannon Q or hammer Q. Poke while his E is on cooldown.
Jax.early_game: Poke him heavily pre-6. He's vulnerable before his ult.
Jax.mid_game: He becomes a dueling monster. Avoid 1v1 in side lane.
Jax.late_game: He hard outscales your dueling. Group and play for poke.
Jax.win_condition: Build a big pre-6 lead from cannon poke. After that avoid direct duels.
Jax.scaling: Jayce wins early; Jax hard outscales
Jax.positioning: Poke from max cannon range. Don't let him close gap for free.

Malphite.trading_pattern: Poke him endlessly with cannon Q. His armor reduces some damage but your Q cooldown is short enough to out-harass him. His mana depletes fast.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite Q (Seismic Shard) — sidestep it. Movement speed slow is his only harassment tool.
Malphite.early_game: Drain his mana with continuous poke. He can't sustain under harassment.
Malphite.mid_game: His armor reduces your effectiveness. Roam when ahead.
Malphite.late_game: His ult engage is game-winning. Avoid being caught with W active during his ult.
Malphite.win_condition: Drain mana early and build a big lead. His armor matters less with Black Cleaver.
Malphite.scaling: Jayce wins early; Malphite better teamfight initiator
Malphite.positioning: Maintain cannon poke range. Sidestep his Q whenever he walks up.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: Poke from cannon to prevent his passive shield from regenerating. When he ults you, switch to hammer and fight — you can't escape anyway.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser R (Realm of Death) — you're trapped. Switch to hammer form and fight aggressively inside. Cannon form is actually better for burst.
Mordekaiser.early_game: Poke his shield down constantly. His melee range means you can control the lane.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: His ult isolated is a problem. Fight inside realm with hammer burst.
Mordekaiser.late_game: Even in teamfights. Your cannon poke vs his ult threat.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Poke his shield down. Inside his ult, use cannon E shot to burst, then switch to hammer for melee damage.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Even; Mordekaiser better in 1v1 ult scenarios
Mordekaiser.positioning: Don't fight inside his W golden ring zone. Poke from outside it.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Deny every Q stack with cannon harass. He cannot farm safely under your poke. His Wither is dangerous but you can maintain distance in cannon.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W (Wither) — switch to cannon form when he Withers you. The slow matters more in hammer melee range.
Nasus.early_game: Absolutely bully him. Deny every Q stack. Free lane.
Nasus.mid_game: He becomes tankier with stacks. Roam to use your lead.
Nasus.late_game: 500 stacks — avoid 1v1s. Group for teamfights.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny stacks aggressively. Win before 400 stacks. Your poke makes his laning life terrible.
Nasus.scaling: Jayce wins early-mid; Nasus threatens late with stacks
Nasus.positioning: Stand between him and CS in cannon poke range constantly.

Olaf.trading_pattern: Poke from cannon. His ult makes him immune to CC but not to your damage. In hammer form your knockback can still apply even when he ults.
Olaf.ability_to_dodge: Olaf Q (Undertow) — dodge the axe. Punish his retrieve walk with cannon Q.
Olaf.early_game: Even poke war. Dodge his axes from cannon range.
Olaf.mid_game: His ult lets him ignore your hammer knockback CC. He closes the gap and wins melee.
Olaf.late_game: He's very strong in side lane. Group for teamfight poke.
Olaf.win_condition: Poke from range. Avoid melee form when his ult is active. Your knockback works even through his ult.
Olaf.scaling: Even early; Olaf better mid-late in side lane
Olaf.positioning: Cannon range poke. Dodge axe and punish retrieve walk.

Renekton.trading_pattern: Poke him from cannon before he can E onto you. His E closes the gap fast — always have hammer form ready to respond when he gaps close.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton E (Slice and Dice) — back off when he starts sprinting. Switch to hammer when he E's onto you for the melee fight.
Renekton.early_game: Poke him pre-3. After level 3 he can E onto you — have hammer form swap ready.
Renekton.mid_game: He falls off mid-game relative to early. Match shove and roam.
Renekton.late_game: His late game isn't impressive. Group for teamfights.
Renekton.win_condition: Win the poke war early. When he E's in, hammer form swap and trade back — his burst is front-loaded.
Renekton.scaling: Jayce wins early; even mid; Jayce better teamfight poke
Renekton.positioning: Back off when he begins E approach. Cannon poke while he's outside E range.

Riven.trading_pattern: Her mobility makes cannon poke harder. She can windwall your Q with her abilities. When she gaps close, immediately hammer form and trade — her shield expires fast.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven E (Valor shield) — bait it before hammer swapping. She can dash through your Q arc.
Riven.early_game: Even. Poke from cannon when she CS's. She can dash your projectiles.
Riven.mid_game: She outduels in melee. Shove and roam.
Riven.late_game: Her execute ult kills your carries. Group and protect backline.
Riven.win_condition: Poke and roam. Avoid 1v1 duels in side lane. Hammer burst + cannon follow-up on kills.
Riven.scaling: Even early; Riven outscales in melee duels
Riven.positioning: Sidestep during her Q casts. Cannon form when she's outside E range.

Sett.trading_pattern: Poke him constantly. His grit accumulation requires taking damage — reduce his grit by not giving him free autos. Punish his W wind-up with cannon Q.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W (Haymaker) — when he winds up, cannon Q him to poke or sidestep the center explosion.
Sett.early_game: Poke him freely. He can't respond to cannon harass.
Sett.mid_game: He becomes tankier. Keep shove pressure and roam.
Sett.late_game: His ult grabs are scary. Peel for your team and poke from cannon range.
Sett.win_condition: Win lane with poke lead. Avoid his W grit center in fights.
Sett.positioning: Maintain cannon poke range. Never let him auto-attack you to build grit.

Shen.trading_pattern: Poke him down in cannon form. His W blocks your autos but not cannon Q shots. Push and deny his global ult roam value.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: Shen W (Spirit's Refuge) — cannon Q still hits during his W dodge zone. Keep poking with Q even in his W.
Shen.early_game: Free poke lane. His W only blocks autos, not your cannon Q.
Shen.mid_game: His ult changes fights on other lanes. Push fast and counter with Teleport.
Shen.late_game: His ult is incredible for teamfights. Track its cooldown.
Shen.win_condition: Poke him heavily and build CS lead. Track ult cooldown — push objectives when it's down.
Shen.positioning: Maintain cannon poke angle. His W only blocks autos — cannon Q hits through it.

Sion.trading_pattern: His Q charge can be interrupted by your hammer knockback. Bait his Q then hammer E knockback. But he's tanky and you run out of burst quickly.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q (Decimating Smash) — move perpendicular or hammer E knockback him to interrupt charge.
Sion.early_game: He zones you off cannon poke with Q threat. Tricky early.
Sion.mid_game: His HP scaling makes your damage less impactful. Roam.
Sion.late_game: His HP becomes enormous. Serylda's Grudge helps shred him.
Sion.win_condition: Interrupt Q with hammer knockback. Build Serylda's Grudge for armor shred. Roam aggressively.
Sion.scaling: Even; Sion scales tankier which reduces Jayce's poke effectiveness
Sion.positioning: Stay at cannon range. Hammer knockback to interrupt Q charges when he walks up.

Trundle.trading_pattern: Poke from cannon constantly. He can't respond to ranged harass. Only dangerous if he gets his Frozen Domain close to you.
Trundle.ability_to_dodge: Trundle E (Pillar of Ice) — sidestep it. Don't let it trap you in his W domain.
Trundle.early_game: Free poke lane. He can't close gap on you.
Trundle.mid_game: His ult stealing your AD is somewhat impactful on a poke champion. Avoid 1v1 at 6.
Trundle.late_game: Your poke is still relevant. Group for objectives.
Trundle.win_condition: Win poke war early and build CS lead. Avoid extended 1v1 fights where his ult steals AD.
Trundle.scaling: Jayce wins throughout; slight Trundle advantage in 1v1 ult scenario
Trundle.positioning: Stay at cannon range. Sidestep his Pillar to avoid getting trapped in W zone.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Poke him from cannon to deny fury build-up. If he closes gap, hammer form knockback to reset distance. Never fight during his ult.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere E (Spinning Slash) — switch to hammer form when he closes. Knockback him back.
Tryndamere.early_game: Poke him off fury generation. He can't farm safely under your harass.
Tryndamere.mid_game: Crits start hurting if he gets close. Hammer knockback resets distance.
Tryndamere.late_game: His ult makes kills hard. Group and avoid 1v1 side lane.
Tryndamere.win_condition: Deny fury pre-6. Hammer knockback resets distance when he closes. Never commit during his ult.
Tryndamere.scaling: Jayce wins early; Tryndamere better sidelane late
Tryndamere.positioning: Cannon poke at range. When he closes gap, hammer knockback and switch back to cannon.

Urgot.trading_pattern: He out-ranges you in a sense — his W passive shoots at range and deals more total damage than your cannon poke. Hard matchup at all stages.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot W (Purge) — kite backwards. His shotgun knees fire in front of him — angle to reduce their proc chance.
Urgot.early_game: Tough. He wins the poke war with his W passive. Farm defensively.
Urgot.mid_game: He dominates 1v1. Roam to create leads elsewhere.
Urgot.late_game: His ult is game-changing. Group to deny isolated targets.
Urgot.win_condition: Need jungle help. Roam to create leads. You can't win this lane solo.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot outscales in duels; Jayce better in team poke
Urgot.positioning: Angle to reduce number of his knees that proc on you. Don't stand directly in front of him.

Vayne.trading_pattern: Kill her before Guinsoo's. Your cannon Q burst can chunk her hard early. After Guinsoo's her Silver Bolts true damage negates your poke lead.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — never stand near walls. She'll condemn you into walls during her stealth.
Vayne.early_game: All-in her hard early. Cannon burst can chunk her significantly.
Vayne.mid_game: Her Silver Bolts reduce your poke lead. Quick trades.
Vayne.late_game: True damage through your armor is brutal. Group and avoid 1v1.
Vayne.win_condition: Win the early lane decisively. Roam after getting a kill. Avoid isolated 1v1s after her first item.
Vayne.scaling: Jayce wins very early; Vayne hard outscales
Vayne.positioning: Stay center of lane. Never near walls.

Varus.trading_pattern: Both poke each other from range. Your cannon Q vs his Q + W blight. You win in short burst combos, he wins if blight stacks proc. Dodge his Q charges.
Varus.ability_to_dodge: Varus Q (Piercing Arrow) — sidestep the slow projectile. Move laterally when he's charging full Q.
Varus.early_game: Even poke war. Your Q burst is stronger per shot, his W blight stacks compound.
Varus.mid_game: His W blight true damage becomes threatening. Short trades.
Varus.late_game: His ult chains your team. Approach from flanks.
Varus.win_condition: Win the poke war pre-items. Your Q does more damage per hit. Dodge his Q with mobility.
Varus.positioning: Sidestep his Q throws. Keep cannon Q angle available at all times.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Cannon Q poke shreds him and his ghouls. Kill ghouls from range to deny mist charges. He has no reliable way to stick to you.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick E (Mourning Mist) — sidestep the slow projectile. Being marked causes Maiden focus.
Yorick.early_game: Free lane. Poke him and his ghouls constantly. Deny mist charges.
Yorick.mid_game: Maiden + Hullbreaker makes him a split push threat. Match his push.
Yorick.late_game: He'll split while you group. Decide which is more valuable.
Yorick.win_condition: Deny Maiden by destroying ghouls with cannon Q. Build huge CS lead and roam.
Yorick.scaling: Jayce wins early; Yorick outscales in split push
Yorick.positioning: Stay at cannon range. Kill ghouls with Q to deny mist charges.

Talon.trading_pattern: Talon has early mobility and burst. Play defensive and respect his all-in threat.
Talon.ability_to_dodge: Talon Q (Noxian Diplomacy) — dodge by stepping back or to the side.
Talon.early_game: Play very defensively. Talon dominates early. Q the wave for safety.
Talon.mid_game: Use your R to escape his roams. Scale with team.
Talon.late_game: You outscale significantly. Survive early to reach your power spike.
Talon.win_condition: Survive early game without falling behind. Outscale in late teamfights.
Talon.scaling: Kassadin significantly outscales — his late game damage is oppressive
Talon.positioning: Stay at maximum distance. Farm defensively with Q.
