power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate
power_spikes.enemy: Same as you — scaling is identical

Miss Fortune.early_game: Trade carefully with Q bounces. Position well.
Miss Fortune.mid_game: Both scale identically. Team coordination determines advantage.
Miss Fortune.late_game: Whoever lands ultimate first wins fights.
Miss Fortune.win_condition: Better positioning, smarter ultimate usage, team fight execution.
Miss Fortune.scaling: Perfectly even — identical in every way
Miss Fortune.positioning: Mirror opponent's spacing. Time ultimate better than them.

Nilah.trading_pattern: Nilah's healing and burst beat Miss Fortune's poke. Respect her all-in.
Nilah.ability_to_dodge: Nilah E (Splitter) — dodge by moving away from dash, position away from walls.
Nilah.early_game: Play extremely safe. Don't let her get close.
Nilah.mid_game: Miss Fortune catches up. Group and leverage numbers.
Nilah.late_game: Miss Fortune outscales utility. Use ultimate to control fights.
Nilah.win_condition: Survive early aggression, scale with team, leverage ultimate.
Nilah.scaling: Miss Fortune outscales — Nilah early champion without lead
Nilah.positioning: Keep maximum distance. Stay near teammates always.

Samira.trading_pattern: Samira's style stacking burst beats Miss Fortune's poke. Play safe.
Samira.ability_to_dodge: Samira R (Inferno Trigger) — dodge by moving out of range, use E slow.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 2-3 with style stacking, completed Collector

Samira.early_game: Play safe and don't let her all-in. Poke from distance.
Samira.mid_game: Miss Fortune catches up. Group and prevent her snowball.
Samira.late_game: Miss Fortune outscales. Use ultimate to control fights.
Samira.win_condition: Survive early, scale with team, leverage ultimate.
Samira.scaling: Miss Fortune outscales — Samira early champion
Samira.positioning: Keep maximum distance. Stay near teammates.

Sivir.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune's poke beats Sivir. Use Q bounces to pressure.
Sivir.ability_to_dodge: Sivir E (Spell Shield) — bait shield with autos, use E slow wisely.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with ultimate engage, completed RFC

Sivir.early_game: Pressure with poke. She can't match your trading.
Sivir.mid_game: Both scale well. Miss Fortune maintains advantage.
Sivir.late_game: Both strong. Whoever lands ultimate first controls fights.
Sivir.win_condition: Maintain early pressure, scale with utility, land ultimate well.
Sivir.scaling: Even — Both scale well, ultimate usage matters
Sivir.positioning: Keep distance and poke. Position for ultimate.

Tristana.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune's poke dominates but Tristana's E bomb scales. Maintain pressure.
Tristana.ability_to_dodge: Tristana E (Explosive Charge) — position to prevent bomb stacking.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate
power_spikes.enemy: Level 2-3 with E bomb, early attack speed

Tristana.early_game: Dominate with poke. Don't let her stack bombs.
Tristana.mid_game: She scales up. Miss Fortune maintains pressure.
Tristana.late_game: She outscales raw damage. Use ultimate to control.
Tristana.win_condition: Pressure early, maintain advantage, leverage ultimate.
Tristana.scaling: Tristana outscales — more damage late but MF wins early
Tristana.positioning: Keep distance to prevent E stacking. Position for ultimate.

Twitch.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune dominates early while Twitch is weak. Snowball.
Twitch.ability_to_dodge: Twitch R (Spray and Pray) — dodge by moving perpendicular, use cover.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with ultimate spray, completed IE

Twitch.early_game: Completely dominate. Pressure hard and deny farm.
Twitch.mid_game: He scales up. Miss Fortune maintains pressure.
Twitch.late_game: He outscales raw damage. Use ultimate to control fights.
Twitch.win_condition: Snowball early, maintain pressure, leverage ultimate late.
Twitch.positioning: Poke early aggressively. Late game, dodge his R spray.

Varus.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune's poke beats Varus early. Use Q bounces.
Varus.ability_to_dodge: Varus E (Hail of Arrows) — stay spread, move between barrages.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with true damage, completed Manamune

Varus.early_game: Pressure with constant poke. Don't let him free farm.
Varus.mid_game: Both scale. Miss Fortune maintains edge through utility.
Varus.late_game: He outscales with true damage. Use ultimate to control.
Varus.win_condition: Pressure early, maintain advantage, leverage ultimate.
Varus.scaling: Varus slightly favored — true damage scales well
Varus.positioning: Maintain distance and poke. Avoid his stun.

Vayne.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune dominates Vayne completely. Pressure hard early.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — position away from walls, use E slow.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with true damage, completed Sheen/Trinity

Vayne.early_game: Completely dominate with poke. She can't match your range.
Vayne.mid_game: Build lead and leverage numbers. She scales but you're ahead.
Vayne.late_game: Her true damage is scary but you maintain pressure. Use ultimate.
Vayne.win_condition: Leverage early range dominance to build lead, close game.
Vayne.scaling: Even late game but MF's early advantage wins — build and finish
Vayne.positioning: Keep maximum distance early. Position away from walls.

Xayah.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune's poke beats Xayah. Use Q bounces to pressure.
Xayah.ability_to_dodge: Xayah R (Featherstorm) — dodge by moving out of animation range.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with ultimate evasion, completed IE

Xayah.early_game: Pressure with poke. She can't close distance early.
Xayah.mid_game: Both scale well. Miss Fortune maintains slight advantage.
Xayah.late_game: Both strong with ultimates. Positioning determines fights.
Xayah.win_condition: Maintain early pressure, scale with utility, land ultimate.
Xayah.scaling: Even — Both scale similarly, ultimate usage matters
Xayah.positioning: Keep distance and poke. Position for ultimate safely.

Zeri.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune's poke beats Zeri's early weakness. Pressure hard.
Zeri.ability_to_dodge: Zeri E (Sparkpack) — dodge by moving perpendicular, use E slow.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate

Zeri.early_game: Dominate with poke. Don't let her all-in.
Zeri.mid_game: Miss Fortune maintains advantage as Zeri struggles.
Zeri.late_game: Zeri falls off hard. You scale better.
Zeri.win_condition: Dominate early, snowball, close out game.
Zeri.scaling: Miss Fortune heavily outscales — Zeri early champion
Zeri.positioning: Keep distance and poke. Use E slow to prevent gap close.

Senna.trading_pattern: Senna out-ranges and out-pokes Miss Fortune. Play safe.
Senna.ability_to_dodge: Senna Q (Piercing Darkness) — stay spread, don't get marked.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 1-3 with poke, any completed item

Senna.early_game: Senna has significant advantage. Play safe and poke when safe.
Senna.mid_game: Miss Fortune catches up slightly but she maintains range.
Senna.late_game: Senna outscales. Use ultimate to control fights.
Senna.win_condition: Survive early poke, scale with team, leverage ultimate.
Senna.scaling: Senna outscales — longer range and utility
Senna.positioning: Stay spread to reduce her poke. Keep distance.

Seraphine.trading_pattern: Seraphine out-ranges and roots dangerously. Play safe.
Seraphine.ability_to_dodge: Seraphine E (Surround) — dodge by moving perpendicular.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with ultimate damage, completed Shard items

Seraphine.early_game: Play safe against her poke and root. Respect range.
Seraphine.mid_game: Miss Fortune catches up. Group and leverage numbers.
Seraphine.late_game: Both have powerful ultimates. Proper positioning wins.
Seraphine.win_condition: Survive early, scale with team, leverage ultimate.
Seraphine.scaling: Even — Both scale similarly with ultimates
Seraphine.positioning: Position behind cover to block her root. Stay spread.

Smolder.trading_pattern: Miss Fortune's poke beats Smolder early. Maintain pressure.
Smolder.ability_to_dodge: Smolder E (Scorched Earth) — dodge by moving out of radius.

power_spikes.you: Level 1-2 with Q poke, Level 6 with ultimate

Smolder.early_game: Pressure with poke. Don't get caught in flames.
Smolder.mid_game: Both scale similarly. Miss Fortune maintains slight edge.
Smolder.late_game: Both strong. Whoever positions better wins.
Smolder.win_condition: Maintain pressure, scale with team, land ultimate well.
Smolder.scaling: Even — Both scale similarly, ultimate usage matters
Smolder.positioning: Keep distance from his flames. Position for ultimate.

Cassiopeia.trading_pattern: She outranges and outdamages you early. Avoid extended trades before level 3. Use E to gap close when she's overextended.
Cassiopeia.ability_to_dodge: Her Q poison — it applies Conqueror stacks and slows. Back away from poison clouds and position to the side.

power_spikes.you: Level 3 (E all-in potential), Liandry's Torment second item
power_spikes.enemy: Level 2 (Q+W poke spike), Rylai's Crystal Scepter (kite tool)

Cassiopeia.early_game: Play around her Q cooldown. She has weak mobility pre-6. When her Q is down, look for E engages on her.
Cassiopeia.mid_game: Shove and roam. If she gets Rylai's, it becomes much harder to catch her. Play around R windows.
Cassiopeia.late_game: In teamfights, go around the team to catch her. Never let her land her full combo. One R pull wins fights.
Cassiopeia.win_condition: Catch her overextended before she gets tankier. Avoid fighting in her poison clouds. Win early to prevent her scaling advantage.
Cassiopeia.scaling: Cassiopeia scales harder — she outranges and kites indefinitely. Win early or lose value.
Cassiopeia.positioning: Stay behind minions to reduce Q damage. Avoid the center of fights where she can W fear.

Galio.trading_pattern: Galio outdamages early with Q+E burst. Short trades lose to his cooldowns. Trade when E is down.
Galio.ability_to_dodge: His E taunt — dodge by running perpendicular to his charge. Never stand in a line with him at 2+ stacks of Comet Aegis.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (R engage + W taunt combo), Hollow Radiance

Galio.early_game: Don't fight his E-W combo. He's tanky but runs out of mana. Let him shove and gank early.
Galio.mid_game: Post-6, track his ult. He's powerful in grouped fights but weak in side lanes. Pressure elsewhere.
Galio.late_game: In teamfights, stay spread so his R isn't impactful. Your 1v1 is stronger — split and force him to match.
Galio.win_condition: Get a lead early, roam with ult. Split push when he groups. Don't let him dictate teamfight positioning.
Galio.scaling: Galio scales better — he has better teamfight utility. Win before midgame.
Galio.positioning: Stay far from minion clumps to avoid E taunt hitbox. Hug walls to limit his charge angles.

Naafiri.trading_pattern: Naafiri has sustained poke with Q and threaded E. She outdamages short trades. Extended trades favor you slightly post-3.
Naafiri.ability_to_dodge: Her E threading — it splits after hitting a target. Back away when you see it lock on. Don't fight when she has Passive stacked.
Naafiri.early_game: Play safe into her poke spam. Minion waves block some threading. At level 3, if you land E, you win the trade.
Naafiri.mid_game: Shove and roam. Naafiri needs to be mobile. Pin her into side lanes where she has less escape.
Naafiri.late_game: 1v1 is in your favor if she's isolated. Protect squishy carries in teamfights from her threading.
Naafiri.win_condition: Survive poke, hit level 3 E, and trade when her passive is down. Roam to generate pressure elsewhere.
Naafiri.scaling: Even with different win conditions. You win drawn 1v1s; she wins skirmishes.
Naafiri.positioning: Stay behind minion lines to block threading. Space from walls to limit her rotation paths.

Rumble.trading_pattern: Rumble wins early with Overheat Q. Long range, high damage. Trade when Overheat is on cooldown.
Rumble.ability_to_dodge: His Q harpoon — move sideways. Overheat window is your trade time.
Rumble.early_game: Play safe into Overheat spam. Trade when Q is down. Farm and wait for scaling window.
Rumble.mid_game: Teamfight instead of 1v1. His E-Ult is devastating grouped. Spread out.
Rumble.late_game: He outscales through Overheat playstyle. Stick to teamfights with good positioning.
Rumble.win_condition: Survive early, teamfight grouped. Win through team coordination, not raw dueling.
Rumble.scaling: Rumble outscales through Overheat spam. You need teamfights.
Rumble.positioning: Space from clumps for E-Ult positioning. Trade when Overheat is down.

Darius.trading_pattern: Darius wins extended trades at close range. Short trades favor you slightly. Avoid his Q outer ring entirely.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: His Q bleed — walk at him or away from the outer ring. Standing in the ring is a death sentence.
Darius.early_game: Don't fight in his Q spin. Run at him to avoid outer ring damage. Trade short at level 1-2.
Darius.mid_game: Your ult is safer in teamfights. Darius needs to land R to reset. Kite away and ult into him.
Darius.late_game: He scales better into full builds. Stick to fights where ult resets are valuable. Don't 1v1 him if ahead.
Darius.win_condition: Avoid his Q outer ring, build HP. Use ult to lock him down. Win through roaming and map pressure.
Darius.scaling: Darius outscales mid-to-late. Win early, roam, don't let him stack passive.
Darius.positioning: Walk at him to deny Q outer ring. Don't extend into the lane without vision.

Garen.trading_pattern: Garen Q silence shuts down your combo. Trade short before he gets Q. After he Q's, you win the trade back.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: His Q silence — when you see him wind up, back away immediately. Silence prevents E usage completely.
Garen.early_game: He's stronger in short trades because of Q silence. Play around minions for poke. Trade after he uses Q.
Garen.mid_game: He needs to chase with W. Your E pull outranges his engage. In teamfights, position to ult his backline.
Garen.late_game: Garen outscales — Sunfire + Judgment combo is oppressive. Win through teamfight ult impact, not drawn fights.
Garen.win_condition: Trade after his Q is down. Shove and roam with ult. Never sit in his Judgment radius in teamfights.
Garen.scaling: Garen slightly ahead — he has better AOE teamfight. You scale through ult utility.
Garen.positioning: Hug minions to reduce distance Garen can Q you from. Back away when Q winds up.

Fiora.trading_pattern: Fiora parries your burst with W. Play around it by faking E. Extended trades favor her due to Vitals.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Her Riposte (W) — bait it with a fake E, then E for real. She blocks your damage if you're predictable.
Fiora.early_game: Don't spam E — she'll Riposte it. Play around minions. She can dash away with Q, so short trades are risky.
Fiora.mid_game: She duels better but ult groups fights. In teamfights with your team, you win. Isolated 1v1s lose to Vitals.
Fiora.late_game: She's an assassin in late game. Protect your team in grouped fights. Your tankiness wins fights with allies.
Fiora.win_condition: Team fight only. Never isolated duel. Ult into enemy team. Win through teamfight 5v5.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora scales better — she's a split push god late game. Stick to teamfights.
Fiora.positioning: Juke her Q with sideways movement. Don't overextend without vision for jungle.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Aatrox Q has range and healing. Short trades favor him. After he whiffs a Q swing, trade back hard.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: His Q swings — they have 3 charges with healing on hit. Dodge by moving perpendicular to his swings.
Aatrox.early_game: Let him push. He's stronger early. When his Q is on cooldown, look for E pulls. Play around minions for spacing.
Aatrox.mid_game: Aatrox teamfights well with his E range. Your ult is better in chaotic fights. Focus positioning over raw damage.
Aatrox.late_game: He scales through items. Your teamfight ult is stronger. Pin him down in team fights.
Aatrox.win_condition: Farm safely, look for E pulls after his Q cooldown. Win through teamfights, not side lane duels.
Aatrox.scaling: Even — both scale well. Aatrox has better healing; you have better teamfight lockdown.
Aatrox.positioning: Space far from minion clumps to avoid his full Q combo. Don't over-extend toward river.

Illaoi.trading_pattern: Illaoi deals massive single-target damage with tentacles. Short trades go to her. Play around tentacle spawns.
Illaoi.ability_to_dodge: Her E spirit grab — dodge by moving sideways. If hit, you fight at massive disadvantage with tentacle damage.
Illaoi.early_game: Play around tentacle spawns. They tick damage. After she hits E, back away and disengage completely.
Illaoi.mid_game: Avoid her E in fights. If you're caught, ult back and disengage. Teamfight with your team; don't 1v1.
Illaoi.late_game: She's a duelist god. Only teamfight when ahead or with a numbers advantage. Don't isolate.
Illaoi.win_condition: Get ahead early, teamfight when grouped. Avoid E completely. Never fight near walls with many tentacles.
Illaoi.scaling: Illaoi outscales — she's a better duelist. You need team fights.
Illaoi.positioning: Space far from tentacles. Don't fight near walls. Back away immediately when you see E cast.

Jax.trading_pattern: Jax Q-AA burst wins short trades. Extended trades are more even. Trade when his E is on cooldown.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: His E counter-strike — when he jumps at you with E up, back away. He stuns you if the counter triggers.
Jax.early_game: Play short trades, not extended. When Jax E is down, look for aggressive trades. Back off when he walks forward aggressively.
Jax.mid_game: He duels better with items. Your ult is better grouped. Teamfight instead of 1v1.
Jax.late_game: Jax is a hypercarry duelist. You can't 1v1. Stick to teamfights with ult advantage.
Jax.win_condition: Win teamfights, not duels. Ult into his team. Roam before he gets Trinity Force.
Jax.scaling: Jax outscales into a duelist monster. You need teamfights.
Jax.positioning: Don't let him get in melee range without ult available. Space when he walks up aggressively.

Shen.trading_pattern: Shen's E taunt interrupts your combo. Short burst trades lose to his W tankiness. Trade at range when E is down.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: His E taunt — move perpendicular to his charge. Never stand in his charge path. The taunt duration is long.
Shen.early_game: Play around his E taunt. When it's down, you can trade. He's tanky — don't expect short trades to do much.
Shen.mid_game: Post-6, he splits with R up. Track his ult. Teamfight when his ult is down. He's weak in side lanes 1v1.
Shen.late_game: He's a split-pushing tank. Your ult is stronger in team fights. Force fights when he's pinned.
Shen.win_condition: Force teamfights when his ult is down. Punish his split push. Win through 5v4 scenarios.
Shen.scaling: Even — different roles. He scales as a split pusher; you as a teamfighter.
Shen.positioning: Avoid walking into narrow passages where his E can freely taunt. Space from minion clusters.

Urgot.trading_pattern: Urgot's W auto attacks and E fear win early. Long-range poke with Q. He dominates lane pressure.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: His E fear + grab — dodge by moving sideways. If grabbed, you're dead. Back away when he walks forward.
Urgot.early_game: He pokes harder with Q. His W auto-attacking is too much damage. Play safe, farm, and avoid all-ins.
Urgot.mid_game: His R execute scales fast. Post-6, never sit below 40% HP. Your ult is safer but less damaging.
Urgot.late_game: He outscales you in poke damage and teamfight security with R. Stick to grouped fights, avoid isolating.
Urgot.win_condition: Survive early, group for teamfights. Avoid extending. Let him misposition, then ult into him.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot outscales — his R execute is oppressive. You need teamfights.
Urgot.positioning: Stay far from walls to avoid E fear/grab. Don't extend without vision.

Vayne.trading_pattern: Vayne's range advantage lets her kite. Tumble let's her reposition. E you into wall for condemn stun.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Her E condemn — move away from walls so she can't stun you. If near a wall, she will condemn you.

power_spikes.enemy: Trinity Force (late game win condition), Level 6

Vayne.early_game: She's weak early. Pressure and look for kills before her scaling. All-in when her E is on cooldown.
Vayne.mid_game: She starts scaling with items. Teamfight instead of 1v1. Your ult zoning is superior.
Vayne.late_game: Vayne is a hypercarry late. You can't duel. Protect your backline from her; use your ult to zone.
Vayne.win_condition: Abuse early weakness with aggression. Win before Trinity Force. Teamfight with numbers advantage.
Vayne.scaling: Vayne outscales dramatically — she's a late-game monster. Win early or lose.
Vayne.positioning: Never fight near walls. Distance yourself from them. Don't extend alone.

Varus.trading_pattern: Varus pokes with Q and applies on-hit stacks. He outdamages short trades early. Trade when Q is on cooldown.
Varus.ability_to_dodge: His R chain reaction — dodge by moving to the side. If hit, the chain spreads and roots you.
Varus.early_game: Play safe into his Q poke. He applies stacks for AP scaling or AD burst. At level 3, trade when Q is down.
Varus.mid_game: His R is devastating in teamfights. Stay spread. Your ult is safer for engagement.
Varus.late_game: He scales as a poke/control mage. Your tankiness and ult are stronger. Teamfight, don't duel.
Varus.win_condition: Survive poke, hit level 3+ for all-ins. Teamfight grouped. Force him into bad R positions.
Varus.scaling: Even with different win conditions. Varus pokes; you teamfight. Stalemate late game.
Varus.positioning: Space wide from allies to reduce R chain hits. Don't let him stack you easily.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Yasuo dashes through minions with E to harass. Short trades are even. Long fights let you ult-lock him.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: His E-Q combo chain — move away from minions so he can't dash to you. Windwall blocks your projectile effects.
Yasuo.early_game: Stand far from minions to prevent E dashes. When his E is down, trade hard. He scales but wins early.
Yasuo.mid_game: Your ult is stronger grouped. Teamfight with your team. He splitpushes well but loses in 5v5s.
Yasuo.late_game: Yasuo is an assassin split pusher. You're a teamfight tank. Stick to grouped fights.
Yasuo.win_condition: Minimize minion damage early. Teamfight grouped. Ult him when he dashes in.
Yasuo.scaling: Even — Yasuo splits, you teamfight. Depends on game flow.
Yasuo.positioning: Space from minion waves. Don't feed him E stack resets. Back away when he dashes toward you.

Yone.trading_pattern: Yone Q-E harass deals heavy damage. His E soul tether lets him reposition. Trade short when his E is down.
Yone.ability_to_dodge: His R spirit dive — dodge by running perpendicular. He marks you with charged Q; avoid full rotation.
Yone.early_game: Yone's poke is strong. His E gives safety. Play defensive early. At level 3+, trade around his abilities.
Yone.mid_game: He scales fast with items. Teamfight instead of 1v1. Your ult is safer for engagement.
Yone.late_game: Yone is a late-game duelist/split pusher. Stick to teamfights. Don't isolate.
Yone.win_condition: Survive early, teamfight grouped. Roam to generate pressure. Win through coordination.
Yone.scaling: Yone outscales — he's a split pusher with late-game power. You scale as a teamfighter.
Yone.positioning: Space from his Q-E combo path. Don't extend into river. Trade when E is on cooldown.

Malphite.trading_pattern: Malphite is tanky but low damage early. You outdamage him. All-in when his E is on cooldown.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: His E shockwave — move away from him when he winds up. It doesn't slow but reduces armor.
Malphite.early_game: He's a weak early laner. All-in him at level 2-3. He scales to teamfight god but is killable now.
Malphite.mid_game: Post-6, his R is powerful in grouped fights. Your ult is stronger. Teamfight instead of dueling.
Malphite.late_game: He's a teamfight god; you're even. Stick to grouped fights. Don't 1v1.
Malphite.win_condition: Kill him early before scaling. All-in at level 2-3. Roam with ult.
Malphite.scaling: Malphite scales better as a teamfighter. You need early kills.
Malphite.positioning: Don't stand clumped for his R. Space for teamfight efficiency.

Kayle.trading_pattern: Kayle farms early with Q. She's weak in short trades. All-in her when she walks up carelessly.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Her W healing — it heals her and gives movespeed. Focus her down before she heals herself back up.
Kayle.early_game: She's incredibly weak early. All-in at level 1-3. Deny her farm. She scales to a monster if allowed to farm.
Kayle.mid_game: She gets her melee form at 11. Post-11, she outscales you. Win before then.
Kayle.late_game: Full build Kayle beats you. You need to win before her power spikes.
Kayle.win_condition: Abuse her early weakness relentlessly. Don't let her farm for free. Get a lead.
Kayle.scaling: Kayle outscales dramatically — she becomes unkillable late. You must win early.
Kayle.positioning: Stay close to kill her. Don't let her farm safely at range.

Ornn.trading_pattern: Ornn is tanky but slow early. You outdamage short trades. All-in when his W is down.
Ornn.ability_to_dodge: His R volcanic rupture — move sideways to avoid knockback. If hit, it interrupts your combo.
Ornn.early_game: He's weak to early all-ins. Pressure heavily. All-in at level 2-3.
Ornn.mid_game: He scales with items and upgrades. Your ult is still stronger grouped. Teamfight.
Ornn.late_game: He's a tanky support-like teammate. You're an engager. Stick to teamfights.
Ornn.win_condition: Kill him early. All-in at level 2-3. Don't let him scale up with free farm.
Ornn.scaling: Ornn scales well as a tanky support. You scale as a teamfighter.
Ornn.positioning: Get close for all-ins. Don't let him kite freely. Force fights when he's extended.

Teemo.trading_pattern: Teemo pokes with Q from range. He's untargetable briefly. Short trades go to you. Rush him at level 2-3.
Teemo.ability_to_dodge: His Q blind — it hits you at range, avoid by moving unpredictably. He blinded, all-in his next trade.
Teemo.early_game: He pokes safely. All-in him hard at level 2-3. He scales to a late-game monster if farm-heavy.
Teemo.mid_game: He's weak in grouped fights. Teamfight when you have numbers. His split push is weak to teamfights.
Teemo.late_game: He outscales as a split pusher. Force grouped fights before that. Don't let him split.
Teemo.win_condition: All-in him early. Don't let him scale to split-push phase. Teamfight grouped.
Teemo.scaling: Teemo outscales as a split pusher. You need early kills.
Teemo.positioning: Rush him; don't let him kite. Run him down at level 2-3.

Gnar.trading_pattern: Gnar mini-range let's him poke. Mega Gnar all-in is oppressive. Trade when he's mini and E is down.
Gnar.ability_to_dodge: His mega transformation — brace for impact when he's about to transform. His R knockback hurts.
Gnar.early_game: Mini Gnar pokes early. Mega is scary. Play safe around his transformation timer. All-in mini.
Gnar.mid_game: Mega Gnar teamfights well. Your ult is safer. Fight when he's mini form.
Gnar.late_game: He's a scaling teamfighter. You're equal. Depends on teamfight positioning.
Gnar.win_condition: All-in him when mini. Avoid mega form all-ins. Teamfight grouped.
Gnar.positioning: Target him when mini. Avoid when mega. Space from his R knockback.

Urgot.trading_pattern: Urgot's W constant autos and E fear dominate early. He pokes with Q safely. You can't trade back effectively.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: His E fear + grab — move sideways when charging. If grabbed, you're pulled and destroyed.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Q scaling into late), Frozen Heart (armor stacking)

Urgot.early_game: You lose all trades pre-6. Play safe, farm passively, let him push. Never all-in him.
Urgot.mid_game: He R executes you if low. Stay above 40% HP always. Your Q stacks aren't enough to duel him yet.
Urgot.late_game: Full stacks let you duel, but his poke resets you. Teamfight grouped. Don't isolate.
Urgot.win_condition: Survive early, stack Q safely. Teamfight with armor items to survive his R. Never duel.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot outscales through R execute. You scale through stacks but lose to execute.
Urgot.positioning: Stay far from walls to avoid E pull. Don't extend into river.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Yorick summons minions to damage you. His ghouls swarm you quickly. Extended trades lose to minion damage.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: His E minion spawn — when he targets you, move sideways. Don't let him spawn ghouls on you.
Yorick.early_game: Yorick's ghouls are oppressive. He pressure you constantly. Play safe and farm Q passively.
Yorick.mid_game: His maiden plus ghouls destroy you in extended fights. Teamfight grouped where ghouls are weak.
Yorick.late_game: He's a split-pushing god. You can't duel him 1v1. Force grouped fights; never let him split.
Yorick.win_condition: Survive early stacking Q. Teamfight grouped. Don't isolate or split against him.
Yorick.scaling: Yorick outscales dramatically — split pusher god late. You scale through stacks.
Yorick.positioning: Stay spread to reduce ghoul swarm damage. Back away from his E targeting.

Briar.trading_pattern: Briar all-ins with W and E rapidly. She has high damage and healing. You can't duel her early.
Briar.ability_to_dodge: Her E leap — move perpendicular when she charges at you. Back away or she heals off your damage.
Briar.early_game: She's incredibly strong early. All her abilities heal. Play safe and don't trade.
Briar.mid_game: She needs items for damage. Your stacks growing helps. Teamfight instead of 1v1.
Briar.late_game: Full stacks let you duel, but she heals through your damage. Stick to grouped fights.
Briar.win_condition: Survive early with passive farming. Don't get caught alone. Teamfight grouped.
Briar.scaling: Briar scales well through healing. You scale through stacks.
Briar.positioning: Kite away from her E leap. Don't let her close and all-in.

Shen.trading_pattern: Shen's E taunt interrupts your Q. His W tankiness neutralizes your damage. You can't all-in him.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: His E taunt — move perpendicular to his charge. The taunt duration is long; avoid his path.
Shen.early_game: Shen is tanky and has good engage. Play safe, stack Q, avoid his E taunt.
Shen.mid_game: His ult makes him a teamfight god. You scale slower. Group for fights; don't split.
Shen.late_game: He's a tank support. You're a damage dealer. Teamfight grouped.
Shen.win_condition: Stack Q passively. Teamfight grouped with team. Avoid his E taunt.
Shen.scaling: Shen scales as a tank. You scale as a damage dealer.
Shen.positioning: Avoid walking into narrow passages for E taunt. Space from minion clusters.

Pantheon.trading_pattern: Pantheon spears poke constantly from range. His E stun blocks your Q. You can't trade effectively.
Pantheon.ability_to_dodge: His E spear stun — move sideways to dodge. His range is too great early.
Pantheon.early_game: Pantheon outpokes and outzones you. Play safe, minimize trades, farm with Q.
Pantheon.mid_game: Your stacks growing help dueling. He roams for kills. Teamfight instead of 1v1.
Pantheon.late_game: You outscale him as a duelist. Force fights where he's caught.
Pantheon.win_condition: Survive poke, stack passively. Outscale into mid-game. Duel when ahead on stacks.
Pantheon.scaling: You outscale — Pantheon is an early game bully. Survive early.
Pantheon.positioning: Hide behind minions for poke reduction. Don't extend into river.

Garen.trading_pattern: Garen Q silence shuts down your combo. His Judgment AOE is oppressive. Avoid extended trades.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: His Q silence — when he winds up, back away. Silence prevents your Q usage.

power_spikes.you: Level 9 (Q stacking into mid), Trinity Force

Garen.late_game: You outscale as a duelist. Force isolated fights where you have the advantage.

Malphite.late_game: He outscales as a tank. You scale as a duelist. Split push if ahead.

Briar.trading_pattern: Use fully charged Q (Decimating Smash) to zone Briar away from the wave — she has no reliable way to disengage once rooted. Activate W (Soul Furnace) shield before she leaps in with E, then release Q to punish her landing position and force her out of range.
Briar.ability_to_dodge: Briar's E (Chilling Scream) leap and follow-up E2 knockback combo is her primary engage tool — watch her body language and sidestep her leap angle so she lands into your charged Q rather than behind you, preventing her from pinning you to terrain.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Unstoppable Onslaught gives kill threat and escape) and Heartsteel first item (massive HP scaling makes you nearly unkillable in extended trades)
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 (full kit online — Q bleed, W shield, E engage) and Sundered Sky / Black Cleaver first item drastically increases her burst and armor shred

Briar.early_game: Levels 1-2 favor Sion — stack Q poke on Briar whenever she walks up to last-hit. From level 3 onward Briar can all-in you; keep your W shield active before any trade and use E (Roar of the Slayer) to slow her so she cannot freely gap-close. Do not let her stack bleed on you by standing in long auto-attack trades without your shield up.
Briar.mid_game: After Heartsteel, your HP pool becomes massive and Sion's passive (Glory in Death) becomes a genuine deterrent against Briar diving you under tower. Continue to Q poke in lane, proxy the wave occasionally to starve Briar of gold, and look for TP flanks in mid-lane skirmishes where your CC is game-changing.
Briar.late_game: In teamfights, use Unstoppable Onslaught (R) to catch Briar out before she snowballs onto your backline, or use it to peel by body-blocking her dive. Your Glory in Death passive means Briar must either disengage after killing you or die to your zombie form — use this deterrent to front-line aggressively.
Briar.win_condition: Build into Heartsteel and Titanic Hydra to out-stat Briar in bulk and waveclear, deny her kill pressure by keeping W shield ready before every trade, and leverage Sion's superior teleport + ult map presence to win objectives while Briar is stuck trying to kill you in lane.
Briar.scaling: Sion outscales Briar in the very late game due to unlimited HP stacking via Heartsteel and passive, but Briar has a mid-game spike around first item (Sundered Sky) where she has kill pressure if Sion overextends without vision.
Briar.positioning: Hug the center of the lane or your minion wave — never stand near walls or the edge of the lane, as Briar's E can pin you against terrain for a full bleed combo. Stand behind your caster minions to partially bodyblock her E leap trajectory and force her to commit deeper than she wants to.

Cho'Gath.trading_pattern: Use W (Soul Furnace) shield proactively before Cho'Gath engages to absorb his Rupture burst, then auto-attack to proc and pop the shield for bonus damage. Look to land E (Roar of the Slayer) to slow Cho'Gath and set up a fully-charged Q (Decimating Smash) during the slow window.
Cho'Gath.ability_to_dodge: Cho'Gath's Q (Rupture) — the knock-up AoE circle — is the most punishing ability to avoid; step sideways immediately when you see the ground indicator animate, as it has a 0.625s delay before eruption. Getting hit stops your Q charge and sets up Cho'Gath's full combo.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (Sion gains Unstoppable Onslaught for engage/disengage) and first completed tank item (Unending Despair or Heartsteel for passive HP stacking synergy)
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (Cho'Gath's Feast gives him a permanent HP stack and executes you if low) and after 4+ Feast stacks he becomes nearly unkillable in short trades

Cho'Gath.early_game: Levels 1-5 are roughly even in raw damage, but Cho'Gath's passive (Carnivore) lets him sustain off every minion kill — pressure him off last-hitting by using E-slow into Q to deny him free CS and healing. Stack your W shield before trading and pop it during his retaliatory autos to win short exchanges.
Cho'Gath.mid_game: After your first back, look to build Heartsteel to accelerate your passive HP growth and start split-pushing. Cho'Gath wants to stack Feast stacks safely in lane; deny him by all-inning with R (Unstoppable Onslaught) on a straight corridor to guarantee the knockup, then follow up with E+Q.
Cho'Gath.late_game: In teamfights, your Unstoppable Onslaught can peel or engage more reliably than Cho'Gath's slow telegraphed Rupture. Body-block his Feral Scream (W silence cone) away from your carries by positioning between Cho'Gath and your backline. Your Death passive also provides a second threat to distract him.
Cho'Gath.win_condition: Stack Heartsteel HP early through consistent trades and lane pressure, deny Cho'Gath Feast stacks by forcing him off minions with E+Q poke, and use Unstoppable Onslaught to open teamfights or split-push pressure — Sion's siege/split tools outpace Cho'Gath's if ahead in stacks.
Cho'Gath.scaling: Cho'Gath outscales Sion in raw tankiness with infinite Feast stacks by late game (5+ stacks), but Sion's passive HP from Glory in Death and Heartsteel can compensate — Cho'Gath becomes the stronger frontline at 30+ minutes if not snowballed early.
Cho'Gath.positioning: Hug the side of the minion wave opposite to Cho'Gath's most recent Rupture — he tends to drop it on your current position. Keep terrain walls nearby so your Unstoppable Onslaught has a clean, straight-line path for guaranteed contact, and avoid standing in his Feral Scream cone angle, which he typically aims at the river-side of the lane.

Kassadin.trading_pattern: Use Gold Card (W) to stun Kassadin before he can proc his Null Sphere (Q) silence on you, then follow up immediately with Wild Cards (Q) and auto attacks. Trade short and back off — never let him get sustained melee range where his E and passive shred your damage.
Kassadin.ability_to_dodge: Kassadin's Force Pulse (E) — it only becomes castable after 6 nearby spell casts, so track ability usage in lane and step back when it's charged. Being hit by it slows you severely and lets him chain into a Riftwalk gap-close.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with Destiny (R) — use it immediately to impact other lanes before Kassadin stabilizes. First item Shadowflame or Stormsurge for poke burst.
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 Riftwalk plus Rod of Ages first item (~12–15 minutes) — Kassadin's all-in potential becomes extremely dangerous once he has both the R mobility and mana sustain online.

Kassadin.early_game: Abuse your range advantage levels 1–5 to poke Kassadin with autos and W-Q combos whenever he steps up to last-hit. His Null Sphere (Q) only interrupts channels and silences briefly, so your poke pattern is safe as long as you avoid trading at melee range. Force him to farm under tower by pushing the wave.
Kassadin.mid_game: Roam aggressively with Destiny (R) the moment Kassadin backs or misses a wave — you cannot win a sustained 1v1 post-6 once he has items, so your gold and pressure must come from map plays. Ping his position constantly since his Riftwalk makes him a permanent roam threat to side lanes.
Kassadin.late_game: In teamfights, stay at maximum range and use Stacked Deck (E) auto resets to peel poke damage safely. Never front-line — if Kassadin gets on you without your Gold Card up, you will die. Use Destiny's global vision to track Kassadin at all times and avoid being caught alone.
Kassadin.win_condition: Snowball other lanes before Kassadin reaches his level 11–16 spike. Use Destiny (R) to teleport to winning skirmishes elsewhere, build a gold lead through roaming, and force the game to end before late game when Kassadin outscales you completely.
Kassadin.scaling: Kassadin heavily outscales Twisted Fate — by level 11 with two items, Kassadin's burst and mobility make him nearly impossible to deal with in a direct fight. Twisted Fate must end the game or be significantly ahead before this point.
Kassadin.positioning: Stand behind your minion wave to force Kassadin to walk into your range to farm. The minions partially block his Null Sphere (Q) angle. Keep max auto-attack distance — if he ever closes the gap without you having Gold Card ready, disengage immediately rather than trading.

Brand.trading_pattern: Avoid extended poke trades entirely — use minions as shields to block Brand's W (Pillar of Flame) and Q (Sear), then punish him only when his Blaze passive has expired on you. Short Q trades only when he has no stun available.
Brand.ability_to_dodge: Brand's W (Pillar of Flame) is the most important ability to dodge — it has a visible cast animation, so sidestep immediately when you see him channel it, as landing it on a Blazed target sets up his Q stun and his entire combo.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with Luden's Tempest first item — your R damage scales with enemy AP so Brand's own stacks inflate your ult damage; also level 9 with Event Horizon (E) maxed for a reliable cage stun.
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with Sear (Q) + Pillar of Flame (W) + Pyroclasm (R) full combo available, and Luden's Tempest first item; Brand's passive Blaze burn bouncing through his R can burst you down instantly.

Brand.early_game: Levels 1–5 are very difficult — position at max Q range to farm and stack Phenomenal Evil passively, and never stand in line with minions that are already ablaze or he will chain his W onto you. Back off when Blaze is on you rather than trading.
Brand.mid_game: After first back, rush Seraph's Embrace (or Luden's Tempest) and use your E cage proactively to set up your own W+Q+R combo before he can ignite you. Roam with your jungler to get ahead since you will not reliably out-damage Brand in a straight 1v1 at this stage.
Brand.late_game: Veigar outscales Brand significantly due to infinite AP stacking — in teamfights, stand at maximum R range and use Event Horizon on the flank to cage Brand away from your team before he can proc his bouncing Pyroclasm, then burst him down with your full combo.
Brand.win_condition: Survive the early laning phase with sufficient CS and AP stacks, avoid getting one-rotated by Brand's stun-into-ult combo, and outscale into a late-game carry by using your cage (E) to set up picks on Brand before he can chain his passive.
Brand.scaling: Veigar outscales Brand significantly in the late game due to infinite AP stacking via Phenomenal Evil; Brand is the much stronger early-to-mid game champion but falls off as Veigar's AP stack count climbs past 300+.
Brand.positioning: Always stay behind your minion wave to block Brand's W ground-target; never stand near a teammate who is already Blazed, as Brand's Conflagration (E) spreads Blaze to nearby enemies. Hug the side of the lane away from Brand to reduce angle on his stun chain.

Yasuo.trading_pattern: Bait out Yasuo's Wind Wall by throwing Event Horizon (E) first — if he wastes it, immediately follow with Dark Matter (W) into Primordial Burst (R) for a full burst combo. Never throw your Q or R into an active Wind Wall; instead, step inside his Wind Wall hitbox and cast R point-blank where it cannot be blocked.
Yasuo.ability_to_dodge: Yasuo's Q3 tornado (Steel Tempest whirlwind) — watch and listen for the glowing Q animation after two consecutive Q hits, then sidestep laterally since the projectile is very narrow and only travels in a straight line.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 + Luden's Tempest first item — your R one-shot threat becomes real, and every AP stack from passive amplifies your R damage further
power_spikes.enemy: Level 3 + Immortal Shieldbow first item — Yasuo gains consistent trading power with his double crit passive and the lifesteal shield item protects him from your burst

Yasuo.early_game: Pre-6, respect Yasuo's all-in potential and focus on stacking AP via Baleful Strike (Q) on minions while staying max range. Place Event Horizon (E) on the lane boundary so Yasuo gets stunned trying to dash to you through minions, as he cannot E through a wall of the cage.
Yasuo.mid_game: After completing Luden's Tempest, your cage-into-burst combo one-shots or nearly one-shots Yasuo. Force his Wind Wall by throwing a decoy auto or E from an angle, then follow immediately with W+R. Roam with your jungler — Yasuo is highly gankable and Veigar's E makes him trivial to lock down.
Yasuo.late_game: In teamfights, position at max range and use Event Horizon to cage priority targets or peel for yourself if Yasuo dives you. Your stacked AP R will one-shot or nearly one-shot Yasuo even with defensive items. Never use R when his Wind Wall is active; instead walk toward him and cast at point-blank range.
Yasuo.win_condition: Stack AP aggressively through passive, avoid dying to Yasuo's early all-ins, and reach the mid-game with enough AP stacks that your R + W + Q burst kills him through his shield. Force his Wind Wall on a non-threatening ability before committing to your full combo.
Yasuo.scaling: Veigar outscales hard — his passive AP stacking means his R damage grows every minute of the game. Yasuo falls off significantly if he cannot snowball, making a patient Veigar a severe late-game threat.
Yasuo.positioning: Stand directly behind your own minion wave so Yasuo cannot dash to you with E without burning a minion first. Hug the wall on the side opposite to where Yasuo wants to dash, forcing him to use a longer E path and giving you time to drop Event Horizon at his feet before he reaches you.

Xerath.mid_game: Both scale similarly through utility and poke.
Xerath.win_condition: Stay attached and sustain, execute ultimate well.

Yuumi.early_game: Trade with careful attachment. Better poke wins.
Yuumi.mid_game: Both scale identically. Team coordination matters.
Yuumi.late_game: Whoever executes ultimate better controls fights.
Yuumi.win_condition: Better poke accuracy, superior ultimate execution, team coordination.
Yuumi.scaling: Perfectly even — identical in every way
Yuumi.positioning: Mirror opponent's attachment choices. Execute ultimate better.

Zyra.ability_to_dodge: Zyra E (Rampant Growth) — dodge by moving with ADC around roots.

Morgana.ability_to_dodge: Morgana Q (Dark Binding) — dodge by moving with ADC.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 with ultimate cc, Liandry's completed
power_spikes.enemy: Level 1-2 with poke, Level 6 with ultimate root

Morgana.win_condition: Stay attached and sustain, execute ultimate well.
Morgana.positioning: Stay attached to ADC throughout game.

Darius.trading_pattern: Both have a pull ability (his E, your W) — whoever lands theirs first controls the fight. His bleed prevents your passive Determination from recharging between trades. Short trades only until you have stacks.
Darius.ability_to_dodge: Darius E (Apprehend) — never stand still at medium range. He pulls you into bleed stacks which shuts down your passive regen window.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult sustain + damage reduction), 12 Determination stacks, Stridebreaker

Darius.early_game: He out-trades you pre-stacks. Build Determination on minions safely. Avoid his pull at all costs — it leads to full bleed.
Darius.mid_game: With 12 stacks your passive revive threat makes him play differently. Your ult's 50% damage reduction lets you survive his burst.
Darius.late_game: Your ult heal + passive revive make you harder to kill than him. Win extended fights.
Darius.win_condition: Hit 12 Determination stacks before committing. Your W pull vs his E pull — bait his first then punish with yours. Use ult to absorb his burst and heal back.
Darius.scaling: Darius wins early; Zaahen even or favored mid-late with passive stacks
Darius.positioning: Stay far enough to avoid his E but close enough to land W pull. Never stand in his Q outer ring.

Garen.trading_pattern: His Q silence stops your Q recast knockup during the silence window. Trade when Q silence is on cooldown. Your W pull after he uses Q brings him into your optimal range.
Garen.ability_to_dodge: Garen Q — back off when he sprints toward you. His silence prevents your Q recast knockup, which is critical for your combo.
Garen.early_game: He out-trades you short-term. Stack Determination on minions. Engage only when his Q is on cooldown.
Garen.mid_game: Your ult damage reduction absorbs his E spin damage. W pull him when he uses Q to close, then Q double-hit.
Garen.late_game: Your passive revive makes 1v1 kills on you nearly impossible if stacked. Outscale him significantly.
Garen.win_condition: Time trades around his Q cooldown. W pull after his Q is used. Your passive revive at max stacks makes you nearly unkillable.
Garen.scaling: Garen wins early; Zaahen outscales with passive stacks
Garen.positioning: Stay outside his Q sprint range. W him after he uses Q to keep him in your range.

Fiora.trading_pattern: She can Riposte your W pull or your Q knockup recast — bait one before committing the other. Your passive stacks don't help much if she heals via Vitals. Keep fights short.
Fiora.ability_to_dodge: Fiora W (Riposte) — bait it with a basic attack first, then use W pull or Q knockup. Never commit both abilities when her W is available.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult with damage reduction), 12 Determination stacks

Fiora.early_game: She wins early with Vitals healing. Trade carefully and don't extend fights.
Fiora.mid_game: Your ult damage reduction absorbs her burst window. Bait W before W pull combo.
Fiora.late_game: She outduels you in extended 1v1s via Vitals. Group for teamfights.
Fiora.win_condition: Bait Riposte with a basic attack before committing W pull + Q knockup. Use Ignite to cancel Vital healing. Don't let her proc multiple Vitals per trade.
Fiora.scaling: Fiora slightly better in pure 1v1; Zaahen better in teamfights
Fiora.positioning: Reposition off her active Vital sides. Bait W before using your pull.

Aatrox.trading_pattern: Both have pull/displacement abilities. His Q hitboxes are manageable — stand in the safe zones and W pull him during his Q animation. Extended fights favor you with Determination stacks.
Aatrox.ability_to_dodge: Aatrox Q — stand between the two safe zones between his Q hitboxes. Dash with E through him to avoid Q zones.
Aatrox.early_game: Fairly even. Your W pull while he commits to Q is a strong punish. Stack Determination in every trade.
Aatrox.mid_game: His Goredrinker healing competes with yours. Both have strong sustain — Determination stacks give you the edge.
Aatrox.late_game: Your passive revive makes you harder to kill. Win extended teamfight brawls.
Aatrox.win_condition: Stack Determination on every trade. W pull him during his Q animation. Your ult damage reduction negates his burst window.
Aatrox.scaling: Even; Zaahen slightly favored with full Determination stacks
Aatrox.positioning: Stand between his Q hitbox zones. E dash through him to avoid the sweet spots.

Camille.trading_pattern: Her true damage in E bypasses your passive Determination healing. She wins most early trades. Her ult isolates you 1v1 — use your ult damage reduction inside her hex zone.
Camille.ability_to_dodge: Camille E (Hookshot) — stay center of lane away from walls. No wall geometry means no clean E angle.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult damage reduction inside her hex zone), Determination stacks

Camille.early_game: She out-trades you early with true damage and passive shield. Farm safely and build stacks on minions.
Camille.mid_game: Her ult isolates you — immediately use ult for 50% damage reduction and heal. Fight back.
Camille.late_game: Your passive revive and ult sustain make you hard to solo kill. Outlast her.
Camille.win_condition: Use ult during her hex zone isolation for damage reduction. Stack Determination before fights. Stay away from walls.
Camille.scaling: Camille wins early; even mid; Zaahen harder to kill late
Camille.positioning: Center of lane, no walls. Use ult preemptively when she ults you.

Irelia.trading_pattern: Her Q resets at max stacks destroy you before you can build Determination. Keep her passive stacks low by not feeding her minion resets. Your W pull counters her dash-in.
Irelia.ability_to_dodge: Irelia E (Flawless Duet) — sidestep the two stun blades. W pull can interrupt her E combo setup.
Irelia.early_game: She bullies you at max passive stacks. Only trade when she has 0-2 stacks.
Irelia.mid_game: Your ult sustain starts competing with her. Stack Determination and fight at max stacks.
Irelia.late_game: Your passive revive makes killing you very difficult. Win extended fights.
Irelia.win_condition: Deny her passive stacks by keeping low HP minions away from her. W pull when she dashes in. Hit max Determination before engaging.
Irelia.scaling: Irelia wins early; Zaahen outscales with passive revive
Irelia.positioning: Keep low HP minions away from her to deny Q resets and passive stacks.

Jax.trading_pattern: Your Q double strike auto does not get blocked by his E (it's a special attack, not a basic attack — confirm based on Riot mechanics). His E blocks basic attacks. Trade with Q and W during his E cooldown.
Jax.ability_to_dodge: Jax E (Counterstrike) — don't basic attack during his E spin. Use Q ability during his E since it may bypass the dodge.
Jax.early_game: Fairly even. Your W pull gives you engage that his E can't stop. Build stacks on him safely.
Jax.mid_game: His Trinity Counterstrike pattern is strong. Your ult sustain competes.
Jax.late_game: Both scale well. Your passive revive gives you the edge in extended duels.
Jax.win_condition: W pull him outside his E range. Trade with Q ability strikes during his E cooldown. Max Determination before all-ins.
Jax.scaling: Even; Zaahen slightly favored with passive revive late
Jax.positioning: W pull him after he uses E to re-enter the fight when he wants to back off.

Renekton.trading_pattern: His burst combo kills you before you can stack Determination. His E gap close is faster than your response window. Survive early and respond with ult.
Renekton.ability_to_dodge: Renekton W (Ruthless Predator) — W pull him when he uses E to close so his W stun lands mid-pull and you control the fight position.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult damage reduction absorbs his burst), Determination stacks

Renekton.early_game: He dominates early. Farm under tower. Your ult at 6 gives you a fighting chance.
Renekton.mid_game: He falls off mid-game. With ult and stacks you can fight back.
Renekton.late_game: You outscale significantly. Your passive revive means he can never reliably kill you.
Renekton.win_condition: Survive to level 6. Use ult to absorb his burst burst combo. W pull to control fight position when he E's in.
Renekton.scaling: Renekton wins early; Zaahen outscales mid-late
Renekton.positioning: Farm under tower early. W pull timing when he commits E is key.

Nasus.trading_pattern: Bully him off every Q stack attempt. W pull him when he goes for CS and combo him. He has no early damage. Your Determination stacks while he farms.
Nasus.ability_to_dodge: Nasus W (Wither) — W pull him before he can Wither you, or disengage immediately when Wither lands.
Nasus.early_game: Free bully lane. W pull him off every CS attempt. Stack Determination constantly.
Nasus.mid_game: He starts getting stacks. Keep harassing. Your W pull is great for catching him.
Nasus.late_game: 500 stacks Nasus challenges you. Your passive revive is insurance. Group for objectives.
Nasus.win_condition: Deny every Q stack you can. Win before 400 stacks. Your Determination stacking means you're always at high passive bonus.
Nasus.scaling: Zaahen wins early; Nasus threatens past 400 stacks
Nasus.positioning: Stand between him and the CS he's trying to Q. W pull him away from it.

Malphite.trading_pattern: His armor reduces your physical Q damage but your sheer trading frequency builds Determination fast. W pull him when he goes for CS and trade freely.
Malphite.ability_to_dodge: Malphite Q (Seismic Shard) — sidestep it. The movement speed slow makes your W pull harder to dodge for him.
Malphite.early_game: You win early. W pull him and trade. His passive shield is an annoyance but your frequency of trades keeps it down.
Malphite.mid_game: His armor stacking limits your physical damage. Build Black Cleaver or Lord Dom's.
Malphite.late_game: His ult engage in teamfights is strong but your passive revive insurance is better for 1v1.
Malphite.win_condition: W pull into Q double-hit combo repeatedly. Build Black Cleaver for armor shred. Win lane and snowball.
Malphite.scaling: Zaahen wins lane; Malphite better multi-target teamfight
Malphite.positioning: Pull him away from his passive shield recharge safe zones. W pull → Q combo is your bread and butter.

Mordekaiser.trading_pattern: His ult removes your Determination stacks from the teamfight context — it's 1v1 in the realm. Inside the realm, your ult damage reduction + Q heal makes it winnable.
Mordekaiser.ability_to_dodge: Mordekaiser R (Realm of Death) — you can't escape it. Use your ult immediately inside the realm for 50% damage reduction and the heal.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult in realm fight), Determination stacks

Mordekaiser.early_game: He wins early with passive shield sustain. Short trades with W pull.
Mordekaiser.mid_game: His ult isolated is dangerous. Fight inside realm with ult active.
Mordekaiser.late_game: Your passive revive makes his ult less reliable as a kill tool. Extended fights favor you.
Mordekaiser.win_condition: Use ult immediately when he ults you for 50% DR + heal. Stack Determination before he can ult again. Track his ult cooldown.
Mordekaiser.scaling: Mordekaiser wins lane; even mid-late with passive stacks
Mordekaiser.positioning: Don't stand in his W golden zone. Outside it you can W pull him on your terms.

Olaf.trading_pattern: His ult makes him immune to your W pull — you lose your primary engage tool. Fight before his ult activates or wait it out. His axes at range are dangerous.
Olaf.ability_to_dodge: Olaf Q (Undertow) — dodge the axe. Punish his retrieve walk with W pull toward you.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 (ult CC immunity — makes your W pull less effective), Stridebreaker

Olaf.early_game: His axes hurt early. Build stacks safely and avoid his all-in levels.
Olaf.mid_game: His ult negates your W pull. Fight before he activates Ragnarok.
Olaf.late_game: Your passive revive insurance is strong. Force extended fights when his ult is down.
Olaf.win_condition: W pull before he activates ult. Your ult damage reduction absorbs his burst. Fight extended when his ult is on cooldown.
Olaf.scaling: Olaf favored mid; even late with Zaahen's passive revive
Olaf.positioning: Dodge axe throw. Punish his retrieve walk with W pull. Don't engage when his ult is active.

Tryndamere.trading_pattern: Abuse him pre-6. Your W pull prevents his Spinning Slash escape. Your passive Determination stacks fast off his aggressive crits. Never fight during his ult.
Tryndamere.ability_to_dodge: Tryndamere E (Spinning Slash) — W pull him back when he tries to Spinning Slash away from you.
Tryndamere.early_game: Bully him pre-6. Your W pull keeps him in your range. Stack Determination off every trade.
Tryndamere.mid_game: His crits compete with your healing. Your W pull denies his escape.
Tryndamere.late_game: His ult makes killing him impossible for 5s. Your passive revive makes you unkillable simultaneously. Coin flip fights.
Tryndamere.win_condition: W pull to deny his Spinning Slash escape. When he ults, back off and wait it out. Your passive revive vs his ult creates interesting dynamics.
Tryndamere.scaling: Zaahen wins early; even mid; both have anti-death mechanics late
Tryndamere.positioning: W pull him when he tries to Spinning Slash away. Keep him in your optimal range at all times.

Yorick.trading_pattern: Kill his ghouls with E AoE to deny mist charges. Your W pull keeps him in range and prevents E escape. Avoid fighting with Maiden active.
Yorick.ability_to_dodge: Yorick E (Mourning Mist) — sidestep the mark. Being marked causes Maiden and ghouls to prioritize you.
Yorick.early_game: Even early. Kill ghouls with E AoE. W pull him off CS attempts.
Yorick.mid_game: Maiden + Hullbreaker makes him dangerous. Match his push.
Yorick.late_game: Your passive revive insurance is great in split push scenarios. W pull denies his escape.
Yorick.win_condition: Kill ghouls with E AoE to deny Maiden. W pull keeps him in range. Your passive revive makes 1v1 splits very hard for him.
Yorick.scaling: Even; Zaahen slightly better 1v1, Yorick better split with Maiden
Yorick.positioning: Use E AoE to clear ghoul clusters. W pull to keep him from escaping to Maiden.

Sett.trading_pattern: His grit generates from taking damage — your frequent trading builds his W. W pull him when his grit is low. Sidestep his W center explosion.
Sett.ability_to_dodge: Sett W (Haymaker) — sidestep the center true damage. W pull him sideways so he throws W at an awkward angle.
Sett.early_game: Even early. W pull to keep him in range. Don't let him freely auto-attack you to build grit.
Sett.mid_game: He becomes tankier. Your ult sustain competes with his bulk.
Sett.late_game: Your passive revive + ult heal makes you very hard to kill. Extended fights favor you.
Sett.win_condition: W pull at low grit. Sidestep W center. Your passive revive means he needs to burst you which is hard against your DR ult.
Sett.scaling: Even; Zaahen harder to kill late
Sett.positioning: Don't stand directly in front of him when grit is high. W pull sideways to create bad W angles.

Shen.trading_pattern: His W dodge blocks your basic attacks but not your Q (ability strike) or W pull. Use Q recast knockup and W pull freely even during his W dodge window.
Shen.ability_to_dodge: Shen W (Spirit's Refuge) — stop basic attacking. Use Q ability cast and W pull since they hit through his dodge zone.
Shen.early_game: You win early. Q and W hit through his dodge. Bully him.
Shen.mid_game: His global ult changes other lanes. Push fast and deny his roam.
Shen.late_game: Even direct fight. Your passive revive vs his global utility.
Shen.win_condition: Q ability + W pull hit through his W dodge zone. Build CS lead and track his ult cooldown for objectives.
Shen.positioning: W pull him away from his dodge zone walls. Q through his W dodge since it's an ability cast.

Sion.trading_pattern: W pull him when he charges Q to interrupt it and pull him into your range. His immobility is a gift. Stack Determination for free.
Sion.ability_to_dodge: Sion Q (Decimating Smash) — W pull him toward you to interrupt his Q charge. Sidestep the knockup direction.
Sion.early_game: Free lane. W pull interrupts his Q. E dash through him for AoE damage.
Sion.mid_game: He becomes tankier. Build Black Cleaver.
Sion.late_game: Your passive revive is more useful than his passive zombie form.
Sion.win_condition: W pull to interrupt Q charges. Build Black Cleaver for armor shred. Dominate lane and roam.
Sion.scaling: Zaahen wins throughout
Sion.positioning: W pull when he begins Q windup to interrupt. E dash through for AoE damage.

Vayne.trading_pattern: Kill her early before Guinsoo's. Your W pull closes the gap efficiently. Her Silver Bolts true damage shreds your HP in extended fights and you can't heal through true damage.
Vayne.ability_to_dodge: Vayne E (Condemn) — never stand near walls. She'll condemn you into terrain and remove your fighting ability.
Vayne.early_game: All-in her early with W pull + Q combo. She's squishy and can't escape your pull.
Vayne.mid_game: Silver Bolts true damage starts melting you despite Q healing. Short trades only.
Vayne.late_game: True damage bypasses your Determination healing. Avoid 1v1s. Burst her fast in teamfights.
Vayne.win_condition: Kill her before Guinsoo's. Take Ignite. W pull to prevent her backstep. Your W pull denies her kite pattern.
Vayne.scaling: Zaahen wins very early; Vayne hard outscales
Vayne.positioning: W pull toward you when she tries to reposition. Stay away from walls.

Kayle.trading_pattern: Abuse her hard pre-11. W pull closes the gap instantly. Your Q heal + Determination stacking makes early trades very favorable. Kill her before she scales.
Kayle.ability_to_dodge: Kayle E (Starfire Spellblade) — when she's progressive ranged at later levels, W pull negates her kite by bringing her to you.
Kayle.early_game: Dominate her. W pull prevents any kite. Kill her at level 2-3.
Kayle.mid_game: Keep pressure through level 10. At 11 her range becomes dangerous.
Kayle.late_game: At level 11+ she kites. Your W pull's range matters — try to land it before she escapes range.
Kayle.win_condition: Kill her before level 6. W pull negates her kite. Snowball lead before she hits 11.
Kayle.scaling: Zaahen wins early; Kayle wins past level 11
Kayle.positioning: Stay on top of her constantly pre-11. W pull every time she tries to step back.

Trundle.trading_pattern: W pull to keep him from running into his Frozen Domain. His ult steals your AD — very painful for a melee fighter. Avoid 1v1 when his ult is available.
Trundle.ability_to_dodge: Trundle E (Pillar of Ice) — W pull him before he places it, or sidestep the Pillar once placed. Don't let it trap you.
Trundle.early_game: You win early. W pull to keep him in range.
Trundle.mid_game: His ult stealing your AD is brutal for a primarily physical fighter. Avoid 1v1.
Trundle.late_game: Your passive revive insurance. Group for teamfights where his ult is less impactful.
Trundle.win_condition: Win lane early. Avoid 1v1 when his ult is up — it steals your AD which is your entire damage source.
Trundle.scaling: Zaahen wins early; Trundle's ult is very painful mid-late
Trundle.positioning: W pull to keep him outside his Frozen Domain speed boost.

Urgot.trading_pattern: His shotgun knees deal massive damage per trade and your Q healing barely keeps up. His W passive fires constantly. Only commit with W pull + full combo when his W is on cooldown.
Urgot.ability_to_dodge: Urgot W (Purge) — kite backwards when it activates. His knees are directional — angle away from his front.

power_spikes.you: Level 6 (ult with 50% DR helps survive his burst), Determination stacks

Urgot.early_game: He wins early. Farm under tower. His passive knees outdamage your Q heal.
Urgot.mid_game: With ult and stacks you can fight. His ult execute threatens you at low HP — use passive revive insurance.
Urgot.late_game: Your passive revive denies his execute ult. Very interesting anti-synergy.
Urgot.win_condition: Passive revive insurance means his execute ult doesn't kill you on first proc. Engage with ult DR. W pull to keep him in range.
Urgot.scaling: Urgot wins early; even late due to Zaahen's passive revive countering his execute
Urgot.positioning: Angle away from his knee direction. Don't stand directly in front of his W active. Use ult preemptively.

Riven.trading_pattern: Her shield and mobility make short trades go her way. Your W pull after her E shield expires keeps her in range. Time W pull for after her E.
Riven.ability_to_dodge: Riven E (Valor) — bait her shield before W pulling. If she shielded, wait for it to expire then W pull.

power_spikes.enemy: Level 9 (CDR combo), Ravenous Hydra + Black Cleaver

Riven.early_game: Even. Your W pull is great for keeping her in range when she tries to disengage.
